Explore the codebase at /Users/damian/Code/inception-engine thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. The overall project structure (directories, key files)
2. What "agent definition deployment" currently means in this codebase - look for any existing deployer/deployment code
3. What agents are supported (look for adapter files, agent configs, manifests)
4. What "dedicated agent directories" and "frontmatter-based agent files" mean in context - look for any existing examples in limbo/, fixtures/, or docs/
5. What the current deploy flow looks like (entry points, deployer classes, interfaces)
6. Any existing tests for deployment

Please be very thorough. Return:
- The full directory tree (top 2 levels)
- Key source files and their purposes
- All agent/adapter types found
- Any existing agent definition or persona deployment code
- Any relevant interfaces or types
- Example agent files from limbo/ or fixtures/
- Test structure
I'll explore the codebase thoroughly. Let me start with multiple parallel searches to build a comprehensive picture.

<content>1: import type { AgentId } from "./schemas/manifest.ts";
2: 
3: export type {
4:   AgentId,
5:   ConfigEntry,
6:   FileEntry,
7:   Manifest,
8:   SkillEntry,
9: } from "./schemas/manifest.ts";
10: 
11: export interface AgentPaths {
12:   posix: string[];
13:   windows: string[];
14: }
15: 
16: export type Confidence = "documented" | "implementation-only" | "provisional";
17: 
18: export interface SupportedAgentSurface {
19:   status: "supported";
20:   path: AgentPaths;
21:   schemaLabel: string;
22:   // Override the top-level key used when patching a JSON config file.
23:   // Defaults to "mcpServers" when absent. Allows agents like OpenCode that
24:   // store MCP servers under a different key (e.g. "mcp") to reuse the
25:   // existing JSON merge-patch adapter without a new action kind.
26:   mcpPatchKey?: string;
27: }
28: 
29: export interface UnsupportedAgentSurface {
30:   status: "unsupported";
31:   schemaLabel: string;
32:   reason: string;
33: }
34: 
35: /**
36:  * A surface that is confirmed as Copilot-specific and genuinely justified for
37:  * future dedicated implementation, but not yet supported. Distinct from
38:  * "unsupported" (which means the surface is either covered by another target or
39:  * will never be added) so adapters can emit a forward-looking "planned" notice
40:  * rather than a blocking skip warning.
41:  */
42: export interface PlannedAgentSurface {
43:   status: "planned";
44:   schemaLabel: string;
45:   /** Short description of the concrete file / config surface to be implemented. */
46:   plannedSurface: string;
47:   reason: string;
48: }
49: 
50: export type AgentSurfaceSupport =
51:   | SupportedAgentSurface
52:   | UnsupportedAgentSurface
53:   | PlannedAgentSurface;
54: 
55: export interface AgentProvenance {
56:   /** Omit when the agent has no separate skill deployment path. */
57:   skills?: Confidence;
58:   detectPaths: Confidence;
59:   detectBinary: Confidence;
60:   mcpConfig?: Confidence;
61:   agentRules?: Confidence;
62:   permissions?: Confidence;
63: }
64: 
65: export interface AgentConfig {
66:   id: AgentId;
67:   displayName: string;
68:   /**
69:    * The on-disk path template for deploying skills to this agent.
70:    * Omit for agents that consume skills from another agent's path natively
71:    * (e.g. GitHub Copilot reads `.claude/skills/` directly).
72:    */
73:   skills?: AgentPaths;
74:   detectPaths: AgentPaths;
75:   detectBinary: string | null;
76:   provenance: AgentProvenance;
77:   // Agent-specific MCP surface support. Unsupported entries are carried
78:   // explicitly so adapter warnings can explain whether support is blocked by
79:   // TOML, frontmatter, repo-scoped files, or other non-JSON schemas.
80:   mcpSupport?: AgentSurfaceSupport;
81:   // Agent-specific persistent instruction/rules surface support.
82:   agentRulesSupport?: AgentSurfaceSupport;
83:   // Agent-specific execution/safety permission and approval surface support.
84:   permissionsSupport?: AgentSurfaceSupport;
85:   policyNote?: string;
86: }
87: 
88: export interface PlanWarning {
89:   kind: "confidence" | "collision" | "ambiguity";
90:   message: string;
91: }
92: 
93: export interface SkillDirDeployAction {
94:   kind: "skill-dir";
95:   skill: string;
96:   agent: AgentId;
97:   source: string;
98:   target: string;
99:   method: "symlink" | "copy";
100:   confidence: Confidence;
101: }
102: 
103: export interface FileWriteDeployAction {
104:   kind: "file-write";
105:   skill: string;
106:   agent: AgentId;
107:   source: string;
108:   target: string;
109:   confidence?: Confidence;
110: }
111: 
112: export interface ConfigPatchDeployAction {
113:   kind: "config-patch";
114:   skill: string;
115:   agent: AgentId;
116:   target: string;
117:   patch: unknown;
118:   confidence?: Confidence;
119: }
120: 
121: export interface TomlPatchDeployAction {
122:   kind: "toml-patch";
123:   skill: string;
124:   agent: AgentId;
125:   target: string;
126:   config: Record<string, unknown>;
127:   confidence?: Confidence;
128: }
129: 
130: export interface FrontmatterEmitDeployAction {
131:   kind: "frontmatter-emit";
132:   skill: string;
133:   agent: AgentId;
134:   // Absolute path to the .md file to create/update.
135:   target: string;
136:   // Key-value pairs to write into the YAML frontmatter block.
137:   frontmatter: Record<string, unknown>;
138:   confidence?: Confidence;
139: }
140: 
141: export type DeployAction =
142:   | SkillDirDeployAction
143:   | FileWriteDeployAction
144:   | ConfigPatchDeployAction
145:   | TomlPatchDeployAction
146:   | FrontmatterEmitDeployAction;
147:

152:   target: string;

154:

178:   skill: string;

182: 
183: export type RevertAction =
184:   | SkillDirRevertAction

186:   | ConfigPatchRevertAction
187:   | TomlPatchRevertAction
188:   | FrontmatterEmitRevertAction;
189: 
190: export type PlannedChangeVerb =
191:   | "create-symlink"

195:   | "patch-toml"
196:   | "emit-frontmatter"
197:   | "remove"
198:   | "unapply-patch";
199: 
200: export interface PlannedChange {
201:   verb: PlannedChangeVerb;
202:   kind:
203:     | "skill-dir"
204:     | "file-write"
205:     | "config-patch"
206:     | "toml-patch"
207:     | "frontmatter-emit";
208:   skill: string;
209:   agent: AgentId;
210:   source?: string;
211:   target: string;
212:   method?: "symlink" | "copy";
213:   patch?: Record<string, unknown>;
214:   frontmatter?: Record<string, unknown>;
215:   confidence?: Confidence;
216: }
217: 
218: export interface CliOptions {

<path>/Users/damian/Code/inception-engine/src/index.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: 
3: import path from "node:path";
4: import { parseArgs } from "node:util";
5: import { AGENT_REGISTRY } from "./config/agents.ts";
6: import { loadManifest } from "./config/manifest.ts";
7: import { executeDeploy, planDeploy } from "./core/deploy.ts";
8: import { detectInstalledAgents } from "./core/detect.ts";
9: import { runInit } from "./core/init.ts";
10: import { runPreflight } from "./core/preflight.ts";
11: import { resolveHome } from "./core/resolve.ts";
12: import { executeRevert, planRevert, planRevertAll } from "./core/revert.ts";
13: import type { ErrorCode } from "./errors.ts";
14: import { UserError } from "./errors.ts";
15: import { dryRunPrefix, logger } from "./logger.ts";
16: import { AgentListSchema } from "./schemas/manifest.ts";
17: import type { AgentId, CliOptions, Manifest, PlannedChange } from "./types.ts";
18: 
19: const USAGE = `
20: inception-engine - Deploy AI agent skills
21: 
22: Usage:
23:   inception-engine <directory> [options]
24:   inception-engine revert <directory> [options]
25:   inception-engine init <directory> [options]
26: 
27: Commands:
28:   <directory>         Deploy skills from the manifest in the given directory
29:   revert <directory>  Remove previously deployed skills
30:   init <directory>    Scan a directory for skill folders and generate inception.json
31: 
32: Options:
33:   --dry-run        Show what would be done without doing it
34:   --agents <list>  Comma-separated list of agent IDs to target
35:   --force          (init only) Overwrite an existing inception.json
36:   --verbose        Show detailed output
37:   --debug          Show full error stack traces
38:   --help           Show this help message
39: 
40: Supported agents:
41:   ${AGENT_REGISTRY.map((a) => `${a.id} (${a.displayName})`).join(", ")}
42: `.trim();
43: 
44: function parseCLI(argv: string[]): CliOptions {
45:   const args = argv.slice(2);
46: 
47:   if (args.length === 0) {
48:     return {
49:       command: "help",
50:       directory: "",
51:       dryRun: false,
52:       agents: null,
53:       verbose: false,
54:       debug: false,
55:       force: false,
56:     };
57:   }
58: 
59:   let parsed: ReturnType<typeof parseArgs>;
60:   try {
61:     parsed = parseArgs({
62:       args,
63:       allowPositionals: true,
64:       options: {
65:         "dry-run": { type: "boolean", default: false },
66:         verbose: { type: "boolean", default: false },
67:         debug: { type: "boolean", default: false },
68:         help: { type: "boolean", default: false },
69:         force: { type: "boolean", default: false },
70:         agents: { type: "string" },
71:       },
72:     });
73:   } catch (err) {
74:     throw new UserError("INVALID_ARGS", (err as Error).message);
75:   }
76: 
77:   const { values, positionals } = parsed;
78: 
79:   if (values.help) {
80:     return {
81:       command: "help",
82:       directory: "",
83:       dryRun: false,
84:       agents: null,
85:       verbose: false,
86:       debug: false,
87:       force: false,
88:     };
89:   }
90: 
91:   let command: "deploy" | "revert" | "init" = "deploy";
92:   let pos = positionals;
93:   if (pos[0] === "revert") {
94:     command = "revert";
95:     pos = pos.slice(1);
96:   } else if (pos[0] === "init") {
97:     command = "init";
98:     pos = pos.slice(1);
99:   }
100: 
101:   if (pos.length > 1) {
102:     throw new UserError("INVALID_ARGS", `Unexpected argument: ${pos[1]}`);
103:   }
104:   const rawDir = pos[0];
105:   if (!rawDir) {
106:     throw new UserError(
107:       "INVALID_ARGS",
108:       "Missing required <directory> argument",
109:     );
110:   }
111: 
112:   let agents: AgentId[] | null = null;
113:   if (typeof values.agents === "string") {
114:     const r = AgentListSchema.safeParse(values.agents);
115:     if (!r.success) {
116:       throw new UserError("INVALID_ARGS", r.error.issues[0].message);
117:     }
118:     agents = r.data;
119:   }
120: 
121:   return {
122:     command,
123:     directory: path.resolve(rawDir),
124:     dryRun: values["dry-run"] as boolean,
125:     agents,
126:     verbose: values.verbose as boolean,
127:     debug: values.debug as boolean,
128:     force: values.force as boolean,
129:   };
130: }
131: 
132: async function main(): Promise<number> {
133:   const options = parseCLI(process.argv);
134: 
135:   if (options.command === "help") {
136:     console.log(USAGE);
137:     return 0;
138:   }
139: 
140:   if (options.command === "init") {
141:     return runInit({
142:       directory: options.directory,
143:       agents: options.agents,
144:       dryRun: options.dryRun,
145:       force: options.force,
146:       verbose: options.verbose,
147:     });
148:   }
149: 
150:   const manifest = await loadManifest(options.directory);
151:   const home = resolveHome();
152: 
153:   if (options.command === "deploy") {
154:     return runDeploy(options, manifest, home);
155:   }
156:   return runRevert(options, manifest, home);
157: }
158: 
159: function renderDryRunPlan(planned: PlannedChange[]): void {
160:   for (const change of planned) {
161:     logger.plan(`[${change.agent}] ${change.verb}  ${change.skill}`);
162:     if (change.source !== undefined) {
163:       logger.detail(`source: ${change.source}`);
164:     }
165:     logger.detail(`target: ${change.target}`);
166:     if (change.verb === "patch-config" && change.patch !== undefined) {
167:       logger.detail(`patch:  ${JSON.stringify(change.patch)}`);
168:     } else if (change.verb === "unapply-patch" && change.patch !== undefined) {
169:       logger.detail(`undo:   ${JSON.stringify(change.patch)}`);
170:     }
171:   }
172: }
173: 
174: async function runDeploy(
175:   options: CliOptions,
176:   manifest: Manifest,
177:   home: string,
178: ): Promise<number> {
179:   let detectedAgents: AgentId[];
180:   if (options.agents) {
181:     detectedAgents = options.agents;
182:     if (options.verbose) {
183:       logger.info(`Using specified agents: ${detectedAgents.join(", ")}`);
184:     }
185:   } else {
186:     detectedAgents = await detectInstalledAgents(home);
187:     if (detectedAgents.length === 0) {
188:       logger.info("No supported AI agents detected on this system.");
189:       logger.info(
190:         `Install one of: ${AGENT_REGISTRY.map((a) => a.displayName).join(", ")}`,
191:       );
192:       return 0;
193:     }
194:     if (options.verbose) {
195:       logger.info(`Detected agents: ${detectedAgents.join(", ")}`);
196:     }
197:   }
198: 
199:   const preflightWarnings = await runPreflight(
200:     options,
201:     manifest,
202:     home,
203:     detectedAgents,
204:   );
205:   for (const w of preflightWarnings) {
206:     const label = w.kind === "policy" ? "policy" : "preflight";
207:     logger.warn(label, w.message);
208:   }
209: 
210:   const { actions, warnings: planWarnings } = await planDeploy(
211:     manifest,
212:     options.directory,
213:     detectedAgents,
214:     home,
215:   );
216:   for (const w of planWarnings) {
217:     logger.warn("plan", w.message);
218:   }
219:   if (actions.length === 0) {
220:     logger.info("No skills to deploy for detected agents.");
221:     return 0;
222:   }
223: 
224:   logger.info(
225:     `${dryRunPrefix(options.dryRun)}Deploying ${actions.length} action(s):`,
226:   );
227:   const { succeeded, failed, planned } = await executeDeploy(
228:     actions,
229:     options.dryRun,
230:     options.verbose,
231:     home,
232:   );
233: 
234:   if (options.dryRun) {
235:     logger.info("");
236:     renderDryRunPlan(planned);
237:     logger.info("");
238:     logger.info(`${planned.length} action(s) would be applied (dry-run)`);
239:     return 0;
240:   }
241: 
242:   logger.info("");
243:   if (failed.length > 0) {
244:     logger.info(`${succeeded} succeeded, ${failed.length} failed`);
245:     return 1;
246:   }
247:   logger.info(`${succeeded} action(s) deployed`);
248:   return 0;
249: }
250: 
251: async function runRevert(
252:   options: CliOptions,
253:   manifest: Manifest,
254:   home: string,
255: ): Promise<number> {
256:   const actions = options.agents
257:     ? planRevert(manifest, options.agents, home)
258:     : planRevertAll(manifest, home);
259:   if (actions.length === 0) {
260:     logger.info("No skills to revert.");
261:     return 0;
262:   }
263: 
264:   logger.info(
265:     `${dryRunPrefix(options.dryRun)}Reverting ${actions.length} action(s):`,
266:   );
267:   const { succeeded, skipped, failed, planned } = await executeRevert(
268:     actions,
269:     options.dryRun,
270:     options.verbose,
271:     home,
272:   );
273: 
274:   if (options.dryRun) {
275:     logger.info("");
276:     renderDryRunPlan(planned);
277:     logger.info("");
278:     logger.info(`${planned.length} action(s) would be removed (dry-run)`);
279:     return 0;
280:   }
281: 
282:   logger.info("");
283:   if (failed.length > 0) {
284:     const parts = [`${succeeded} removed`];
285:     if (skipped > 0) parts.push(`${skipped} skipped`);
286:     parts.push(`${failed.length} failed`);
287:     logger.info(parts.join(", "));
288:     return 1;
289:   }
290:   const parts = [`${succeeded} removed`];
291:   if (skipped > 0) parts.push(`${skipped} skipped`);
292:   logger.info(parts.join(", "));
293:   return 0;
294: }
295: 
296: const USER_ERROR_EXIT: Record<ErrorCode, number> = {
297:   INVALID_ARGS: 2,
298:   MANIFEST_INVALID: 3,
299:   DEPLOY_FAILED: 1,
300:   RESOLVE_FAILED: 1,
301: };
302: 
303: const debugMode = process.argv.includes("--debug");
304: 
305: try {
306:   process.exit(await main());
307: } catch (err) {
308:   if (err instanceof UserError) {
309:     logger.error(`Error: ${err.message}`);
310:     if (debugMode) {
311:       logger.errorRaw(err);
312:     }
313:     process.exit(USER_ERROR_EXIT[err.code]);
314:   } else {
315:     logger.error("Unexpected error. Run with --debug for details.");
316:     if (debugMode) {
317:       logger.errorRaw(err);
318:     }
319:     process.exit(1);
320:   }
321: }

(End of file - total 321 lines)

77: function computeUndoPatch(
78:   original: Record<string, unknown>,
79:   patch: Record<string, unknown>,
80: ): Record<string, unknown> {
81:   const undoPatch: Record<string, unknown> = {};
82:   for (const key of Object.keys(patch)) {
83:     const patchVal = patch[key];
84:     if (isPlainObject(patchVal) && isPlainObject(original[key])) {
85:       undoPatch[key] = computeUndoPatch(
86:         original[key] as Record<string, unknown>,
87:         patchVal as Record<string, unknown>,
88:       );
89:     } else {
90:       undoPatch[key] = key in original ? original[key] : null;
91:     }
92:   }
93:   return undoPatch;
94: }

96: function applyMergePatch(
97:   original: Record<string, unknown>,
98:   patch: Record<string, unknown>,
99: ): Record<string, unknown> {
100:   const patched: Record<string, unknown> = { ...original };
101:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(patch)) {
102:     if (value === null) {
103:       delete patched[key];
104:     } else if (isPlainObject(value) && isPlainObject(patched[key])) {
105:       patched[key] = applyMergePatch(
106:         patched[key] as Record<string, unknown>,
107:         value as Record<string, unknown>,
108:       );
109:     } else {
110:       patched[key] = value;
111:     }
112:   }
113:   return patched;
114: }

333: ): Promise<{

336:   planned: PlannedChange[];

510:   prepareBackup: () => Promise<string | null>,
511:   commit: () => Promise<void>,
512: ): Promise<void> {
513:   const fileOps = deps.fileOps ?? defaultDeployFileOps;
514:   const tempPath = createAtomicTempPath(targetPath);
515:   let backupPath: string | null = null;

519:     await mkdir(path.dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true });
520:     await stageTempFile(tempPath, fileOps);
521:     backupPath = await prepareBackup();
522:     await fileOps.rename(tempPath, targetPath);
523:     replacedTarget = true;
524:     await commit();
525:   } catch (writeErr) {
526:     if (replacedTarget) {

540:     if (backupPath) {
541:       try {
542:         await fileOps.rename(backupPath, targetPath);

556: async function deploySkillDir(

566:   try {

569:     const msg = sourceAccessError(err, action.source);
570:     logger.fail(label, msg);
571:     return { error: msg };
572:   }
573: 
574:   if (dryRun) {
575:     planned.push({
576:       verb: action.method === "symlink" ? "create-symlink" : "copy-dir",
577:       kind: "skill-dir",
578:       skill: action.skill,
579:       agent: action.agent,
580:       source: action.source,
581:       target: action.target,
582:       method: action.method,
583:       confidence: action.confidence,
584:     });
585:     return { error: null };
586:   }
587: 
588:   try {
589:     await executeDeployAction(action, verbose, home, deps);
590:     return { error: null };
591:   } catch (err) {
592:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
593:     logger.fail(label, msg);
594:     return { error: msg };
595:   }
596: }
597: 
598: async function deployFileWrite(
599:   action: FileWriteDeployAction,
600:   dryRun: boolean,
601:   verbose: boolean,
602:   home: string,
603:   planned: PlannedChange[],
604:   deps: DeployDependencies,
605: ): Promise<{ error: string | null }> {
606:   const label = `${action.skill} -> ${action.agent}`;
607: 
608:   try {
609:     await access(action.source);
610:   } catch (err) {
611:     const msg = sourceAccessError(err, action.source);
612:     logger.fail(label, msg);
613:     return { error: msg };
614:   }
615: 
616:   if (dryRun) {
617:     planned.push({
618:       verb: "write-file",
619:       kind: "file-write",
620:       skill: action.skill,
621:       agent: action.agent,
622:       source: action.source,
623:       target: action.target,
624:     });
625:     return { error: null };
626:   }
627: 
628:   try {
629:     await replaceFileAtomically(
630:       action.target,
631:       deps,
632:       (tempPath, fileOps) => fileOps.copyFile(action.source, tempPath),
633:       () => backupManagedFileWriteTarget(action, home, deps),
634:       () =>
635:         registerDeployment(
636:           home,
637:           action.target,
638:           {
639:             kind: "file-write",
640:             source: action.source,
641:             skill: action.skill,
642:             agent: action.agent,
643:           },
644:           deps.registry,
645:         ),
646:     );
647:     logger.ok(label);
648:     if (verbose) {
649:       logger.detail(`write-file: ${action.source} -> ${action.target}`);
650:     }
651:     return { error: null };
652:   } catch (err) {
653:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
654:     logger.fail(label, msg);
655:     return { error: msg };
656:   }
657: }
658: 
659: async function deployConfigPatch(
660:   action: ConfigPatchDeployAction,
661:   dryRun: boolean,
662:   verbose: boolean,
663:   home: string,
664:   planned: PlannedChange[],
665:   deps: DeployDependencies,
666: ): Promise<{ error: string | null }> {
667:   const label = `${action.skill} -> ${action.agent}`;
668: 
669:   if (!isPlainObject(action.patch)) {
670:     const msg = `Config patch for skill "${action.skill}" must be a plain object`;
671:     logger.fail(label, msg);
672:     return { error: msg };
673:   }
674: 
675:   const patch = action.patch;
676: 
677:   if (dryRun) {
678:     planned.push({
679:       verb: "patch-config",
680:       kind: "config-patch",
681:       skill: action.skill,
682:       agent: action.agent,
683:       target: action.target,
684:       patch,
685:     });
686:     return { error: null };
687:   }
688: 
689:   try {
690:     // Guard against double-patching by a different skill/agent
691:     const existingEntry = await lookupDeployment(
692:       home,
693:       action.target,
694:       deps.registry,
695:     );
696:     if (
697:       existingEntry &&
698:       (existingEntry.skill !== action.skill ||
699:         existingEntry.agent !== action.agent)
700:     ) {
701:       throw new Error(
702:         `Config "${action.target}" is already patched by skill "${existingEntry.skill}" for agent "${existingEntry.agent}" — refusing to double-patch`,
703:       );
704:     }
705: 
706:     const original = await readJsonConfigFile(action.target);
707:     const undoPatch = computeUndoPatch(original, patch);
708:     const patched = applyMergePatch(original, patch);
709: 
710:     await replaceFileAtomically(
711:       action.target,
712:       deps,
713:       (tempPath, fileOps) =>
714:         fileOps.writeFile(
715:           tempPath,
716:           `${JSON.stringify(patched, null, 2)}\n`,
717:           "utf-8",
718:         ),
719:       async () => {
720:         const backupPath = `${action.target}.inception-backup`;
721:         await (deps.fileOps ?? defaultDeployFileOps).rm(backupPath, {
722:           recursive: true,
723:           force: true,
724:         });
725:         await (deps.fileOps ?? defaultDeployFileOps).rename(
726:           action.target,
727:           backupPath,
728:         );
729:         return backupPath;
730:       },
731:       () =>
732:         registerDeployment(
733:           home,
734:           action.target,
735:           {
736:             kind: "config-patch",
737:             patch,
738:             undoPatch,
739:             skill: action.skill,
740:             agent: action.agent,
741:           },
742:           deps.registry,
743:         ),
744:     );
745: 
746:     logger.ok(label);
747:     if (verbose) {
748:       logger.detail(
749:         `patch-config: applied ${Object.keys(patch).length} key(s) to ${action.target}`,
750:       );
751:     }
752:     return { error: null };
753:   } catch (err) {
754:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
755:     logger.fail(label, msg);
756:     return { error: msg };
757:   }
758: }
759: 
760: async function validateSkillContract(
761:   source: string,
762:   skillPath: string,
763: ): Promise<void> {
764:   let stat: import("node:fs").Stats;
765:   try {
766:     stat = await lstat(source);
767:   } catch (err) {
768:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
769:     if (code === "ENOENT") {
770:       throw new UserError(
771:         "DEPLOY_FAILED",
772:         `Skill "${skillPath}" source not found: ${source}`,
773:       );
774:     }
775:     if (code === "EACCES" || code === "EPERM") {
776:       throw new UserError(
777:         "DEPLOY_FAILED",
778:         `Permission denied accessing skill "${skillPath}" source: ${source}`,
779:       );
780:     }
781:     throw new UserError(
782:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
783:       `Cannot access skill "${skillPath}" source: ${source}`,
784:     );
785:   }
786:   if (!stat.isDirectory()) {
787:     throw new UserError(
788:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
789:       `Skill "${skillPath}" source is not a directory: ${source}`,
790:     );
791:   }
792:   try {
793:     await access(source, constants.R_OK);
794:   } catch (err) {
795:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
796:     if (code === "EACCES" || code === "EPERM") {
797:       throw new UserError(
798:         "DEPLOY_FAILED",
799:         `Permission denied reading skill directory "${skillPath}": ${source}`,
800:       );
801:     }
802:     throw new UserError(
803:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
804:       `Cannot read skill directory "${skillPath}": ${source}`,
805:     );
806:   }
807:   try {
808:     await access(path.join(source, "SKILL.md"), constants.R_OK);
809:   } catch (err) {
810:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
811:     if (code === "EACCES" || code === "EPERM") {
812:       throw new UserError(
813:         "DEPLOY_FAILED",
814:         `Permission denied reading SKILL.md in skill "${skillPath}": ${source}`,
815:       );
816:     }
817:     throw new UserError(
818:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
819:       `Skill "${skillPath}" source is missing SKILL.md: ${source}`,
820:     );
821:   }
822:   await validateSkillDefinitionFile(path.join(source, "SKILL.md"), skillPath);
823: }
824: 
825: async function assertTargetAbsent(targetPath: string): Promise<void> {
826:   try {
827:     await lstat(targetPath);
828:     throw new Error(
829:       `Target path appeared unexpectedly after backup: ${targetPath}`,
830:     );
831:   } catch (err) {
832:     if (err instanceof Error && err.message.startsWith("Target path appeared"))
833:       throw err;
834:     // ENOENT is expected — target should not exist after backup
835:   }
836: }
837: 
838: async function createDeployTarget(
839:   action: SkillDirDeployAction,
840:   home: string,
841:   deps: DeployDependencies,
842: ): Promise<void> {
843:   await (deps.skillDirOps ?? defaultSkillDirOps).createTarget(action);
844:   await registerDeployment(
845:     home,
846:     action.target,
847:     {
848:       kind: action.kind,
849:       source: action.source,
850:       skill: action.skill,
851:       agent: action.agent,
852:       method: action.method,
853:     },
854:     deps.registry,
855:   );
856: }
857: 
858: async function executeDeployAction(
859:   action: SkillDirDeployAction,
860:   verbose: boolean,
861:   home: string,
862:   deps: DeployDependencies,
863: ): Promise<void> {
864:   const label = `${action.skill} -> ${action.agent}`;
865:   const backupPath = await backupExisting(
866:     action.target,
867:     verbose,
868:     home,
869:     {
870:       kind: action.kind,
871:       source: action.source,
872:       skill: action.skill,
873:       agent: action.agent,
874:     },
875:     deps,
876:   );
877:   await mkdir(path.dirname(action.target), { recursive: true });
878: 
879:   try {
880:     await assertTargetAbsent(action.target);
881:     await createDeployTarget(action, home, deps);
882:   } catch (createErr) {
883:     if (backupPath) {
884:       try {
885:         await (deps.skillDirOps ?? defaultSkillDirOps)
886:           .removeTarget(action.target)
887:           .catch(() => {
888:             /* best-effort cleanup */
889:           });
890:         await rename(backupPath, action.target);
891:       } catch {
892:         // Best-effort rollback
893:       }
894:     }
895:     throw createErr;
896:   }
897: 
898:   if (backupPath) {
899:     await (deps.skillDirOps ?? defaultSkillDirOps).removeTarget(backupPath);
900:   }
901: 
902:   logger.ok(label);
903:   if (verbose) {
904:     logger.detail(`${action.method}: ${action.source} -> ${action.target}`);
905:   }
906: }
907: 
908: async function backupExisting(
909:   targetPath: string,
910:   verbose: boolean,
911:   home: string,
912:   expected: { kind: string; source: string; skill: string; agent: AgentId },
913:   deps: DeployDependencies,
914: ): Promise<string | null> {
915:   try {
916:     await lstat(targetPath);
917:   } catch {
918:     return null;
919:   }
920: 
921:   if (
922:     !(await verifyDeployment(
923:       home,
924:       targetPath,
925:       {
926:         kind: "skill-dir",
927:         source: expected.source,
928:         skill: expected.skill,
929:         agent: expected.agent,
930:       },
931:       deps.registry,
932:     ))
933:   ) {
934:     throw new Error(
935:       `Target "${targetPath}" exists but is not managed by inception-engine — refusing to overwrite`,
936:     );
937:   }
938: 
939:   const backupPath = `${targetPath}.inception-backup`;
940: 
941:   if (verbose) {
942:     logger.detail(`backing up existing target: ${targetPath}`);
943:   }
944: 
945:   // Remove any stale backup from a previous failed attempt. Using rm with
946:   // { force: true } avoids a separate lstat existence check and handles
947:   // the case where the stale backup is a directory (which rename cannot
948:   // atomically replace on POSIX). This reduces the window between the
949:   // stale-backup removal and the rename to a single step.
950:   await rm(backupPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
951:   await rename(targetPath, backupPath);
952:   return backupPath;
953: }

(End of file - total 953 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import { execFile } from "node:child_process";
2: import { access } from "node:fs/promises";
3: import { promisify } from "node:util";
4: import { AGENT_REGISTRY } from "../config/agents.ts";
5: import type { AgentConfig, AgentId } from "../types.ts";
6: import { resolveAgentDetectPath } from "./resolve.ts";
7: 
8: const execFileAsync = promisify(execFile);
9: 
10: // Injectable executor for testing: receives a command and its arguments and
11: // must throw on non-zero exit (same contract as the promisified execFile).
12: export type ExecFn = (cmd: string, args: readonly string[]) => Promise<void>;
13: 
14: const defaultExecFn: ExecFn = async (cmd, args) => {
15:   await execFileAsync(cmd, args as string[]);
16: };
17: 
18: export async function detectInstalledAgents(home: string): Promise<AgentId[]> {
19:   const detected: AgentId[] = [];
20: 
21:   for (const agent of AGENT_REGISTRY) {
22:     if (await isAgentInstalled(agent, home)) {
23:       detected.push(agent.id);
24:     }
25:   }
26: 
27:   return detected;
28: }
29: 
30: async function isAgentInstalled(
31:   agent: AgentConfig,
32:   home: string,
33: ): Promise<boolean> {
34:   const detectPath = resolveAgentDetectPath(agent, home);
35:   try {
36:     await access(detectPath);
37:     return true;
38:   } catch {
39:     // path does not exist — fall through to binary detection
40:   }
41: 
42:   if (agent.detectBinary) {
43:     return isBinaryInPath(agent.detectBinary);
44:   }
45: 
46:   return false;
47: }
48: 
49: export async function isBinaryInPath(
50:   binary: string,
51:   execFn: ExecFn = defaultExecFn,
52:   platform: NodeJS.Platform = process.platform,
53: ): Promise<boolean> {
54:   if (platform === "win32") {
55:     return isBinaryViaWhereExe(binary, execFn);
56:   }
57: 
58:   try {
59:     await execFn("which", [binary]);
60:     return true;
61:   } catch (err: unknown) {
62:     // `which` itself is not installed — fall back to the POSIX shell built-in
63:     if (isENOENT(err)) {
64:       return isBinaryViaCommandV(binary, execFn);
65:     }
66:     return false;
67:   }
68: }
69: 
70: // Windows-only: use where.exe to check if a binary is in PATH.
71: export async function isBinaryViaWhereExe(
72:   binary: string,
73:   execFn: ExecFn = defaultExecFn,
74: ): Promise<boolean> {
75:   try {
76:     await execFn("where.exe", [binary]);
77:     return true;
78:   } catch {
79:     return false;
80:   }
81: }
82: 
83: // Used only when `which` is absent (e.g. minimal Alpine containers).
84: // `command -v` is a POSIX shell built-in available wherever /bin/sh is.
85: export async function isBinaryViaCommandV(
86:   binary: string,
87:   execFn: ExecFn = defaultExecFn,
88: ): Promise<boolean> {
89:   try {
90:     // Pass binary as a positional arg ($1) to avoid any shell-injection risk.
91:     await execFn("sh", ["-c", 'command -v "$1"', "--", binary]);
92:     return true;
93:   } catch {
94:     return false;
95:   }
96: }
97: 
98: // POSIX: use `which` to check if a binary is in PATH.
99: export async function isBinaryViaWhich(
100:   binary: string,
101:   execFn: ExecFn = defaultExecFn,
102: ): Promise<boolean> {
103:   try {
104:     await execFn("which", [binary]);
105:     return true;
106:   } catch {
107:     return false;
108:   }
109: }
110: 
111: function isENOENT(err: unknown): boolean {
112:   return (
113:     typeof err === "object" &&
114:     err !== null &&
115:     (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT"
116:   );
117: }

<content>1: import nodePath from "node:path";
2: import { z } from "zod";

4: const AGENT_IDS = [
5:   "claude-code",
6:   "codex",
7:   "gemini-cli",
8:   "antigravity",
9:   "opencode",
10:   "github-copilot",
11: ] as const;
12: 
13: export { AGENT_IDS };
14: 
15: const SAFE_NAME_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/;
16: 
17: // Target templates must be rooted at a known placeholder and may only add
18: // descendant path segments beneath that root. e.g. "{home}/.claude/settings.json"
19: // is valid, while "{home}/../.ssh/config" is rejected.
20: // {repo} resolves to the manifest directory at deploy time, enabling repo-local
21: // targets (e.g. Antigravity's .agents/rules/ surface).
22: const TARGET_TEMPLATE_RE = /^\{(home|appdata|xdg_config|repo)\}(?:[\\/].*)?$/;
23: 
24: // Standalone schema used for type derivation and single-ID validation (e.g. index.ts).
25: export const AgentIdSchema = z.enum(AGENT_IDS);
26: export type AgentId = z.output<typeof AgentIdSchema>;
27: 
28: // Used inside SkillEntrySchema.agents so that enum failures embed the received
29: // value in the message (Zod 4's invalid_value issue omits the received value).
30: // The .pipe(AgentIdSchema) at the end provides the AgentId output type.
31: const agentIdElement = z
32:   .string()
33:   .superRefine((v, ctx) => {
34:     if (!(AGENT_IDS as readonly string[]).includes(v)) {
35:       ctx.addIssue({
36:         code: "custom",
37:         message: `unknown agent "${v}". Valid agents: ${AGENT_IDS.join(", ")}`,
38:       });
39:     }
40:   })
41:   .pipe(AgentIdSchema);
42: 
43: const nameField = z
44:   .string({ message: "name must be a non-empty string" })
45:   .min(1, { message: "name must be a non-empty string" })
46:   .regex(SAFE_NAME_RE, {
47:     message:
48:       "name must contain only letters, digits, hyphens, underscores, and dots, and must not start with a dot",
49:   });
50: 
51: const agentsField = z
52:   .array(agentIdElement, { message: "agents must be a non-empty array" })
53:   .min(1, { message: "agents must be a non-empty array" })
54:   .transform((arr) => [...new Set(arr)]);
55: 
56: const sourcePathField = z
57:   .string({ message: "path must be a non-empty string" })
58:   .min(1, { message: "path must be a non-empty string" })
59:   .refine((p) => !nodePath.isAbsolute(p), {
60:     message: "path must be a relative path",
61:   })
62:   .refine((p) => !nodePath.normalize(p).startsWith(".."), {
63:     message: "path must not escape the repository root",
64:   });
65: 
66: const targetTemplateField = z
67:   .string({ message: "target must be a non-empty string" })
68:   .min(1, { message: "target must be a non-empty string" })
69:   .refine((t) => TARGET_TEMPLATE_RE.test(t), {
70:     message:
71:       "target must start with a known placeholder: {home}, {appdata}, {xdg_config}, or {repo}",
72:   })
73:   .refine((t) => !t.split(/[\\/]+/).includes(".."), {
74:     message: "target must not escape its placeholder root",
75:   });
76: 
77: export const SkillEntrySchema = z.object({
78:   name: nameField,
79:   path: sourcePathField,
80:   agents: agentsField,
81: });
82: 
83: export const FileEntrySchema = z.object({
84:   name: nameField,
85:   path: sourcePathField,
86:   target: targetTemplateField,
87:   agents: agentsField,
88: });
89: 
90: export const ConfigEntrySchema = z.object({
91:   name: nameField,
92:   target: targetTemplateField,
93:   patch: z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()),
94:   agents: agentsField,
95: });
96: 
97: export const McpServerEntrySchema = z.object({
98:   name: nameField,
99:   agents: agentsField,
100:   // Raw server descriptor passed verbatim to each agent adapter.
101:   // Adapters are responsible for validating the shape they need.
102:   config: z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()),
103: });
104: 
105: export const AgentRuleEntrySchema = z.object({
106:   name: nameField,
107:   agents: agentsField,
108:   // Relative path to the rules/instruction file within the source bundle.
109:   path: sourcePathField,
110: });
111: 
112: export const PermissionsEntrySchema = z.object({
113:   name: nameField,
114:   agents: agentsField,

143: export type FileEntry = z.infer<typeof FileEntrySchema>;
144: export type ConfigEntry = z.infer<typeof ConfigEntrySchema>;
145: export type McpServerEntry = z.infer<typeof McpServerEntrySchema>;
146: export type AgentRuleEntry = z.infer<typeof AgentRuleEntrySchema>;
147: export type PermissionsEntry = z.infer<typeof PermissionsEntrySchema>;
148: export type Manifest = z.infer<typeof ManifestSchema>;

156:   );

<content>1: import { readFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2: import path from "node:path";
3: import { UserError } from "../errors.ts";
4: import { formatZodPath } from "../schemas/errors.ts";
5: import type { Manifest } from "../schemas/manifest.ts";
6: import { ManifestSchema } from "../schemas/manifest.ts";
7: 
8: export async function loadManifest(directory: string): Promise<Manifest> {
9:   const manifestPath = path.join(directory, "inception.json");
10: 
11:   let raw: string;
12:   try {
13:     raw = await readFile(manifestPath, "utf-8");
14:   } catch (err) {
15:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
16:     if (code === "ENOENT") {
17:       throw new UserError(
18:         "MANIFEST_INVALID",
19:         `No inception.json found in ${directory}. Are you pointing to the right repo?`,
20:       );
21:     }
22:     if (code === "EACCES" || code === "EPERM") {
23:       throw new UserError(
24:         "MANIFEST_INVALID",
25:         `Permission denied reading ${manifestPath}. Check file permissions.`,
26:       );
27:     }
28:     const detail = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
29:     throw new UserError(
30:       "MANIFEST_INVALID",
31:       `Failed to read ${manifestPath}: ${detail}`,
32:     );
33:   }
34: 
35:   let parsed: unknown;
36:   try {
37:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
38:   } catch {
39:     throw new UserError("MANIFEST_INVALID", `Invalid JSON in ${manifestPath}`);
40:   }
41: 
42:   return validateManifest(parsed, manifestPath);
43: }
44: 
45: function validateManifest(data: unknown, filePath: string): Manifest {
46:   const result = ManifestSchema.safeParse(data);
47: 
48:   if (!result.success) {
49:     const issue = result.error.issues[0];
50:     const issuePath = issue.path as (string | number)[];
51: 
52:     // Top-level "skills" key: missing or wrong type → uniform message
53:     if (issuePath.length === 1 && issuePath[0] === "skills") {
54:       throw new UserError(
55:         "MANIFEST_INVALID",
56:         `${filePath}: "skills" must be an array`,
57:       );
58:     }
59: 
60:     // Top-level array fields with wrong type → uniform message
61:     if (
62:       issuePath.length === 1 &&
63:       (issuePath[0] === "mcpServers" ||
64:         issuePath[0] === "agentRules" ||
65:         issuePath[0] === "files" ||
66:         issuePath[0] === "configs")
67:     ) {
68:       throw new UserError(
69:         "MANIFEST_INVALID",
70:         `${filePath}: "${issuePath[0]}" must be an array`,
71:       );
72:     }
73: 
74:     throw new UserError(
75:       "MANIFEST_INVALID",
76:       `${filePath}: ${formatZodPath(issuePath)}${issue.message}`,
77:     );
78:   }
79: 
80:   return result.data;
81: }

<content>1: import { mkdir, readFile, rename, rm, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
3: import path from "node:path";
4: 
5: const _require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
6: // front-matter is a CommonJS module whose export is the callable parse function.
7: const parseFrontmatter = _require("front-matter") as <T>(input: string) => {
8:   attributes: T;
9:   body: string;
10: };
11: 
12: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13: // Hand-rolled flat-YAML serializer
14: // Handles the shapes found in MCP server descriptors: strings, numbers,
15: // booleans, string arrays, and one-level nested objects (e.g. "env").
16: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17: 
18: function serializeScalar(value: string | number | boolean): string {
19:   if (typeof value === "string") {
20:     const needsQuotes = /[:#[\]{},|>&*!'"@`]|^\s|\s$/.test(value);
21:     return needsQuotes
22:       ? `"${value.replace(/\\/g, "\\\\").replace(/"/g, '\\"')}"`
23:       : value;
24:   }
25:   return String(value);
26: }
27: 
28: function serializeArray(key: string, arr: unknown[], pad: string): string {
29:   if (arr.length === 0) return `${pad}${key}: []`;
30:   const items = arr.map((item) => {
31:     const scalar =
32:       typeof item === "string" ||
33:       typeof item === "number" ||
34:       typeof item === "boolean"
35:         ? serializeScalar(item as string | number | boolean)
36:         : JSON.stringify(item);
37:     return `${pad}  - ${scalar}`;
38:   });
39:   return [`${pad}${key}:`, ...items].join("\n");
40: }
41: 
42: function serializeObject(
43:   key: string,
44:   obj: Record<string, unknown>,
45:   pad: string,
46: ): string {
47:   const inner = serializeFrontmatterAtDepth(obj, `${pad}  `);
48:   return inner ? `${pad}${key}:\n${inner}` : `${pad}${key}: {}`;
49: }
50: 
51: function serializeFrontmatterAtDepth(
52:   data: Record<string, unknown>,
53:   pad: string,
54: ): string {
55:   const lines: string[] = [];
56:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(data)) {
57:     if (value === null || value === undefined) continue;
58:     if (Array.isArray(value)) {
59:       lines.push(serializeArray(key, value, pad));
60:     } else if (typeof value === "object") {
61:       lines.push(serializeObject(key, value as Record<string, unknown>, pad));
62:     } else {
63:       lines.push(
64:         `${pad}${key}: ${serializeScalar(value as string | number | boolean)}`,
65:       );
66:     }
67:   }
68:   return lines.join("\n");
69: }
70: 
71: /**
72:  * Serializes a flat (one-level) object to a YAML block suitable for use inside
73:  * `---` frontmatter delimiters.
74:  */
75: export function serializeFrontmatter(data: Record<string, unknown>): string {
76:   return serializeFrontmatterAtDepth(data, "");
77: }
78: 
79: /**
80:  * Builds the full content of a frontmatter-bearing Markdown file.
81:  * If `body` is provided it is appended after the closing delimiter.
82:  */
83: export function buildFrontmatterDocument(
84:   frontmatter: Record<string, unknown>,
85:   body = "",
86: ): string {
87:   const serialized = serializeFrontmatter(frontmatter);
88:   return `---\n${serialized}\n---\n${body}`;
89: }
90: 
91: function createAtomicTempPath(targetPath: string): string {
92:   return `${targetPath}.inception-tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}-${Math.random()
93:     .toString(36)
94:     .slice(2)}`;
95: }
96: 
97: /**
98:  * Reads an existing `.md` file and parses its frontmatter (using the
99:  * `front-matter` package). Returns the parsed attributes and raw body.
100:  * Returns `{ attributes: {}, body: "" }` if the file does not exist.
101:  */
102: export async function readFrontmatterFile(filePath: string): Promise<{
103:   attributes: Record<string, unknown>;
104:   body: string;
105: }> {
106:   let raw: string;
107:   try {
108:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
109:   } catch (err) {
110:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
111:     if (code === "ENOENT") return { attributes: {}, body: "" };
112:     throw err;
113:   }
114:   const parsed = parseFrontmatter<Record<string, unknown>>(raw);
115:   return { attributes: parsed.attributes, body: parsed.body };
116: }
117: 
118: /**
119:  * Atomically writes a Markdown file with the given frontmatter and optional
120:  * body. Preserves any existing body content when `preserveBody` is true.
121:  */
122: export async function writeFrontmatterFile(
123:   filePath: string,
124:   frontmatter: Record<string, unknown>,
125:   options: { preserveBody?: boolean } = {},
126: ): Promise<void> {
127:   let body = "";
128:   if (options.preserveBody) {
129:     const existing = await readFrontmatterFile(filePath);
130:     body = existing.body;
131:   }
132:   const content = buildFrontmatterDocument(frontmatter, body);
133:   const tempPath = createAtomicTempPath(filePath);
134:   try {
135:     await mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
136:     await writeFile(tempPath, content, "utf-8");
137:     await rename(tempPath, filePath);
138:   } catch (err) {
139:     try {
140:       await rm(tempPath, { force: true });
141:     } catch {
142:       /* best-effort cleanup */
143:     }
144:     throw err;
145:   }
146: }

<content>1: import path from "node:path";
2: import { AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID } from "../../config/agents.ts";
3: import type { McpServerEntry } from "../../schemas/manifest.ts";
4: import type {
5:   AgentId,
6:   ConfigPatchDeployAction,
7:   ConfigPatchRevertAction,
8:   FrontmatterEmitDeployAction,
9:   FrontmatterEmitRevertAction,
10:   PlanWarning,
11:   TomlPatchDeployAction,
12:   TomlPatchRevertAction,
13: } from "../../types.ts";
14: import { getPlatformKey, resolvePlaceholders } from "../resolve.ts";
15: import { validateMcpServerConfigShape } from "../validation.ts";
16: 
17: export interface McpAdapterResult {
18:   actions: Array<
19:     | ConfigPatchDeployAction
20:     | TomlPatchDeployAction
21:     | FrontmatterEmitDeployAction
22:   >;
23:   warnings: PlanWarning[];
24: }
25: 
26: /**
27:  * Determines whether a resolved config path targets a TOML file.
28:  */
29: function isTomlTarget(targetPath: string): boolean {
30:   return path.extname(targetPath).toLowerCase() === ".toml";
31: }
32: 
33: /**
34:  * Determines whether a resolved config path targets a Markdown file
35:  * (indicates a frontmatter-emit surface, e.g. Antigravity's .agents/rules/).
36:  */
37: function isMarkdownTarget(targetPath: string): boolean {
38:   const ext = path.extname(targetPath).toLowerCase();
39:   return ext === ".md" || ext === ".markdown";
40: }
41: 
42: type McpDeployAction =
43:   | ConfigPatchDeployAction
44:   | TomlPatchDeployAction
45:   | FrontmatterEmitDeployAction;
46: 
47: function buildMcpDeployAction(
48:   entry: McpServerEntry,
49:   agentId: AgentId,
50:   resolvedTarget: string,
51:   confidence: import("../../types.ts").Confidence,
52:   mcpPatchKey: string,
53: ): McpDeployAction {
54:   if (isMarkdownTarget(resolvedTarget)) {
55:     return {
56:       kind: "frontmatter-emit",
57:       skill: entry.name,
58:       agent: agentId,
59:       target: resolvedTarget,
60:       frontmatter: { "mcp-servers": { [entry.name]: entry.config } },
61:       confidence,
62:     } satisfies FrontmatterEmitDeployAction;
63:   }
64:   if (isTomlTarget(resolvedTarget)) {
65:     return {
66:       kind: "toml-patch",
67:       skill: entry.name,
68:       agent: agentId,
69:       target: resolvedTarget,
70:       config: entry.config,
71:       confidence,
72:     } satisfies TomlPatchDeployAction;
73:   }
74:   return {
75:     kind: "config-patch",
76:     skill: entry.name,
77:     agent: agentId,
78:     target: resolvedTarget,
79:     patch: { [mcpPatchKey]: { [entry.name]: entry.config } },
80:     confidence,
81:   } satisfies ConfigPatchDeployAction;
82: }

<content>1: import { mkdir, readFile, rename, rm, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2: import path from "node:path";
3: import { parse, stringify } from "smol-toml";
4: 
5: /**
6:  * Reads and parses a TOML file. Returns an empty object if the file does not
7:  * exist (treat a missing config.toml as an empty config).
8:  */
9: export async function readTomlConfig(
10:   filePath: string,
11: ): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
12:   let raw: string;
13:   try {
14:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
15:   } catch (err) {
16:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
17:     if (code === "ENOENT") return {};
18:     throw err;
19:   }
20:   const parsed = parse(raw);
21:   return parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
22: }
23: 
24: function createAtomicTempPath(targetPath: string): string {
25:   return `${targetPath}.inception-tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}-${Math.random()
26:     .toString(36)
27:     .slice(2)}`;
28: }
29: 
30: async function writeTomlConfigAtomic(
31:   filePath: string,
32:   obj: Record<string, unknown>,
33: ): Promise<void> {
34:   const tempPath = createAtomicTempPath(filePath);
35:   try {
36:     await mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
37:     await writeFile(tempPath, stringify(obj), "utf-8");
38:     await rename(tempPath, filePath);
39:   } catch (err) {
40:     try {
41:       await rm(tempPath, { force: true });
42:     } catch {
43:       /* best-effort cleanup */
44:     }
45:     throw err;
46:   }
47: }
48: 
49: /**
50:  * Merges a named MCP server entry into `config.toml`'s `[mcpServers]` table.
51:  * Returns the undo record (the previous value under that key, or null if it
52:  * was absent) so revert can restore the exact prior state.
53:  */
54: export async function applyTomlMcpPatch(
55:   filePath: string,
56:   name: string,
57:   config: Record<string, unknown>,
58: ): Promise<{ previousValue: unknown | null }> {
59:   const current = await readTomlConfig(filePath);
60: 
61:   const mcpServers =
62:     typeof current.mcpServers === "object" &&
63:     current.mcpServers !== null &&
64:     !Array.isArray(current.mcpServers)
65:       ? (current.mcpServers as Record<string, unknown>)
66:       : {};
67: 
68:   const previousValue = Object.hasOwn(mcpServers, name)
69:     ? mcpServers[name]
70:     : null;
71: 
72:   const patched: Record<string, unknown> = {
73:     ...current,
74:     mcpServers: { ...mcpServers, [name]: config },
75:   };
76: 
77:   await writeTomlConfigAtomic(filePath, patched);
78:   return { previousValue };
79: }
80: 
81: /**
82:  * Removes a named MCP server entry from `config.toml`'s `[mcpServers]` table.
83:  * If the entry is absent, this is a no-op. If the table becomes empty after
84:  * removal, the `mcpServers` key itself is removed from the document.
85:  */
86: export async function revertTomlMcpPatch(
87:   filePath: string,
88:   name: string,
89: ): Promise<void> {
90:   let current: Record<string, unknown>;
91:   try {
92:     current = await readTomlConfig(filePath);
93:   } catch {
94:     // File gone — nothing to revert.
95:     return;
96:   }
97: 
98:   const mcpServers =
99:     typeof current.mcpServers === "object" &&
100:     current.mcpServers !== null &&
101:     !Array.isArray(current.mcpServers)
102:       ? (current.mcpServers as Record<string, unknown>)
103:       : {};
104: 
105:   if (!Object.hasOwn(mcpServers, name)) return; // already absent
106: 
107:   const { [name]: _removed, ...remainingServers } = mcpServers;
108:   const reverted: Record<string, unknown> = { ...current };
109: 
110:   if (Object.keys(remainingServers).length === 0) {
111:     delete reverted.mcpServers;
112:   } else {
113:     reverted.mcpServers = remainingServers;
114:   }
115: 
116:   await writeTomlConfigAtomic(filePath, reverted);
117: }

(End of file - total 117 lines)
</content>

29: function buildSkillDirReverts(
30:   manifest: Manifest,
31:   home: string,
32:   agentFilter: AgentId[] | null,
33: ): SkillDirRevertAction[] {
34:   const actions: SkillDirRevertAction[] = [];
35:   for (const skill of manifest.skills) {
36:     for (const agentId of skill.agents) {
37:       if (agentFilter && !agentFilter.includes(agentId)) continue;
38:       const agent = AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID[agentId];
39:       if (!agent) continue;
40:       if (!agent.skills) continue;
41:       actions.push({

56: ): FileWriteRevertAction[] {
57:   const actions: FileWriteRevertAction[] = [];
58:   for (const fileEntry of manifest.files ?? []) {
59:     for (const agentId of fileEntry.agents) {
60:       if (agentFilter && !agentFilter.includes(agentId)) continue;
61:       const agent = AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID[agentId];
62:       if (!agent) continue;
63:       actions.push({
64:         kind: "file-write",
65:         skill: fileEntry.name,
66:         agent: agentId,
67:         target: resolveTargetTemplate(fileEntry.target, home),
68:       });
69:     }

95: 
96: export function planRevert(

98:   detectedAgents: AgentId[],
99:   home: string,
100:   repo?: string,
101: ): RevertAction[] {

104:     ...buildFileWriteReverts(manifest, home, detectedAgents),
105:     ...buildConfigPatchReverts(manifest, home, detectedAgents),
106:     ...(manifest.mcpServers ?? []).flatMap((e) =>
107:       compileMcpServerReverts(e, detectedAgents, home, repo),

109:     ...(manifest.agentRules ?? []).flatMap((e) =>

116: }
117: 
118: export function planRevertAll(

127:     ...(manifest.mcpServers ?? []).flatMap((e) =>
128:       compileMcpServerReverts(e, null, home, repo),
129:     ),
130:     ...(manifest.agentRules ?? []).flatMap((e) =>
131:       compileAgentRuleReverts(e, null, home, repo),
132:     ),
133:     ...(manifest.permissions ?? []).flatMap((e) =>
134:       compilePermissionsReverts(e, null, home),
135:     ),
136:   ];
137: }
138: 
139: type RevertOutcome =
140:   | { outcome: "ok" }
141:   | { outcome: "skip" }
142:   | { outcome: "fail"; error: string };
143: 
144: function recordOutcome(
145:   result: RevertOutcome,
146:   action: RevertAction,
147:   counts: { succeeded: number; skipped: number },
148:   failed: Array<{ action: RevertAction; error: string }>,
149: ): void {
150:   if (result.outcome === "fail") {
151:     failed.push({ action, error: result.error });
152:   } else if (result.outcome === "skip") {
153:     counts.skipped++;
154:   } else {
155:     counts.succeeded++;
156:   }
157: }
158: 
159: async function readJsonConfig(
160:   filePath: string,
161: ): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
162:   const rawContent = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
163:   let parsed: unknown;
164:   try {
165:     parsed = JSON.parse(rawContent);
166:   } catch {
167:     throw new Error(`Config file is not valid JSON: ${filePath}`);
168:   }
169:   if (typeof parsed !== "object" || parsed === null || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
170:     throw new Error(`Config file is not a JSON object: ${filePath}`);
171:   }
172:   return parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
173: }
174: 
175: function isPlainObject(v: unknown): v is Record<string, unknown> {
176:   return typeof v === "object" && v !== null && !Array.isArray(v);
177: }
178: 
179: function applyUndoPatch(
180:   current: Record<string, unknown>,
181:   undoPatch: Record<string, unknown>,
182: ): Record<string, unknown> {
183:   const restored: Record<string, unknown> = { ...current };
184:   for (const [key, originalValue] of Object.entries(undoPatch)) {
185:     if (originalValue === null) {
186:       delete restored[key];
187:     } else if (isPlainObject(originalValue) && isPlainObject(restored[key])) {
188:       restored[key] = applyUndoPatch(
189:         restored[key] as Record<string, unknown>,
190:         originalValue as Record<string, unknown>,
191:       );
192:     } else {
193:       restored[key] = originalValue;
194:     }
195:   }
196:   return restored;
197: }

200:   err: unknown,
201: ): { outcome: "skip" } | { outcome: "fail"; error: string } {
202:   if ((err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT") {
203:     return { outcome: "skip" };
204:   }
205:   const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
206:   return { outcome: "fail", error: msg };

209: export async function executeRevert(
210:   actions: RevertAction[],
211:   dryRun: boolean,

213:   home: string,

215: ): Promise<{

217:   skipped: number;
218:   failed: Array<{ action: RevertAction; error: string }>;
219:   planned: PlannedChange[];
220: }> {
221:   let succeeded = 0;
222:   let skipped = 0;
223:   const failed: Array<{ action: RevertAction; error: string }> = [];
224:   const planned: PlannedChange[] = [];
225:   const counts = { succeeded, skipped };
226: 
227:   for (const action of actions) {
228:     let result: RevertOutcome;
229:     switch (action.kind) {
230:       case "skill-dir":
231:         result = await executeRevertAction(
232:           action,
233:           dryRun,
234:           verbose,
235:           home,
236:           planned,
237:           deps,
238:         );
239:         break;
240:       case "file-write":
241:         result = await revertFileWrite(
242:           action,
243:           dryRun,
244:           verbose,
245:           home,
246:           planned,
247:           deps,
248:         );
249:         break;
250:       case "config-patch":
251:         result = await revertConfigPatch(
252:           action,
253:           dryRun,
254:           verbose,
255:           home,
256:           planned,
257:           deps,
258:         );
259:         break;
260:       case "toml-patch":
261:         result = await revertTomlPatch(
262:           action,
263:           dryRun,
264:           verbose,
265:           home,
266:           planned,
267:           deps,
268:         );
269:         break;
270:       case "frontmatter-emit":
271:         // Frontmatter-emit files are fully managed by inception — revert
272:         // deletes the file, same as a file-write revert.
273:         result = await revertFileWrite(
274:           {
275:             kind: "file-write",
276:             skill: action.skill,
277:             agent: action.agent,
278:             target: action.target,
279:           },
280:           dryRun,
281:           verbose,
282:           home,
283:           planned,
284:           deps,
285:         );
286:         break;
287:       default:
288:         throw new Error(
289:           `Unhandled revert action kind: ${(action as RevertAction).kind}`,
290:         );
291:     }
292:     recordOutcome(result, action, counts, failed);
293:   }
294: 
295:   succeeded = counts.succeeded;
296:   skipped = counts.skipped;
297: 
298:   return { succeeded, skipped, failed, planned };
299: }

301: interface RevertDependencies {
302:   registry?: RegistryPersistence;
303: }
304: 
305: async function executeRevertAction(
306:   action: RevertAction,
307:   dryRun: boolean,
308:   verbose: boolean,
309:   home: string,
310:   planned: PlannedChange[],
311:   deps: RevertDependencies,
312: ): Promise<RevertOutcome> {
313:   const label = `${action.skill} -> ${action.agent}`;
314: 
315:   try {
316:     await lstat(action.target);
317:   } catch (err) {
318:     const result = lstatOutcome(err);
319:     if (result.outcome === "skip") {
320:       logger.skip(label, "(not found, skipping)");
321:       return result;
322:     }
323:     logger.fail(label, result.error);
324:     return result;
325:   }
326: 
327:   const entry = await lookupDeployment(home, action.target, deps.registry);
328:   if (!entry || entry.skill !== action.skill || entry.agent !== action.agent) {
329:     logger.warn(
330:       label,
331:       `skipping: ${action.target} is not in the deployment registry — not managed by inception-engine`,
332:     );
333:     return { outcome: "skip" };
334:   }
335: 
336:   if (dryRun) {
337:     planned.push({
338:       verb: "remove",
339:       kind: "skill-dir",
340:       skill: action.skill,
341:       agent: action.agent,
342:       target: action.target,
343:     });
344:     return { outcome: "ok" };
345:   }
346: 
347:   try {
348:     // Re-stat immediately before deletion to minimise the window between the
349:     // type-check and the removal syscall.
350:     const currentStat = await lstat(action.target);
351:     if (currentStat.isSymbolicLink()) {
352:       await unlink(action.target);
353:     } else {
354:       await rm(action.target, { recursive: true });
355:     }
356:     await unregisterDeployment(home, action.target, deps.registry);
357:     logger.ok(label);
358:     if (verbose) {
359:       logger.detail(`removed: ${action.target}`);
360:     }
361:     return { outcome: "ok" };
362:   } catch (err) {
363:     if ((err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT") {
364:       // Target disappeared between ownership check and removal — treat as skip.
365:       logger.skip(label, "(disappeared before removal, skipping)");
366:       return { outcome: "skip" };
367:     }
368:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
369:     logger.fail(label, msg);
370:     return { outcome: "fail", error: msg };
371:   }
372: }

<content>1: import { execFileSync } from "node:child_process";
2: import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
3: import os from "node:os";
4: import { UserError } from "../errors.ts";
5: import type { AgentConfig } from "../types.ts";
6: import { getPathApi, resolveRuntimePaths } from "./runtime-paths.ts";
7: 
8: export function resolveHome(): string {
9:   if (process.platform === "win32") {
10:     return os.homedir();
11:   }
12: 
13:   const sudoUser = process.env.SUDO_USER;
14:   if (sudoUser && process.getuid?.() === 0) {
15:     return lookupHomeForUser(sudoUser);
16:   }
17: 
18:   return os.homedir();
19: }
20: 
21: export function lookupHomeForUserWith(
22:   username: string,
23:   platform: NodeJS.Platform,
24:   execFileFn: typeof execFileSync,
25:   readFileFn: typeof readFileSync,
26: ): string {
27:   if (platform !== "darwin") {
28:     const home = lookupViaGetent(username, execFileFn);
29:     if (home) return home;
30:   }
31: 
32:   if (platform === "darwin") {
33:     const home = lookupViaDscl(username, execFileFn);
34:     if (home) return home;
35:   }
36: 
37:   const home = lookupViaEtcPasswd(username, readFileFn);
38:   if (home) return home;
39: 
40:   throw new UserError(
41:     "RESOLVE_FAILED",
42:     `Cannot determine home directory for user "${username}". ` +
43:       `Tried getent, dscl, and /etc/passwd. ` +
44:       `Run without sudo, or set HOME to the correct path before invoking with sudo.`,
45:   );
46: }
47: 
48: function lookupViaGetent(
49:   username: string,
50:   execFileFn: typeof execFileSync,
51: ): string | null {
52:   try {
53:     const out = execFileFn("getent", ["passwd", username], {
54:       encoding: "utf8",
55:       stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "ignore"],
56:     }).trim();
57:     const home = (out as string).split(":")[5];
58:     if (typeof home === "string" && home.startsWith("/")) return home;
59:   } catch {
60:     // getent unavailable or user not found
61:   }
62:   return null;
63: }
64: 
65: function lookupViaDscl(
66:   username: string,
67:   execFileFn: typeof execFileSync,
68: ): string | null {
69:   try {
70:     const out = execFileFn(
71:       "dscl",
72:       [".", "-read", `/Users/${username}`, "NFSHomeDirectory"],
73:       { encoding: "utf8", stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "ignore"] },
74:     ).trim();
75:     const home = (out as string).replace(/^NFSHomeDirectory:\s*/, "").trim();
76:     if (home.startsWith("/")) return home;
77:   } catch {
78:     // dscl unavailable or user record not found
79:   }
80:   return null;
81: }
82: 
83: function lookupViaEtcPasswd(
84:   username: string,
85:   readFileFn: typeof readFileSync,
86: ): string | null {
87:   try {
88:     const passwd = readFileFn("/etc/passwd", "utf8") as string;
89:     for (const line of passwd.split("\n")) {
90:       const parts = line.split(":");
91:       if (parts[0] === username) {
92:         const home = parts[5];
93:         if (typeof home === "string" && home.startsWith("/")) return home;
94:       }
95:     }
96:   } catch {
97:     // /etc/passwd unavailable
98:   }
99:   return null;
100: }
101: 
102: function lookupHomeForUser(username: string): string {
103:   return lookupHomeForUserWith(
104:     username,
105:     process.platform,
106:     execFileSync,
107:     readFileSync,
108:   );
109: }
110: 
111: export function getPlatformKey(): "posix" | "windows" {
112:   return process.platform === "win32" ? "windows" : "posix";
113: }
114: 
115: export function getDeployMethod(): "symlink" | "copy" {
116:   return process.platform === "win32" ? "copy" : "symlink";
117: }
118: 
119: export function resolveAgentSkillPathFor(
120:   agent: AgentConfig,
121:   skillName: string,
122:   home: string,
123:   platform: "posix" | "windows",
124: ): string {
125:   if (!agent.skills) {
126:     throw new Error(
127:       `Agent "${agent.id}" does not have a skills deployment path. ` +
128:         `Deploy skills via another agent target that covers this agent natively (e.g. claude-code for github-copilot).`,
129:     );
130:   }
131:   return resolvePlaceholders(agent.skills[platform], skillName, home);
132: }
133: 
134: export function resolveAgentDetectPathFor(
135:   agent: AgentConfig,
136:   home: string,
137:   platform: "posix" | "windows",
138: ): string {
139:   return resolvePlaceholders(agent.detectPaths[platform], "", home);
140: }
141: 
142: export function resolveAgentSkillPath(
143:   agent: AgentConfig,
144:   skillName: string,
145:   home: string,
146: ): string {
147:   return resolveAgentSkillPathFor(agent, skillName, home, getPlatformKey());
148: }
149: 
150: export function resolveAgentDetectPath(
151:   agent: AgentConfig,
152:   home: string,
153: ): string {
154:   return resolveAgentDetectPathFor(agent, home, getPlatformKey());
155: }
156: 
157: export function resolvePlaceholders(
158:   segments: string[],
159:   skillName: string,
160:   home: string,
161:   repo?: string,
162: ): string {
163:   const { appdata, xdgConfig } = resolveRuntimePaths(home);
164:   const resolved = segments.map((seg) =>
165:     seg
166:       .replace("{home}", home)
167:       .replace("{name}", skillName)
168:       .replace("{appdata}", appdata)
169:       .replace("{xdg_config}", xdgConfig)
170:       .replace("{repo}", repo ?? ""),
171:   );
172:   const root = resolved.find((segment) => segment.length > 0) ?? home;
173:   return getPathApi(root).join(...resolved);
174: }

35:     const parsed = JSON.parse(content);

47:   await writeFile(filePath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\n`);

118:   if (entry.skill !== expected.skill) return null;
119:   if (entry.agent !== expected.agent) return null;
120:   if (
121:     (expected.kind === "skill-dir" || expected.kind === "file-write") &&
122:     (entry.kind === "skill-dir" || entry.kind === "file-write") &&
123:     entry.source !== expected.source
124:   ) {
125:     return null;
126:   }
127:   return entry;
128: }

(End of file - total 128 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import path from "node:path";
2: 
3: export interface RuntimePaths {
4:   appdata: string;
5:   xdgConfig: string;
6: }
7: 
8: type TargetRoot = "home" | "appdata" | "xdg_config" | "repo";
9: 
10: const TARGET_TEMPLATE_RE =
11:   /^\{(home|appdata|xdg_config|repo)\}(?<suffix>(?:[\\/].*)?)$/;
12: 
13: export function getPathApi(
14:   root: string,
15: ): typeof path.posix | typeof path.win32 {
16:   if (root.includes("\\") || /^[a-zA-Z]:/.test(root)) {
17:     return path.win32;
18:   }
19:   if (root.startsWith("/")) {
20:     return path.posix;
21:   }
22:   return process.platform === "win32" ? path.win32 : path.posix;
23: }
24: 
25: function normalizePathForComparison(
26:   candidate: string,
27:   pathApi: typeof path.posix | typeof path.win32,
28: ): string {
29:   const normalized = pathApi.normalize(candidate);
30:   return pathApi === path.win32 ? normalized.toLowerCase() : normalized;
31: }
32: 
33: function isSameOrDescendantPath(candidate: string, root: string): boolean {
34:   const pathApi = getPathApi(root);
35:   const normalizedCandidate = normalizePathForComparison(candidate, pathApi);
36:   const normalizedRoot = normalizePathForComparison(root, pathApi);
37:   return (
38:     normalizedCandidate === normalizedRoot ||
39:     normalizedCandidate.startsWith(normalizedRoot + pathApi.sep)
40:   );
41: }
42: 
43: export function resolveRuntimePaths(home: string): RuntimePaths {
44:   const appdataRaw = process.env.APPDATA;
45:   const homePathApi = getPathApi(home);
46:   const appdata =
47:     appdataRaw && getPathApi(appdataRaw).isAbsolute(appdataRaw)
48:       ? appdataRaw
49:       : homePathApi.join(home, "AppData", "Roaming");
50:   const xdgRaw = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME;
51:   const xdgConfig =
52:     xdgRaw && getPathApi(xdgRaw).isAbsolute(xdgRaw)
53:       ? xdgRaw
54:       : homePathApi.join(home, ".config");
55:   return { appdata, xdgConfig };
56: }
57: 
58: export function resolveTargetTemplate(
59:   template: string,
60:   home: string,
61:   repo?: string,
62: ): string {
63:   const { appdata, xdgConfig } = resolveRuntimePaths(home);
64:   const match = TARGET_TEMPLATE_RE.exec(template);
65:   if (!match) {
66:     throw new Error(`Invalid target template: ${template}`);
67:   }
68: 
69:   const root = match[1] as TargetRoot;
70:   if (root === "repo") {
71:     if (!repo) {
72:       throw new Error(
73:         `Target template uses {repo} but no manifest directory was provided: ${template}`,
74:       );
75:     }
76:     const suffix = match.groups?.suffix ?? "";
77:     const segments = suffix.split(/[\\/]+/).filter(Boolean);
78:     const repoPathApi = getPathApi(repo);
79:     const resolved =
80:       segments.length === 0 ? repo : repoPathApi.join(repo, ...segments);
81:     if (!isSameOrDescendantPath(resolved, repo)) {
82:       throw new Error(
83:         `Target template resolves outside its placeholder root: ${template}`,
84:       );
85:     }
86:     return suffix === "" ? repo : `${repo}${suffix}`;
87:   }
88: 
89:   const baseByRoot: Record<Exclude<TargetRoot, "repo">, string> = {
90:     home,
91:     appdata,
92:     xdg_config: xdgConfig,
93:   };
94:   const suffix = match.groups?.suffix ?? "";
95:   const segments = suffix.split(/[\\/]+/).filter(Boolean);
96:   const pathApi = getPathApi(baseByRoot[root]);
97:   const resolved =
98:     segments.length === 0
99:       ? baseByRoot[root]
100:       : pathApi.join(baseByRoot[root], ...segments);
101: 
102:   if (!isSameOrDescendantPath(resolved, baseByRoot[root])) {
103:     throw new Error(
104:       `Target template resolves outside its placeholder root: ${template}`,
105:     );
106:   }
107: 
108:   return suffix === "" ? baseByRoot[root] : `${baseByRoot[root]}${suffix}`;
109: }
