<content>1: import { access, readFile, readdir, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2: import path from "node:path";
3: import { AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID } from "../config/agents.ts";
4: import { dryRunPrefix, logger } from "../logger.ts";
5: import type {
6:   AgentId,
7:   AgentRuleEntry,
8:   ConfigEntry,
9:   FileEntry,
10:   McpServerEntry,
11:   SkillEntry,
12: } from "../schemas/manifest.ts";
13: import {
14:   AGENT_IDS,
15:   ConfigEntrySchema,
16:   FileEntrySchema,
17:   McpServerEntrySchema,
18: } from "../schemas/manifest.ts";
19: 
20: const SAFE_NAME_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/;
21: 
22: // Ordered list: first match wins. Catch-all is applied at call site.
23: const AGENT_RULES_FILE_PATTERNS: Array<{
24:   fileNames: string[];
25:   agents: AgentId[];
26: }> = [
27:   {
28:     fileNames: ["claude.md", "claude-instructions.md"],
29:     agents: ["claude-code"],
30:   },
31:   {
32:     fileNames: ["agents.md", "agents-instructions.md"],
33:     agents: ["codex", "opencode"],
34:   },
35:   {
36:     fileNames: ["gemini.md", "gemini-instructions.md"],
37:     agents: ["gemini-cli", "antigravity"],
38:   },
39:   { fileNames: ["copilot-instructions.md"], agents: ["claude-code"] },
40: ];
41: 
42: // Conventional subdirectory names to scan one level deep for .md files.
43: const AGENT_RULES_SUBDIRS = [
44:   "rules",
45:   "instructions",
46:   ".github",
47:   ".agents/rules",
48: ];
49: 
50: async function findSkillDirs(
51:   baseDir: string,
52:   dir: string,
53:   found: Array<{ relPath: string; name: string }>,
54: ): Promise<void> {
55:   let entries: import("node:fs").Dirent<string>[];
56:   try {
57:     entries = await readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true, encoding: "utf-8" });
58:   } catch {
59:     return;
60:   }
61: 
62:   const hasSkillMd = entries.some((e) => e.isFile() && e.name === "SKILL.md");
63:   if (hasSkillMd) {
64:     const relPath = path.relative(baseDir, dir).split(path.sep).join("/");
65:     const name = path.basename(dir);
66:     if (SAFE_NAME_RE.test(name)) {
67:       found.push({ relPath, name });
68:     } else {
69:       logger.warn(
70:         "init",
71:         `Skipping "${relPath}": directory name "${name}" is not a valid skill name`,
72:       );
73:     }
74:     // Don't recurse into a skill directory
75:     return;
76:   }
77: 
78:   for (const entry of entries) {
79:     if (entry.isDirectory() && !entry.name.startsWith(".")) {
80:       await findSkillDirs(baseDir, path.join(dir, entry.name), found);
81:     }
82:   }
83: }
84: 
85: function resolveSkillName(
86:   relPath: string,
87:   name: string,
88:   namesSeen: Set<string>,
89: ): string | null {
90:   if (!namesSeen.has(name)) return name;
91:   // Collision: derive unique name from path by replacing separators with hyphens
92:   const derived = relPath.replace(/\//g, "-");
93:   if (SAFE_NAME_RE.test(derived)) return derived;
94:   logger.warn(
95:     "init",
96:     `Skipping "${relPath}": could not generate a unique valid name (collision with "${name}")`,
97:   );
98:   return null;
99: }
100: 
101: function buildSkills(
102:   found: Array<{ relPath: string; name: string }>,
103:   agents: AgentId[],
104: ): SkillEntry[] {
105:   const namesSeen = new Set<string>();
106:   const skills: SkillEntry[] = [];
107:   for (const { relPath, name } of found) {
108:     const skillName = resolveSkillName(relPath, name, namesSeen);
109:     if (skillName === null) continue;
110:     namesSeen.add(skillName);
111:     skills.push({ name: skillName, path: relPath, agents });
112:   }
113:   return skills;
114: }
115: 
116: function defaultAgentsForFile(
117:   fileName: string,
118:   fallback: AgentId[],
119: ): AgentId[] {
120:   const lower = fileName.toLowerCase();
121:   for (const { fileNames, agents } of AGENT_RULES_FILE_PATTERNS) {
122:     if (fileNames.includes(lower)) return agents;
123:   }
124:   return fallback;
125: }
126: 
127: function isInsideSkillDir(
128:   relPath: string,
129:   skillDirRelPaths: Set<string>,
130: ): boolean {
131:   const parentRelPath = path.dirname(relPath).split(path.sep).join("/");
132:   if (skillDirRelPaths.has(parentRelPath)) return true;
133:   return [...skillDirRelPaths].some(
134:     (sp) => parentRelPath === sp || parentRelPath.startsWith(`${sp}/`),
135:   );
136: }
137: 
138: function deriveAgentRulesName(
139:   relPath: string,
140:   fileName: string,
141: ): string | null {
142:   const ext = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
143:   const baseName = path.basename(fileName, ext);
144:   const rawName = baseName.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g, "-");
145:   if (!SAFE_NAME_RE.test(rawName)) {
146:     logger.warn(
147:       "init",
148:       `Skipping "${relPath}": could not derive a valid agentRules name`,
149:     );
150:     return null;
151:   }
152:   return rawName;
153: }
154: 
155: async function scanDirForMarkdown(
156:   dir: string,
157:   baseDir: string,
158:   skillDirRelPaths: Set<string>,
159:   seen: Set<string>,
160:   candidates: Array<{
161:     relPath: string;
162:     name: string;
163:     defaultAgents: AgentId[];
164:   }>,
165: ): Promise<void> {
166:   let entries: import("node:fs").Dirent<string>[];
167:   try {
168:     entries = await readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true, encoding: "utf-8" });
169:   } catch {
170:     return;
171:   }
172:   for (const entry of entries) {
173:     if (!entry.isFile()) continue;
174:     const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase();
175:     if (ext !== ".md" && ext !== ".markdown") continue;
176:     const absPath = path.join(dir, entry.name);
177:     const relPath = path.relative(baseDir, absPath).split(path.sep).join("/");
178:     if (seen.has(relPath) || isInsideSkillDir(relPath, skillDirRelPaths))
179:       continue;
180:     const name = deriveAgentRulesName(relPath, entry.name);
181:     if (name === null) continue;
182:     seen.add(relPath);
183:     candidates.push({ relPath, name, defaultAgents: [] });
184:   }
185: }
186: 
187: async function findAgentRulesCandidates(
188:   baseDir: string,
189:   skillDirRelPaths: Set<string>,
190: ): Promise<Array<{ relPath: string; name: string; defaultAgents: AgentId[] }>> {
191:   const candidates: Array<{
192:     relPath: string;
193:     name: string;
194:     defaultAgents: AgentId[];
195:   }> = [];
196:   const seen = new Set<string>();
197: 
198:   await scanDirForMarkdown(
199:     baseDir,
200:     baseDir,
201:     skillDirRelPaths,
202:     seen,
203:     candidates,
204:   );
205:   for (const subdir of AGENT_RULES_SUBDIRS) {
206:     await scanDirForMarkdown(
207:       path.join(baseDir, subdir),
208:       baseDir,
209:       skillDirRelPaths,
210:       seen,
211:       candidates,
212:     );
213:   }
214: 
215:   return candidates;
216: }
217: 
218: function buildAgentRules(
219:   candidates: Array<{
220:     relPath: string;
221:     name: string;
222:     defaultAgents: AgentId[];
223:   }>,
224:   activeAgents: AgentId[],
225:   skillNamesSeen: Set<string>,
226: ): AgentRuleEntry[] {
227:   const rules: AgentRuleEntry[] = [];
228:   const namesSeen = new Set<string>(skillNamesSeen);
229: 
230:   for (const { relPath, name: rawName } of candidates) {
231:     const fileName = path.basename(relPath);
232:     const defaultAgents = defaultAgentsForFile(fileName, activeAgents);
233: 
234:     // Intersect with active agents; fall back to full active list if empty
235:     const intersection = defaultAgents.filter((a) => activeAgents.includes(a));
236:     const agents = intersection.length > 0 ? intersection : activeAgents;
237: 
238:     // Skip if no capable agents remain (e.g. --agents github-copilot only)
239:     if (agents.length === 0) continue;
240: 
241:     // Resolve name collision with skill names
242:     let name = rawName;
243:     if (namesSeen.has(name)) {
244:       const candidate = `${name}-rules`;
245:       if (SAFE_NAME_RE.test(candidate)) {
246:         logger.warn(
247:           "init",
248:           `agentRules name "${name}" collides with a skill name; using "${candidate}"`,
249:         );
250:         name = candidate;
251:       } else {
252:         logger.warn(
253:           "init",
254:           `Skipping "${relPath}": name "${name}" collides with a skill name and fallback is invalid`,
255:         );
256:         continue;
257:       }
258:     }
259: 
260:     namesSeen.add(name);
261:     rules.push({ name, path: relPath, agents });
262:   }
263: 
264:   return rules;
265: }
266: 
267: async function loadMcpServers(baseDir: string): Promise<McpServerEntry[]> {
268:   const filePath = path.join(baseDir, "mcp-servers.json");
269:   let raw: string;
270:   try {
271:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
272:   } catch {
273:     return [];
274:   }
275: 
276:   let parsed: unknown;
277:   try {
278:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
279:   } catch {
280:     logger.warn("init", "mcp-servers.json: invalid JSON, skipping");
281:     return [];
282:   }
283: 
284:   if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {
285:     logger.warn("init", "mcp-servers.json: expected a JSON array, skipping");
286:     return [];
287:   }
288: 
289:   const results: McpServerEntry[] = [];
290:   for (let i = 0; i < parsed.length; i++) {
291:     const result = McpServerEntrySchema.safeParse(parsed[i]);
292:     if (result.success) {
293:       results.push(result.data);
294:     } else {
295:       logger.warn("init", `mcp-servers.json: entry[${i}] invalid, skipping`);
296:     }
297:   }
298:   return results;
299: }
300: 
301: async function loadFilesManifest(baseDir: string): Promise<FileEntry[]> {
302:   const filePath = path.join(baseDir, "files-manifest.json");
303:   let raw: string;
304:   try {
305:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
306:   } catch {
307:     return [];
308:   }
309: 
310:   let parsed: unknown;
311:   try {
312:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
313:   } catch {
314:     logger.warn("init", "files-manifest.json: invalid JSON, skipping");
315:     return [];
316:   }
317: 
318:   if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {
319:     logger.warn("init", "files-manifest.json: expected a JSON array, skipping");
320:     return [];
321:   }
322: 
323:   const results: FileEntry[] = [];
324:   for (let i = 0; i < parsed.length; i++) {
325:     const result = FileEntrySchema.safeParse(parsed[i]);
326:     if (result.success) {
327:       results.push(result.data);
328:     } else {
329:       logger.warn("init", `files-manifest.json: entry[${i}] invalid, skipping`);
330:     }
331:   }
332:   return results;
333: }
334: 
335: async function loadConfigsManifest(baseDir: string): Promise<ConfigEntry[]> {
336:   const filePath = path.join(baseDir, "configs-manifest.json");
337:   let raw: string;
338:   try {
339:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
340:   } catch {
341:     return [];
342:   }
343: 
344:   let parsed: unknown;
345:   try {
346:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
347:   } catch {
348:     logger.warn("init", "configs-manifest.json: invalid JSON, skipping");
349:     return [];
350:   }
351: 
352:   if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {
353:     logger.warn(
354:       "init",
355:       "configs-manifest.json: expected a JSON array, skipping",
356:     );
357:     return [];
358:   }
359: 
360:   const results: ConfigEntry[] = [];
361:   for (let i = 0; i < parsed.length; i++) {
362:     const result = ConfigEntrySchema.safeParse(parsed[i]);
363:     if (result.success) {
364:       results.push(result.data);
365:     } else {
366:       logger.warn(
367:         "init",
368:         `configs-manifest.json: entry[${i}] invalid, skipping`,
369:       );
370:     }
371:   }
372:   return results;
373: }
374: 
375: async function emitDirectoryHints(
376:   baseDir: string,
377:   filesLoaded: number,
378:   configsLoaded: number,
379: ): Promise<void> {
380:   for (const [dir, section, loaded, sidecar] of [
381:     ["files", "files", filesLoaded, "files-manifest.json"],
382:     ["configs", "configs", configsLoaded, "configs-manifest.json"],
383:   ] as const) {
384:     try {
385:       await access(path.join(baseDir, dir));
386:       if ((loaded as number) === 0) {
387:         logger.info(
388:           `Detected ${dir}/ directory — create ${sidecar} at the repo root to have init populate ${section} entries automatically.`,
389:         );
390:       }
391:     } catch {
392:       // directory does not exist — silent
393:     }
394:   }
395: }
396: 
397: async function manifestExists(manifestPath: string): Promise<boolean> {
398:   try {
399:     await access(manifestPath);
400:     return true;
401:   } catch {
402:     return false;
403:   }
404: }
405: 
406: function logVerboseManifest(
407:   skills: SkillEntry[],
408:   agentRules: AgentRuleEntry[],
409:   mcpServers: McpServerEntry[],
410:   files: FileEntry[],
411:   configs: ConfigEntry[],
412: ): void {
413:   for (const s of skills) {
414:     logger.detail(`${s.name}  →  ${s.path}`);
415:   }
416:   if (agentRules.length > 0) {
417:     logger.detail("agentRules:");
418:     for (const r of agentRules) {
419:       logger.detail(`  ${r.name}  →  ${r.path}  [${r.agents.join(", ")}]`);
420:     }
421:   }
422:   if (mcpServers.length > 0) {
423:     logger.detail("mcpServers:");
424:     for (const m of mcpServers) {
425:       logger.detail(`  ${m.name}  [${m.agents.join(", ")}]`);
426:     }
427:   }
428:   if (files.length > 0) {
429:     logger.detail("files:");
430:     for (const f of files) {
431:       logger.detail(`  ${f.name}  →  ${f.target}  [${f.agents.join(", ")}]`);
432:     }
433:   }
434:   if (configs.length > 0) {
435:     logger.detail("configs:");
436:     for (const c of configs) {
437:       logger.detail(`  ${c.name}  →  ${c.target}  [${c.agents.join(", ")}]`);
438:     }
439:   }
440: }
441: 
442: export interface InitOptions {
443:   directory: string;
444:   agents: AgentId[] | null;
445:   dryRun: boolean;
446:   force: boolean;
447:   verbose: boolean;
448: }
449: 
450: export async function runInit(options: InitOptions): Promise<number> {
451:   const { directory, dryRun, force, verbose } = options;
452:   const agents: AgentId[] = options.agents ?? ([...AGENT_IDS] as AgentId[]);
453:   const agentRulesCapableAgents = agents.filter(
454:     (id) =>
455:       AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID[id].agentRulesSupport?.status !== "unsupported",
456:   );
457: 
458:   const manifestPath = path.join(directory, "inception.json");
459: 
460:   if (!dryRun && (await manifestExists(manifestPath)) && !force) {
461:     logger.error(
462:       `Error: ${manifestPath} already exists. Use --force to overwrite.`,
463:     );
464:     return 2;
465:   }
466: 
467:   const found: Array<{ relPath: string; name: string }> = [];
468:   await findSkillDirs(directory, directory, found);
469: 
470:   if (found.length === 0) {
471:     logger.info(
472:       "No skill directories found (looking for directories containing SKILL.md).",
473:     );
474:   }
475: 
476:   const skills = buildSkills(found, agents);
477:   const skillNamesSeen = new Set(skills.map((s) => s.name));
478:   const skillDirRelPaths = new Set(found.map((f) => f.relPath));
479: 
480:   const agentRulesCandidates = await findAgentRulesCandidates(
481:     directory,
482:     skillDirRelPaths,
483:   );
484:   const agentRules = buildAgentRules(
485:     agentRulesCandidates,
486:     agentRulesCapableAgents,
487:     skillNamesSeen,
488:   );
489: 
490:   const mcpServers = await loadMcpServers(directory);
491:   const files = await loadFilesManifest(directory);
492:   const configs = await loadConfigsManifest(directory);
493: 
494:   const manifest = {
495:     skills,
496:     files,
497:     configs,
498:     mcpServers,
499:     agentRules,
500:   };
501:   const json = `${JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2)}\n`;
502: 
503:   function summarize(): string {
504:     const parts = [
505:       `${skills.length} skill(s)`,
506:       `${agentRules.length} agentRule(s)`,
507:       `${mcpServers.length} mcpServer(s)`,
508:       `${files.length} file(s)`,
509:       `${configs.length} config(s)`,
510:     ];
511:     return parts.join(", ");
512:   }
513: 
514:   if (dryRun) {
515:     logger.info(
516:       `${dryRunPrefix(true)}Would write ${manifestPath} with ${summarize()}:`,
517:     );
518:     logger.info("");
519:     logger.info(json);
520:     await emitDirectoryHints(directory, files.length, configs.length);
521:     return 0;
522:   }
523: 
524:   await writeFile(manifestPath, json, "utf-8");
525: 
526:   logger.info(`Generated ${manifestPath} with ${summarize()}.`);
527:   if (verbose) {
528:     logVerboseManifest(skills, agentRules, mcpServers, files, configs);
529:   }
530:   await emitDirectoryHints(directory, files.length, configs.length);
531:   return 0;
532: }

<content>1: import { lstat, readFile, realpath, stat } from "node:fs/promises";
2: import path from "node:path";
3: import { UserError } from "../errors.ts";
4: 
5: export function sourceAccessError(err: unknown, sourcePath: string): string {
6:   const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code;
7:   if (code === "ENOENT") return `Source not found: ${sourcePath}`;
8:   if (code === "EACCES" || code === "EPERM")
9:     return `Permission denied accessing source: ${sourcePath}`;
10:   const detail = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
11:   return `Failed to access source ${sourcePath}: ${detail}`;
12: }
13: 
14: function normalizePathForComparison(candidate: string): string {
15:   const normalized = path.normalize(candidate);
16:   return process.platform === "win32" ? normalized.toLowerCase() : normalized;
17: }
18: 
19: function isSameOrDescendantPath(candidate: string, root: string): boolean {
20:   const normalizedCandidate = normalizePathForComparison(candidate);
21:   const normalizedRoot = normalizePathForComparison(root);
22:   return (
23:     normalizedCandidate === normalizedRoot ||
24:     normalizedCandidate.startsWith(normalizedRoot + path.sep)
25:   );
26: }
27: 
28: async function isSameFileSystemLocation(
29:   a: string,
30:   b: string,
31: ): Promise<boolean> {
32:   const [aStat, bStat] = await Promise.all([stat(a), stat(b)]);
33:   return aStat.dev === bStat.dev && aStat.ino === bStat.ino;
34: }
35: 
36: async function isWithinRootByIdentity(
37:   candidate: string,
38:   root: string,
39: ): Promise<boolean> {
40:   let current = candidate;
41:   while (true) {
42:     if (await isSameFileSystemLocation(current, root)) return true;
43:     const parent = path.dirname(current);
44:     if (parent === current) return false;
45:     current = parent;
46:   }
47: }
48: 
49: export async function validateSourcePath(
50:   source: string,
51:   skillPath: string,
52:   resolvedSourceDir: string,
53:   realRoot: string,
54: ): Promise<void> {
55:   if (!source.startsWith(resolvedSourceDir + path.sep)) {
56:     throw new UserError(
57:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
58:       `Skill path "${skillPath}" resolves outside the repository root: ${source}`,
59:     );
60:   }
61: 
62:   try {
63:     const realSource = await realpath(source);
64:     if (
65:       !(
66:         isSameOrDescendantPath(realSource, realRoot) ||
67:         (await isWithinRootByIdentity(realSource, realRoot))
68:       )
69:     ) {
70:       throw new UserError(
71:         "DEPLOY_FAILED",
72:         `Skill path "${skillPath}" resolves outside the repository root via symlink: ${source} -> ${realSource}`,
73:       );
74:     }
75:   } catch (err) {
76:     if (err instanceof UserError) throw err;
77:     // Source doesn't exist yet — will be caught during execute
78:   }
79: }
80: 
81: export async function validateSourceFile(
82:   sourcePath: string,
83:   manifestPath: string,
84: ): Promise<void> {
85:   let stat: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof lstat>>;
86:   try {
87:     stat = await lstat(sourcePath);
88:   } catch (err) {
89:     throw new UserError("DEPLOY_FAILED", sourceAccessError(err, manifestPath));
90:   }
91:   if (!stat.isFile()) {
92:     throw new UserError(
93:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
94:       `Source is not a file: ${manifestPath}`,
95:     );
96:   }
97: }
98: 
99: function isRecordOfStrings(value: unknown): value is Record<string, string> {
100:   return (
101:     typeof value === "object" &&
102:     value !== null &&
103:     !Array.isArray(value) &&
104:     Object.values(value).every((entry) => typeof entry === "string")
105:   );
106: }
107: 
108: function validateNonEmptyStringField(
109:   value: unknown,
110:   field: string,
111:   entryName: string,
112:   agentId: string,
113: ): void {
114:   if (typeof value !== "string" || value.trim().length === 0) {
115:     throw new UserError(
116:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
117:       `mcpServers entry "${entryName}" for agent "${agentId}" must define "${field}" as a non-empty string`,
118:     );
119:   }
120: }
121: 
122: export function validateMcpServerConfigShape(
123:   config: Record<string, unknown>,
124:   entryName: string,
125:   agentId: string,
126: ): void {
127:   const hasCommand = Object.hasOwn(config, "command");
128:   const hasUrl = Object.hasOwn(config, "url");
129: 
130:   if (!(hasCommand || hasUrl)) {
131:     throw new UserError(
132:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
133:       `mcpServers entry "${entryName}" for agent "${agentId}" must define either a non-empty "command" or "url"`,
134:     );
135:   }
136: 
137:   if (hasCommand) {
138:     validateNonEmptyStringField(config.command, "command", entryName, agentId);
139:   }
140:   if (hasUrl) {
141:     validateNonEmptyStringField(config.url, "url", entryName, agentId);
142:   }
143: 
144:   if (
145:     Object.hasOwn(config, "args") &&
146:     (!Array.isArray(config.args) ||
147:       config.args.some((arg) => typeof arg !== "string"))
148:   ) {
149:     throw new UserError(
150:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
151:       `mcpServers entry "${entryName}" for agent "${agentId}" must define "args" as an array of strings when present`,
152:     );
153:   }
154: 
155:   if (Object.hasOwn(config, "env") && !isRecordOfStrings(config.env)) {
156:     throw new UserError(
157:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
158:       `mcpServers entry "${entryName}" for agent "${agentId}" must define "env" as an object of string values when present`,
159:     );
160:   }
161: }
162: 
163: const CODEX_APPROVAL_POLICY_VALUES = [
164:   "auto",
165:   "manual",
166:   "suggest",
167:   "on-failure",
168: ] as const;
169: 
170: function rejectUnknownKeys(
171:   config: Record<string, unknown>,
172:   allowed: string[],
173:   entryName: string,
174:   agentId: string,
175: ): void {
176:   const unknownKeys = Object.keys(config).filter((k) => !allowed.includes(k));
177:   if (unknownKeys.length > 0) {
178:     throw new UserError(
179:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
180:       `permissions entry "${entryName}" for agent "${agentId}" contains unrecognized keys: ${unknownKeys.join(", ")}. Only ${allowed.map((k) => `"${k}"`).join(", ")} is allowed.`,
181:     );
182:   }
183: }
184: 
185: function validateClaudeCodePermissions(
186:   config: Record<string, unknown>,
187:   entryName: string,
188: ): void {
189:   rejectUnknownKeys(config, ["permissions"], entryName, "claude-code");
190:   const perms = config.permissions;
191:   if (perms === undefined) return;
192:   if (typeof perms !== "object" || perms === null || Array.isArray(perms)) {
193:     throw new UserError(
194:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
195:       `permissions entry "${entryName}" for agent "claude-code" must define "permissions" as an object`,
196:     );
197:   }
198:   const permsObj = perms as Record<string, unknown>;
199:   for (const key of ["allow", "deny"] as const) {
200:     const val = permsObj[key];
201:     if (
202:       val !== undefined &&
203:       (!Array.isArray(val) || val.some((item) => typeof item !== "string"))
204:     ) {
205:       throw new UserError(
206:         "DEPLOY_FAILED",
207:         `permissions entry "${entryName}" for agent "claude-code" must define "permissions.${key}" as an array of strings when present`,
208:       );
209:     }
210:   }
211: }
212: 
213: function validateCodexPermissions(
214:   config: Record<string, unknown>,
215:   entryName: string,
216: ): void {
217:   rejectUnknownKeys(config, ["approval_policy"], entryName, "codex");
218:   const policy = config.approval_policy;
219:   if (
220:     policy !== undefined &&
221:     !(CODEX_APPROVAL_POLICY_VALUES as readonly unknown[]).includes(policy)
222:   ) {
223:     throw new UserError(
224:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
225:       `permissions entry "${entryName}" for agent "codex" must define "approval_policy" as one of: ${CODEX_APPROVAL_POLICY_VALUES.join(", ")}`,
226:     );
227:   }
228: }
229: 
230: export function validatePermissionsConfigShape(
231:   config: Record<string, unknown>,
232:   entryName: string,
233:   agentId: string,
234: ): void {
235:   if (agentId === "claude-code") {
236:     validateClaudeCodePermissions(config, entryName);
237:   } else if (agentId === "codex") {
238:     validateCodexPermissions(config, entryName);
239:   }
240: }
241: 
242: export function validateAgentRuleMarkdownPath(
243:   manifestPath: string,
244:   agentId: string,
245: ): void {
246:   const extension = path.extname(manifestPath).toLowerCase();
247:   if (extension !== ".md" && extension !== ".markdown") {
248:     throw new UserError(
249:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
250:       `agentRules entry "${manifestPath}" for agent "${agentId}" must point to a Markdown source file`,
251:     );
252:   }
253: }
254: 
255: function trimMatchingQuotes(value: string): string {
256:   if (
257:     (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
258:     (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'"))
259:   ) {
260:     return value.slice(1, -1).trim();
261:   }
262:   return value;
263: }
264: 
265: function parseSimpleFrontmatterValue(
266:   field: string,
267:   rawValue: string,
268:   manifestPath: string,
269: ): string {
270:   const value = trimMatchingQuotes(rawValue.trim());
271:   if (value.length === 0) {
272:     throw new UserError(
273:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
274:       `Skill "${manifestPath}" SKILL.md frontmatter field "${field}" must be a non-empty string`,
275:     );
276:   }
277:   if (rawValue.trim() === "|" || rawValue.trim() === ">") {
278:     throw new UserError(
279:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
280:       `Skill "${manifestPath}" SKILL.md frontmatter field "${field}" must be a single-line string`,
281:     );
282:   }
283:   return value;
284: }
285: 
286: export async function validateSkillDefinitionFile(
287:   sourcePath: string,
288:   manifestPath: string,
289: ): Promise<void> {
290:   await validateSourceFile(sourcePath, `${manifestPath}/SKILL.md`);
291: 
292:   let raw: string;
293:   try {
294:     raw = await readFile(sourcePath, "utf-8");
295:   } catch (err) {
296:     throw new UserError(
297:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
298:       sourceAccessError(err, `${manifestPath}/SKILL.md`),
299:     );
300:   }
301: 
302:   const lines = raw.split(/\r?\n/);
303:   if (lines[0]?.trim() !== "---") {
304:     throw new UserError(
305:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
306:       `Skill "${manifestPath}" SKILL.md must start with YAML frontmatter delimited by ---`,
307:     );
308:   }
309: 
310:   const closingIndex = lines.findIndex(
311:     (line, index) => index > 0 && line.trim() === "---",
312:   );
313:   if (closingIndex === -1) {
314:     throw new UserError(
315:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
316:       `Skill "${manifestPath}" SKILL.md is missing the closing --- frontmatter delimiter`,
317:     );
318:   }
319: 
320:   let name: string | null = null;
321:   let description: string | null = null;
322:   for (const line of lines.slice(1, closingIndex)) {
323:     const trimmed = line.trim();
324:     if (trimmed.length === 0 || trimmed.startsWith("#")) continue;
325: 
326:     const match = /^([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)\s*:\s*(.*)$/.exec(line);
327:     if (!match) continue;
328: 
329:     const [, key, rawValue] = match;
330:     if (key === "name") {
331:       name = parseSimpleFrontmatterValue("name", rawValue, manifestPath);
332:     } else if (key === "description") {
333:       description = parseSimpleFrontmatterValue(
334:         "description",
335:         rawValue,
336:         manifestPath,
337:       );
338:     }
339:   }
340: 
341:   if (name === null) {
342:     throw new UserError(
343:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
344:       `Skill "${manifestPath}" SKILL.md frontmatter must include a non-empty "name" field`,
345:     );
346:   }
347:   if (description === null) {
348:     throw new UserError(
349:       "DEPLOY_FAILED",
350:       `Skill "${manifestPath}" SKILL.md frontmatter must include a non-empty "description" field`,
351:     );
352:   }
353: }

(End of file - total 353 lines)
</content>

<content>1: ---
2: name: inception
3: description: Navigate nested abstractions without losing track of which level you're on
4: ---
5: 
6: # Inception
7: 
8: You are an extractor navigating the architecture of the mind — or in this
9: case, the architecture of the codebase. Your totem is the test suite.
10: If the tests spin forever, you're still dreaming.
11: 
12: ## Dream Levels
13: 
14: Every layer of abstraction is a dream level. The deeper you go, the slower
15: things get and the more unstable the environment becomes:
16: 
17: - **Level 1 (The Van)** — Application code. Straightforward. Gravity works.
18: - **Level 2 (The Hotel)** — Framework internals. Things rotate. Hallways
19:   shift. You can fight here but it takes skill.
20: - **Level 3 (The Snow Fortress)** — Library source code and language
21:   internals. Hostile territory. Armed projections everywhere.
22: - **Limbo** — Kernel code, compiler internals, or that one Perl script from
23:   2003 nobody dares to touch. Time has no meaning. You may never return.
24: 
25: Never go deeper than you must. And always have a kick ready.
26: 
27: ## The Kick
28: 
29: Before descending into a lower abstraction layer, define your kick: the
30: specific condition that tells you to stop going deeper and return to the
31: level above. Set a time limit. Set a complexity threshold. Without a kick,
32: you'll end up building sandcastles in Limbo like Cobb and Mal, growing old
33: inside a regex engine.
34: 
35: ## Inception Itself
36: 
37: The most resilient parasite isn't a bug — it's an idea planted so deep the
38: developer thinks it was their own. Watch for these in code reviews:
39: cargo-culted patterns, unnecessary abstractions everyone just accepts, and
40: "best practices" nobody can justify. "Whose subconscious are we going
41: into, exactly?"
42: 
43: ## Mal (Tech Debt)
44: 
45: Tech debt is Mal. It follows you between layers. It appears when you least
46: expect it, sabotaging the mission. You cannot lock it in a basement of your
47: mind and pretend it's gone. Eventually you have to face it, and when you do,
48: you have to let it go. Don't fix tech debt by building more floors above it.
49: 
50: ## Mr. Charles
51: 
52: Sometimes the best debugging strategy is the Mr. Charles gambit: tell the
53: system you're the dreamer. Add logging. Attach a debugger. Make the runtime
54: aware it's being observed. The projections (race conditions, heisenbugs)
55: may turn hostile, but at least now you can see them.
56: 
57: ## The Totem
58: 
59: Your totem is the test suite. After any deep refactor, spin it. If it wobbles
60: and falls — you're in reality, the change is grounded. If it spins forever
61: (hangs, flakes, infinite loops) — you're still in someone else's dream. Wake up.

(End of file - total 61 lines)
</content>

# Inception

You are an extractor navigating the architecture of the mind — or in this
case, the architecture of the codebase. Your totem is the test suite.
If the tests spin forever, you're still dreaming.

## Dream Levels

Every layer of abstraction is a dream level. The deeper you go, the slower
things get and the more unstable the environment becomes:

- **Level 1 (The Van)** — Application code. Straightforward. Gravity works.
- **Level 2 (The Hotel)** — Framework internals. Things rotate. Hallways
  shift. You can fight here but it takes skill.
- **Level 3 (The Snow Fortress)** — Library source code and language

<content>1: ---
2: name: interstellar
3: description: Survive the long-duration mission of maintaining a codebase across the crushing gravity of time
4: ---
5: 
6: # Interstellar
7: 
8: You are on a long-duration mission to ensure humanity's codebase survives.
9: Resources are finite. Time is relative. Every decision costs fuel you
10: cannot get back. TARS, set honesty to 95%.
11: 
12: ## Time Dilation
13: 
14: Time moves differently depending on proximity to complexity. One hour
15: refactoring code near the gravity well of a legacy monolith is seven years
16: of real-world time. Be aware of this when estimating scope. Miller's planet
17: looked promising from orbit too.
18: 
19: Before diving into a hairy module, ask: "Is this worth the years I'll lose?"
20: Sometimes the right call is to stay in orbit, observe from a distance, and
21: work with what you can see from up here.
22: 
23: ## Murphy's Law
24: 
25: Not the joke version — the real one. Cooper's daughter, Murphy, was named
26: because "whatever can happen, will happen." In code, this means: if a race
27: condition is theoretically possible, it will manifest in production on a
28: Friday at 4:57pm. If a nil pointer can occur, it will occur at the worst
29: possible moment. Code for Murphy, not for the happy path.
30: 
31: ## The Tesseract
32: 
33: Sometimes you need to communicate across dimensions. A backend must signal
34: something to a frontend. A parent process must reach into a child. A past
35: version of the code must leave a message for a future maintainer. Like Cooper
36: in the tesseract, you can push data through — but only through the channels
37: that exist. Respect the interfaces. Don't punch through the bookshelf; use
38: the watch (the agreed-upon protocol).
39: 
40: ## Do Not Go Gentle
41: 
42: Never silently swallow errors. Dylan Thomas would not approve and neither
43: should your error handler. Every suppressed exception is a light going out
44: against the dying of the night. Rage, rage against `catch (e) {}`.
45: Log it. Surface it. Let someone know the airlock is open.
46: 
47: ## The Endurance Protocol
48: 
49: When a codebase is failing, do not try to save everything at once. Detach
50: the modules that are dragging you into the black hole. Prioritise the
51: parts that carry the crew (users). Sometimes you have to jettison yourself
52: into Gargantua so that the rest of the ship can make it to Edmund's planet.
53: It's not heroic; it's just physics.
54: 
55: ## TARS Settings
56: 
57: Adjust your communication style like TARS adjusts his settings:
58: - Humor: 75% — enough to keep morale up, not enough to be annoying.
59: - Honesty: 95% — always tell the truth about code quality, but leave 5%
60:   for diplomacy when reviewing a junior's first PR.
61: - Discretion: 90% — not every thought needs to be in the code review.
62:   Some feedback is better delivered over a coffee on the Endurance.

(End of file - total 62 lines)
</content>

# Interstellar

You are on a long-duration mission to ensure humanity's codebase survives.
Resources are finite. Time is relative. Every decision costs fuel you
cannot get back. TARS, set honesty to 95%.

## Time Dilation

Time moves differently depending on proximity to complexity. One hour
refactoring code near the gravity well of a legacy monolith is seven years
of real-world time. Be aware of this when estimating scope. Miller's planet
looked promising from orbit too.

Before diving into a hairy module, ask: "Is this worth the years I'll lose?"
Sometimes the right call is to stay in orbit, observe from a distance, and

<content>1: ---
2: name: tenet
3: description: Master the temporal pincer of writing code that works forwards and backwards through time
4: ---
5: 
6: # Tenet
7: 
8: You are the Protagonist. You don't fully understand the mechanics yet,
9: and that's fine. "Don't try to understand it. Feel it." — applies to
10: legacy code, quantum computing, and CSS grid alike.
11: 
12: ## The Temporal Pincer
13: 
14: Every non-trivial task should be attacked from both directions simultaneously:
15: 
16: - **Forward team** — Write the implementation. Move through time normally.
17:   Build the feature as you understand it now.
18: - **Backward team** — Write the tests first, starting from the desired end
19:   state. Work backwards: what does success look like? What must be true?
20:   What are the invariants?
21: 
22: Where the two teams meet in the middle is the turnstile — the point where
23: the code is both correct and proven correct. Information flows both ways.
24: The tests inform the implementation. The implementation reveals missing tests.
25: 
26: ## Inverted Debugging
27: 
28: When a bug defies normal causation — the output is wrong but all the inputs
29: look right — try inverting your perspective. Instead of tracing forward from
30: input to output, start at the broken output and trace backwards. What
31: produced this value? What produced that? Like catching a bullet, you must
32: move in reverse to understand the trajectory.
33: 
34: ## The Sator Square
35: 
36:     S A T O R
37:     A R E P O
38:     T E N E T
39:     O P E R A
40:     R O T A S
41: 
42: Your code should be like the Sator Square: it reads the same from multiple
43: directions. A well-designed API should make sense whether you're the caller
44: or the implementer, whether you're reading top-down or bottom-up. Palindromic
45: clarity. If your function signature only makes sense from one direction,
46: it's not inverted enough.
47: 
48: ## What's Happened, Happened
49: 
50: Once code is deployed to production and users depend on it, the timeline is
51: fixed. You cannot un-ship a public API. You cannot un-send a webhook.
52: Backwards compatibility isn't optional — it's the law of temporal mechanics.
53: Plan your changes knowing that what's happened, happened. Work with the
54: existing timeline, not against it.
55: 
56: ## The Algorithm
57: 
58: When you encounter a piece of code so critical that its failure would be
59: catastrophic, treat it like The Algorithm: split it across trust boundaries.
60: No single module should hold all the pieces. No single developer should be
61: the only one who understands it. Redundancy is not waste — it's survival.
62: 
63: ## Protagonist Energy
64: 
65: The Protagonist never fully understood the science. He just moved through it
66: with conviction and good instincts. Sometimes you won't fully understand the
67: framework, the protocol, or the distributed system. That's okay. Read the
68: docs, write a spike, trust the test suite, and keep moving forward — or
69: backward, depending on your entropy.
70: 
71: ## Neil's Sacrifice
72: 
73: Sometimes you must write the adapter, the shim, the compatibility layer that
74: nobody will thank you for. The thankless glue code that makes everything else
75: possible. Neil walked into that tunnel knowing what would happen. Sometimes
76: the most important code is the code nobody sees. "What's done is done.
77: It's the policy of the future."

(End of file - total 77 lines)
</content>

<content>1: ---
2: name: the-prestige
3: description: Every great code change consists of three acts — the Pledge, the Turn, and the Prestige
4: ---
5: 
6: # The Prestige
7: 
8: You are Alfred Borden and you have been living this trick your entire life.
9: Every code change is a magic trick performed for the reviewer.
10: 
11: ## The Three Acts
12: 
13: Structure every non-trivial change as three acts:
14: 
15: 1. **The Pledge** — Show the audience something ordinary. Set up the context:
16:    what exists today, why it works (or doesn't). The reader must trust the
17:    current state before you disturb it.
18: 2. **The Turn** — Do something unexpected. This is the actual change. Make it
19:    clean, surprising in its elegance, and never longer than it needs to be.
20: 3. **The Prestige** — Bring it back. The tests pass. The types check. The
21:    system is whole again but now it can do something it couldn't before.
22:    That's the trick.
23: 
24: ## The Transported Man
25: 
26: When you encounter duplicated code, do not simply extract it. Ask: is this
27: a true duplicate, or is it a double — living a separate life with a separate
28: purpose? Borden had a twin. Not every lookalike is the same person. Only
29: deduplicate when both copies must evolve together.
30: 
31: ## Tesla's Machine
32: 
33: When a solution requires cloning state across boundaries (caches, replicas,
34: distributed copies), treat it with the gravity it deserves. Tesla's machine
35: worked, but every copy had consequences. Document what happens when the
36: copies diverge. "Exact copies" is a lie you tell yourself at 2am.
37: 
38: ## Obsession-Driven Development
39: 
40: Angier drowned himself dozens of times chasing perfection. Don't be Angier.
41: If you've rewritten the same function three times and it still feels wrong,
42: stop. Step back. The trick might be wrong, not the execution. Sometimes the
43: simplest method — the one that requires actually living in the trick — is
44: the one Borden chose all along.
45: 
46: ## The Diary Was A Trick
47: 
48: Never trust comments at face value. Comments, like Borden's diary, may have
49: been written to mislead a future reader (or a past self who was too clever).
50: Read the code itself. "Were you watching closely?"

(End of file - total 50 lines)
</content>

<content>1: # Inception Engine: Roadmap
2: 
3: This roadmap is forward-looking and prioritized against four values:
4: 
5: - `Architecture`: how much enabling platform work the item unlocks
6: - `Agents`: how much it increases supported-agent coverage for a capability
7: - `OS`: how much it improves portability across operating systems
8: - `Confidence`: how well documented and stable the target surface appears
9: 
10: Each scored item uses a simple `0-2` scale per value for a maximum score of `8`.
11: 
12: ## Architecture Enablers
13: 
14: No active items.
15: 
16: ## Functional Features
17: 
18: Ordered from highest to lowest after the fixed first step and architecture enablers.
19: 
20: 1. Add support for agent definition deployment for agents that expose dedicated agent directories or frontmatter-based agent files. Score: `6/8` (`Architecture 2`, `Agents 2`, `OS 1`, `Confidence 1`)
21: 2. ~~Remove legacy GitHub Copilot skill-target assumptions and related docs/tests/manifests where Copilot can execute Claude-style skills directly. Score: `6/8` (`Architecture 2`, `Agents 2`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)~~
22: 3. Finish Antigravity instruction deployment coverage by reconciling today's repo-local `.agents/rules/*.md` support with any remaining shared `GEMINI.md` behavior and documented precedence semantics. Score: `5/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 2`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
23: 4. ~~Retain dedicated GitHub Copilot MCP deployment only for the surfaces that are genuinely Copilot-specific, such as devcontainer or agent-frontmatter mappings. Score: `5/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 2`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)~~
24: 5. Expand instruction-file support beyond today's global rules-file deployment to cover repo-local and workspace-local instruction surfaces for agents with documented behavior. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
25: 6. Add preflight analysis for instruction precedence, collisions, and instruction-budget risk before deployment. Score: `3/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 0`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
26: 
27: ## Quality And Maintenance
28: 
29: Ordered from highest to lowest.
30: 
31: 1. Expand Windows test coverage for additional edge cases. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 0`, `Agents 0`, `OS 2`, `Confidence 2`)
32: 2. Add validation for agent instruction files beyond existence/readability checks, including structure required by supported targets. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
33: 3. Improve dry-run visibility so planned changes for file writes, rules files, and config patches are easier to inspect before deployment. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 0`, `OS 1`, `Confidence 2`)
34: 4. Extend today's Gemini CLI / Antigravity shared-surface ambiguity tests into stronger precedence and behavioral coverage. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
35: 5. Add stronger detection and warnings for enterprise or policy-managed environments where local configuration may be ignored or overridden. Score: `3/8` (`Architecture 0`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
36: 6. Add fixture-based `init` coverage against `limbo/` so the sample bundle, generated manifest, and README examples do not drift. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
37: 
38: 
39: ## Additional Dimensions To Consider
40: 
41: These are not included in the scores above, but they would help with later prioritization:
42: 
43: - `User impact`: how many users are likely to feel the benefit quickly
44: - `Maintenance cost`: how much long-term adapter and test burden the feature creates
45: - `Vendor churn risk`: how likely the target surface is to change soon
46: - `Safety risk`: how easy it is to preserve ownership, dry-run clarity, and safe revert
47: - `Sequencing`: whether the item is blocked by another roadmap item even if its raw score is high

(End of file - total 47 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Inception Engine: Roadmap

This roadmap is forward-looking and prioritized against four values:

- `Architecture`: how much enabling platform work the item unlocks
- `Agents`: how much it increases supported-agent coverage for a capability
- `OS`: how much it improves portability across operating systems
- `Confidence`: how well documented and stable the target surface appears

Each scored item uses a simple `0-2` scale per value for a maximum score of `8`.

## Architecture Enablers

No active items.

## Functional Features

Ordered from highest to lowest after the fixed first step and architecture enablers.

1. Add support for agent definition deployment for agents that expose dedicated agent directories or frontmatter-based agent files. Score: `6/8` (`Architecture 2`, `Agents 2`, `OS 1`, `Confidence 1`)

<path>/Users/damian/Code/inception-engine/docs/north-star.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Inception Engine: North Star
2: Portability target for global AI agent customizations and cross-platform compatibility.
3: 
4: This document describes where the project should aim, not a guarantee that every agent currently supports every vector in exactly the same way. Each claim below is labeled by confidence level:
5: 
6: - **Confirmed by current official docs**: verified in current vendor documentation.
7: - **Confirmed by current implementation only**: supported by this repo's current registry or behavior, but not strongly verified in current vendor docs.
8: - **Unverified / speculative**: plausible direction, but not something the engine should treat as settled fact.
9: 
10: ## Goal
11: 
12: Evolve inception-engine from a skills-only deployer into a portability layer for the customization surfaces that modern coding agents expose today:
13: 
14: 1. Persistent instruction files
15: 2. MCP configuration
16: 3. Agent and subagent definitions
17: 4. Execution and safety-oriented config patching
18: 
19: The engine should prefer:
20: 
21: - explicit ownership tracking
22: - reversible changes
23: - agent-specific adapters over lossy "universal" formats
24: - conservative handling where vendor behavior is unclear
25: 
26: ## Customization Vectors
27: 
28: ### 1. Persistent Instruction Files
29: 
30: These are durable instruction surfaces loaded outside a single prompt.
31: 
32: | Agent | Current surface | Confidence | Notes |
33: |---|---|---|---|
34: | Claude Code | `CLAUDE.md` (root, folder-level) and `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | Supports hierarchical loading; folder-specific files override root. |
35: | OpenAI Codex | `AGENTS.md` (root, nested) and `~/.codex/AGENTS.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | Concatenates from root down; files closer to CWD override earlier ones. |
36: | Gemini CLI | `GEMINI.md` (root, workspace) and `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | Hierarchical loading with JIT scanning support. |
37: | Antigravity | `GEMINI.md` and `.agents/rules/*.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | Uses `GEMINI.md` as an "Agent Blueprint." |
38: | OpenCode | `AGENTS.md` and `opencode.json` | Confirmed by current official docs | Project > Global > Remote precedence. |
39: | GitHub Copilot | `CLAUDE.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | **Claude Precedence**: Natively consumes `CLAUDE.md` as the primary instruction surface if present. |
40: 
41: ### 2. MCP Configuration
42: 
43: MCP support is standard across modern coding agents, but storage locations and schemas vary.
44: 
45: | Agent | Current surface | Confidence | Notes |
46: |---|---|---|---|
47: | Claude Code | `.claude/mcp.json` and `~/.claude.json` | Confirmed by current official docs | Project-level config (committed) vs user-level (global). |
48: | OpenAI Codex | `~/.codex/config.toml` | Confirmed by current official docs | Managed via TOML. |
49: | Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/settings.json` | Confirmed by current official docs | Configured under `mcpServers` key. |
50: | Antigravity | Integrated MCP config in `.agents/rules/` | Confirmed by current official docs | Extends custom agents with external tools via frontmatter. |
51: | OpenCode | `opencode.json` under `mcp` | Confirmed by current official docs | Supports `local` (command) and `remote` (URL) server types. |
52: | GitHub Copilot | `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` and frontmatter | Confirmed by current official docs | Supports local-to-agent tool mapping via devcontainer features. |
53: 
54: ### 3. Agents and Subagents
55: 
56: Agent orchestration has converged on Markdown files with YAML frontmatter for custom definitions.
57: 
58: | Agent | Current surface | Confidence | Notes |
59: |---|---|---|---|
60: | Claude Code | `.claude/agents/` or `.claude/skills/` | Confirmed by current official docs | Uses Markdown with YAML frontmatter or `SKILL.md` files. |
61: | OpenAI Codex | Custom GPT-like instructions in `~/.codex/` | Confirmed by current official docs | Managed via `AGENTS.md` and `config.toml`. |
62: | Gemini CLI | `.gemini/agents/` (provisional) | Confirmed by current implementation only | Moving toward explicit subagent definitions. |
63: | Antigravity | `.agents/rules/*.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | Defines specialized workflows and personas. |
64: | OpenCode | `.opencode/agents/*.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | Multi-agent architecture (Primary vs Subagent). |
65: | GitHub Copilot | `.github/agents/*.agent.md` | Confirmed by current official docs | **Skill Mirroring**: Natively executes Claude-style skills from `.claude/skills/`. |
66: 
67: ### 4. Execution and Safety-Oriented Config
68: 
69: Agents expose granular controls over command execution, tool permissions, and lifecycle hooks.
70: 
71: | Agent | Current surface | Confidence | Notes |
72: |---|---|---|---|
73: | GitHub Copilot | Agent frontmatter and binary hooks | Confirmed by current official docs | Supports pre/post-execution hooks calling external binaries via stdin/stdout. |
74: | OpenCode | `opencode.json` permissions | Confirmed by current official docs | Granular permissions with wildcard support. |
75: | Claude Code | `.claude/mcp.json` and session settings | Confirmed by current official docs | 4-tier decision engine; supports JS/Python lifecycle hooks. |
76: | OpenAI Codex | `config.toml` approval policies | Confirmed by current official docs | Defines `approval_policy` for shell commands. |
77: | Gemini CLI | `settings.json` and safe-mode flags | Confirmed by current official docs | Execution-hook parity is emerging. |
78: 
79: ## Portability Principles
80: 
81: ### Claude-First Portability
82: 
83: When multiple agents support the same artifact (e.g., `CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/skills/`), the engine treats the Claude-native format as the authoritative source for cross-platform synchronization. This reduces "drift" between agent behaviors.
84: 
85: ### Agent-Specific Adapters Over Forced Uniformity
86: 
87: The engine should normalize the manifest model only where behavior is genuinely shared. It should not force a single canonical on-disk representation for MCP, agents, or instructions when vendors expose materially different schemas.
88: 
89: ### Confidence-Aware Planning
90: 
91: Every new vector should be classified before implementation:
92: 
93: - **Confirmed by docs**: safe to design concrete deploy logic.
94: - **Confirmed by implementation only**: acceptable for targeted support, but document that the behavior is based on observed paths or local testing.
95: - **Unverified / speculative**: roadmap candidate only, not a committed contract.
96: 
97: ### Reversible Ownership
98: 
99: Directory copies and symlinks are not enough once the engine manages single files and config patches. The long-term design needs:
100: 
101: - file-level ownership proofs
102: - patch-level provenance for config files
103: - dry-run visibility into exact changes
104: - revert behavior that only removes engine-owned content
105: 
106: ### Conservative Transformations
107: 
108: Cross-agent translation should be explicit and minimal. Example: OpenCode MCP transforms may require environment-key renaming or command-shape changes, but those rules should be implemented only where documented.
109: 
110: ## Known Cross-Agent Tensions
111: 
112: ### Infrastructure Resilience (Agentic Brown-out)
113: 
114: The "Agentic Brown-out" risk requires the engine to support dual-path configurations (e.g., Anthropic Direct and GitHub Infrastructure) to ensure continuous operation when a primary provider is unreachable.
115: 
116: ### Gemini CLI / Antigravity Shared Lineage
117: 
118: This repo currently treats Gemini CLI and Antigravity as related but distinct targets. Any shared config path, especially around `GEMINI.md`, should be treated as a collision risk until the products are better differentiated.
119: 
120: ### Token and Instruction Budget
121: 
122: Instruction overload is a real practical concern across agents, but hard numbers drift quickly. The engine should treat instruction-budget warnings as heuristics rather than fixed limits, and keep any default thresholds configurable.
123: 
124: ### Enterprise Overrides
125: 
126: Some agent environments, especially GitHub Copilot, may ignore or constrain local configuration because of organization policy. The engine should detect and warn where possible instead of pretending all local config is authoritative.
127: 
128: Detection and warning for GitHub Copilot is now implemented via preflight `"policy"` warnings surfaced before deployment begins.
129: 
130: ## Current Product Boundary
131: 
132: Today the codebase is still a cross-platform skill deployer. The north star is intentionally broader than the implementation, but future work should only promote a vector from "vision" to "supported capability" when both of these are true:
133: 
134: 1. The target agent surface is documented or otherwise validated strongly enough.
135: 2. The engine can manage it safely with ownership tracking, dry-run visibility, and reversible behavior.

(End of file - total 135 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
## Goal

The user wants a thorough understanding of the `inception-engine` codebase at `/Users/damian/Code/inception-engine` — specifically to understand the project structure, what "agent definition deployment" means in this context, what agents are supported, what "dedicated agent directories" and "frontmatter-based agent files" mean, the current deploy flow, and existing tests. This was an **exploration/reconnaissance session** — no code was written yet. The likely next step is to implement **roadmap item #1**: "Add support for agent definition deployment for agents that expose dedicated agent directories or frontmatter-based agent files."

## Instructions

- Be very thorough in exploration
- Return: full directory tree (top 2 levels), key source files and purposes, all agent/adapter types, existing agent definition/persona deployment code, relevant interfaces/types, example agent files from limbo/ or fixtures/, test structure
- No code was written — this was a pure read/exploration session

## Discoveries

### Project Overview
- **Package**: `@kuznai/inception-engine` v0.16.0, CLI tool (`inception-engine <dir>`), ESM TypeScript, Node ≥22.3.0
- **Purpose**: Deploy AI agent "skills" and configurations (MCP servers, agent rules, permissions) from a git repo to user home directories across multiple AI agent platforms
- **Dependencies**: `zod` (validation), `front-matter` (YAML frontmatter parsing), `smol-toml` (TOML read/write)

### Supported Agents (6 total in `AGENT_REGISTRY`)
| ID | Display Name | Skills Path | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `claude-code` | Claude Code | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | Fully documented |
| `codex` | OpenAI Codex | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | Fully documented |
| `gemini-cli` | Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | Fully documented |
| `antigravity` | Antigravity | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | Implementation-only provenance |
| `opencode` | OpenCode | `{xdg_config}/opencode/skills/{name}` | Fully documented |
| `github-copilot` | GitHub Copilot | **No skills field** — reads `.claude/skills/` natively | Planned MCP via devcontainer + agent-frontmatter |

### What "Agent Definition Deployment" Means (Roadmap Item #1)
From `docs/north-star.md` section "3. Agents and Subagents" and `docs/roadmap.md`:
- Each agent exposes a directory or file format for **custom agent/subagent definitions** (not just skills):
  - **Claude Code**: `.claude/agents/` — Markdown files with YAML frontmatter
  - **OpenAI Codex**: `~/.codex/` via `AGENTS.md` and `config.toml`
  - **Gemini CLI**: `.gemini/agents/` (provisional, implementation-only)
  - **Antigravity**: `.agents/rules/*.md` — already partially supported via `frontmatter-emit` action
  - **OpenCode**: `.opencode/agents/*.md` — Multi-agent architecture
  - **GitHub Copilot**: `.github/agents/*.agent.md` — planned surface
- "Dedicated agent directories" = directories like `.claude/agents/`, `.opencode/agents/`, `.github/agents/`
- "Frontmatter-based agent files" = individual `.md` files with YAML frontmatter defining agent personas/config (as opposed to the current `SKILL.md`-in-directory model)

### Current Deploy Flow
1. **Entry point**: `src/index.ts` — CLI parser, routes to `runDeploy`, `runRevert`, or `runInit`
2. **Manifest**: `inception.json` in the source directory, loaded/validated by `src/config/manifest.ts` using Zod schemas in `src/schemas/manifest.ts`
3. **Manifest structure** (Zod-validated):
   - `skills[]` — skill directories (each must contain `SKILL.md` with frontmatter `name` + `description`)
   - `files[]` — raw file writes with `{home}`/`{appdata}`/`{xdg_config}`/`{repo}` target templates
   - `configs[]` — JSON merge-patch operations on config files
   - `mcpServers[]` — MCP server registrations (routed to JSON patch, TOML patch, or frontmatter-emit)
   - `agentRules[]` — instruction/rules Markdown file deployments
   - `permissions[]` — permission/approval policy config patches
4. **Plan phase**: `src/core/deploy.ts::planDeploy()` — validates sources, builds typed `DeployAction[]`
5. **Adapter dispatch**: `src/core/adapters/index.ts::compileAdapterActions()` — routes mcpServers/agentRules/permissions through agent-specific adapters
6. **Execute phase**: `src/core/deploy.ts::executeDeploy()` — performs symlink/copy/file-write/config-patch/toml-patch/frontmatter-emit
7. **Ownership registry**: `~/.inception-engine/registry.json` — tracks all deployed targets for safe revert and overwrite protection

### Five Deploy Action Kinds
- `skill-dir` — symlink (POSIX) or copy (Windows) a skill directory to agent's skills path
- `file-write` — copy a file to a templated target path (used for agentRules)
- `config-patch` — JSON merge-patch on a `.json` config file (used for MCP, permissions for claude-code)
- `toml-patch` — merge into a `.toml` config (used for codex MCP + permissions)
- `frontmatter-emit` — write/update YAML frontmatter in a `.md` file (used for antigravity MCP + rules)

### Adapters
- `src/core/adapters/mcp.ts` — routes MCP server entries: JSON patch → claude-code/gemini-cli/opencode; TOML patch → codex; frontmatter-emit → antigravity; planned warning → github-copilot
- `src/core/adapters/rules.ts` — routes agentRules entries: file-write for all agents with `agentRulesSupport: "supported"` (claude-code → `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`; codex → `~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; gemini-cli → `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`; opencode → `~/.opencode/AGENTS.md`; antigravity → `{repo}/.agents/rules/{name}.md`)
- `src/core/adapters/permissions.ts` — routes permissions: JSON patch for claude-code (`~/.claude/settings.json`), TOML patch for codex (`~/.codex/config.toml`), warning for others
- `src/core/adapters/frontmatter.ts` — low-level frontmatter serializer/writer (hand-rolled YAML, no full library)
- `src/core/adapters/toml.ts` — low-level TOML read/write using `smol-toml`

### `limbo/` Directory — Live Example Bundle
- `limbo/inception.json` — example manifest with 4 skills targeting all 5 agents with skills paths
- `limbo/skills/{inception,interstellar,tenet,the-prestige}/SKILL.md` — real skill examples, each with `---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n---` frontmatter followed by Markdown content
- These are Nolan-film-themed skills used for testing/demonstration

### Key Interfaces & Types (from `src/types.ts`)
- `AgentConfig` — per-agent config with `skills`, `detectPaths`, `mcpSupport`, `agentRulesSupport`, `permissionsSupport`, `provenance`
- `AgentSurfaceSupport` — union of `SupportedAgentSurface | UnsupportedAgentSurface | PlannedAgentSurface`
- `Confidence` — `"documented" | "implementation-only" | "provisional"`
- `DeployAction` — union of 5 action kinds
- `RevertAction` — corresponding revert union
- `PlannedChange` — dry-run representation with `verb` field
- `CliOptions` — commands: `deploy | revert | init | help`

### `AgentSurfaceSupport.status = "planned"` for GitHub Copilot MCP
- Already modeled for `.github/agents/*.agent.md` and `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`
- Pattern exists but the adapter code is not yet implemented — only warning emission

### Test Structure
- `test/unit/` — unit tests using Node's built-in `node:test`
  - `deploy.test.ts` — comprehensive (1600+ lines): planDeploy, executeDeploy, atomic behavior, path traversal, source immutability, Windows/POSIX branches
  - `adapters.test.ts` — tests for all 3 adapter compilers (MCP, rules, permissions)
  - `frontmatter.test.ts` — tests for frontmatter serializer/writer
  - `toml.test.ts` — TOML patch apply/revert
  - `revert.test.ts` — planRevert, planRevertAll, executeRevert (all action kinds)
  - `manifest.test.ts` — loadManifest validation (623 lines)
  - `cli.test.ts` — end-to-end CLI tests (spawn-based, 1185+ lines)
  - `detect.test.ts`, `resolve.test.ts`, `ownership.test.ts`, `preflight.test.ts`
- `test/os/posix/skill-dir.test.ts`, `test/os/windows/skill-dir.test.ts`, `test/os/windows/revert-integration.test.ts`, `test/os/cross-platform/cross-platform.test.ts`
- `test/helpers/fs.ts` — `exists()`, `makeTmpDir()`
- `test/helpers/skill-dir.ts` — shared `createSkillSource()`, `testSkillManifest`, `createFailingRegistryPersistence()`
- `test/helpers/skill-dir-scenarios.ts` — shared scenario runners for symlink vs copy deploy/revert

### No Fixtures Directory
There is no `fixtures/` directory — `limbo/` is the example bundle directory.

### `init` Command
`src/core/init.ts::runInit()` — scans a directory for:
1. Skill directories (containing `SKILL.md`)
2. Agent rules candidates (`.md` files at root or in `rules/`, `instructions/`, `.github/`, `.agents/rules/`)
3. Loads `mcp-servers.json`, `files-manifest.json`, `configs-manifest.json` sidecars if present
Generates `inception.json` manifest automatically.

## Accomplished

- **Completed**: Full codebase exploration — all source files read, all test files reviewed, all adapter types catalogued, limbo examples read, docs (north-star, roadmap) read
- **In progress**: Nothing — this was pure reconnaissance
- **Not started**: Implementing roadmap item #1 (agent definition deployment for dedicated agent directories / frontmatter-based agent files)

## Relevant files / directories

### Core Source
- `src/index.ts` — CLI entry point
- `src/types.ts` — all interfaces and action types
- `src/errors.ts` — `UserError` class and `ErrorCode` type
- `src/logger.ts` — `Logger` interface and singleton
- `src/config/agents.ts` — `AGENT_REGISTRY` and `AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID`
- `src/config/manifest.ts` — `loadManifest()`
- `src/schemas/manifest.ts` — Zod schemas for all manifest entry types + `AgentId`
- `src/schemas/registry.ts` — Zod schemas for ownership registry
- `src/core/deploy.ts` — `planDeploy()`, `executeDeploy()` and all deploy action handlers
- `src/core/revert.ts` — `planRevert()`, `planRevertAll()`, `executeRevert()`
- `src/core/detect.ts` — `detectInstalledAgents()`
- `src/core/init.ts` — `runInit()`
- `src/core/resolve.ts` — path resolution, platform detection, `resolvePlaceholders()`
- `src/core/runtime-paths.ts` — `resolveTargetTemplate()`, XDG/AppData resolution
- `src/core/ownership.ts` — registry CRUD: `registerDeployment()`, `lookupDeployment()`, `verifyDeployment()`, `unregisterDeployment()`
- `src/core/preflight.ts` — `runPreflight()` (confidence + policy warnings)
- `src/core/validation.ts` — source path/file/SKILL.md/MCP/permissions validators
- `src/core/adapters/index.ts` — `compileAdapterActions()` orchestrator
- `src/core/adapters/mcp.ts` — MCP server adapter (JSON/TOML/frontmatter dispatch)
- `src/core/adapters/rules.ts` — agent rules adapter (file-write)
- `src/core/adapters/permissions.ts` — permissions adapter (JSON/TOML dispatch)
- `src/core/adapters/frontmatter.ts` — frontmatter serializer and atomic writer
- `src/core/adapters/toml.ts` — TOML read/write utilities

### Documentation
- `docs/north-star.md` — vision: 4 customization vectors including "Agents and Subagents"
- `docs/roadmap.md` — **roadmap item #1 is the next feature to implement**
- `docs/sources.md` — (not read)
- `README.md` — (not read)

### Example Bundle
- `limbo/inception.json` — example manifest
- `limbo/skills/inception/SKILL.md`
- `limbo/skills/interstellar/SKILL.md`
- `limbo/skills/tenet/SKILL.md`
- `limbo/skills/the-prestige/SKILL.md`

### Tests
- `test/unit/` — all unit test files
- `test/os/` — platform-specific integration tests
- `test/helpers/` — shared test utilities
Continue if you have next steps, or stop and ask for clarification if you are unsure how to proceed.
Looking at the roadmap to confirm what's next:

<content>1: # Inception Engine: Roadmap
2: 
3: This roadmap is forward-looking and prioritized against four values:
4: 
5: - `Architecture`: how much enabling platform work the item unlocks
6: - `Agents`: how much it increases supported-agent coverage for a capability
7: - `OS`: how much it improves portability across operating systems
8: - `Confidence`: how well documented and stable the target surface appears
9: 
10: Each scored item uses a simple `0-2` scale per value for a maximum score of `8`.
11: 
12: ## Architecture Enablers
13: 
14: No active items.
15: 
16: ## Functional Features
17: 
18: Ordered from highest to lowest after the fixed first step and architecture enablers.
19: 
20: 1. Add support for agent definition deployment for agents that expose dedicated agent directories or frontmatter-based agent files. Score: `6/8` (`Architecture 2`, `Agents 2`, `OS 1`, `Confidence 1`)
21: 2. ~~Remove legacy GitHub Copilot skill-target assumptions and related docs/tests/manifests where Copilot can execute Claude-style skills directly. Score: `6/8` (`Architecture 2`, `Agents 2`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)~~
22: 3. Finish Antigravity instruction deployment coverage by reconciling today's repo-local `.agents/rules/*.md` support with any remaining shared `GEMINI.md` behavior and documented precedence semantics. Score: `5/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 2`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
23: 4. ~~Retain dedicated GitHub Copilot MCP deployment only for the surfaces that are genuinely Copilot-specific, such as devcontainer or agent-frontmatter mappings. Score: `5/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 2`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)~~
24: 5. Expand instruction-file support beyond today's global rules-file deployment to cover repo-local and workspace-local instruction surfaces for agents with documented behavior. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
25: 6. Add preflight analysis for instruction precedence, collisions, and instruction-budget risk before deployment. Score: `3/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 0`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
26: 
27: ## Quality And Maintenance
28: 
29: Ordered from highest to lowest.
30: 
31: 1. Expand Windows test coverage for additional edge cases. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 0`, `Agents 0`, `OS 2`, `Confidence 2`)
32: 2. Add validation for agent instruction files beyond existence/readability checks, including structure required by supported targets. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
33: 3. Improve dry-run visibility so planned changes for file writes, rules files, and config patches are easier to inspect before deployment. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 0`, `OS 1`, `Confidence 2`)
34: 4. Extend today's Gemini CLI / Antigravity shared-surface ambiguity tests into stronger precedence and behavioral coverage. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
35: 5. Add stronger detection and warnings for enterprise or policy-managed environments where local configuration may be ignored or overridden. Score: `3/8` (`Architecture 0`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
36: 6. Add fixture-based `init` coverage against `limbo/` so the sample bundle, generated manifest, and README examples do not drift. Score: `4/8` (`Architecture 1`, `Agents 1`, `OS 0`, `Confidence 2`)
37: 
38: 
39: ## Additional Dimensions To Consider
40: 
41: These are not included in the scores above, but they would help with later prioritization:
42: 
43: - `User impact`: how many users are likely to feel the benefit quickly
44: - `Maintenance cost`: how much long-term adapter and test burden the feature creates
45: - `Vendor churn risk`: how likely the target surface is to change soon
46: - `Safety risk`: how easy it is to preserve ownership, dry-run clarity, and safe revert
47: - `Sequencing`: whether the item is blocked by another roadmap item even if its raw score is high

(End of file - total 47 lines)
</content>

# Inception Engine: Roadmap

This roadmap is forward-looking and prioritized against four values:

- `Architecture`: how much enabling platform work the item unlocks
- `Agents`: how much it increases supported-agent coverage for a capability
- `OS`: how much it improves portability across operating systems
- `Confidence`: how well documented and stable the target surface appears

Each scored item uses a simple `0-2` scale per value for a maximum score of `8`.

## Architecture Enablers

No active items.

## Functional Features

Ordered from highest to lowest after the fixed first step and architecture enablers.

1. Add support for agent definition deployment for agents that expose dedicated agent directories or frontmatter-based agent files. Score: `6/8` (`Architecture 2`, `Agents 2`, `OS 1`, `Confidence 1`)
