<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { access, readFile, readdir, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2: import path from "node:path";
3: import { AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID } from "../config/agents.ts";
4: import { dryRunPrefix, logger } from "../logger.ts";
5: import type {
6:   AgentId,
7:   AgentRuleEntry,
8:   ConfigEntry,
9:   FileEntry,
10:   McpServerEntry,
11:   SkillEntry,
12: } from "../schemas/manifest.ts";
13: import {
14:   AGENT_IDS,
15:   ConfigEntrySchema,
16:   FileEntrySchema,
17:   McpServerEntrySchema,
18: } from "../schemas/manifest.ts";
19: 
20: const SAFE_NAME_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/;
21: 
22: // Ordered list: first match wins. Catch-all is applied at call site.
23: const AGENT_RULES_FILE_PATTERNS: Array<{
24:   fileNames: string[];
25:   agents: AgentId[];
26: }> = [
27:   {
28:     fileNames: ["claude.md", "claude-instructions.md"],
29:     agents: ["claude-code"],
30:   },
31:   {
32:     fileNames: ["agents.md", "agents-instructions.md"],
33:     agents: ["codex", "opencode"],
34:   },
35:   {
36:     fileNames: ["gemini.md", "gemini-instructions.md"],
37:     agents: ["gemini-cli", "antigravity"],
38:   },
39:   { fileNames: ["copilot-instructions.md"], agents: ["claude-code"] },
40: ];
41: 
42: // Conventional subdirectory names to scan one level deep for .md files.
43: const AGENT_RULES_SUBDIRS = [
44:   "rules",
45:   "instructions",
46:   ".github",
47:   ".agents/rules",
48: ];
49: 
50: async function findSkillDirs(
51:   baseDir: string,
52:   dir: string,
53:   found: Array<{ relPath: string; name: string }>,
54: ): Promise<void> {
55:   let entries: import("node:fs").Dirent<string>[];
56:   try {
57:     entries = await readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true, encoding: "utf-8" });
58:   } catch {
59:     return;
60:   }
61: 
62:   const hasSkillMd = entries.some((e) => e.isFile() && e.name === "SKILL.md");
63:   if (hasSkillMd) {
64:     const relPath = path.relative(baseDir, dir).split(path.sep).join("/");
65:     const name = path.basename(dir);
66:     if (SAFE_NAME_RE.test(name)) {
67:       found.push({ relPath, name });
68:     } else {
69:       logger.warn(
70:         "init",
71:         `Skipping "${relPath}": directory name "${name}" is not a valid skill name`,
72:       );
73:     }
74:     // Don't recurse into a skill directory
75:     return;
76:   }
77: 
78:   for (const entry of entries) {
79:     if (entry.isDirectory() && !entry.name.startsWith(".")) {
80:       await findSkillDirs(baseDir, path.join(dir, entry.name), found);
81:     }
82:   }
83: }
84: 
85: function resolveSkillName(
86:   relPath: string,
87:   name: string,
88:   namesSeen: Set<string>,
89: ): string | null {
90:   if (!namesSeen.has(name)) return name;
91:   // Collision: derive unique name from path by replacing separators with hyphens
92:   const derived = relPath.replace(/\//g, "-");
93:   if (SAFE_NAME_RE.test(derived)) return derived;
94:   logger.warn(
95:     "init",
96:     `Skipping "${relPath}": could not generate a unique valid name (collision with "${name}")`,
97:   );
98:   return null;
99: }
100: 
101: function buildSkills(
102:   found: Array<{ relPath: string; name: string }>,
103:   agents: AgentId[],
104: ): SkillEntry[] {
105:   const namesSeen = new Set<string>();
106:   const skills: SkillEntry[] = [];
107:   for (const { relPath, name } of found) {
108:     const skillName = resolveSkillName(relPath, name, namesSeen);
109:     if (skillName === null) continue;
110:     namesSeen.add(skillName);
111:     skills.push({ name: skillName, path: relPath, agents });
112:   }
113:   return skills;
114: }
115: 
116: function defaultAgentsForFile(
117:   fileName: string,
118:   fallback: AgentId[],
119: ): AgentId[] {
120:   const lower = fileName.toLowerCase();
121:   for (const { fileNames, agents } of AGENT_RULES_FILE_PATTERNS) {
122:     if (fileNames.includes(lower)) return agents;
123:   }
124:   return fallback;
125: }
126: 
127: function isInsideSkillDir(
128:   relPath: string,
129:   skillDirRelPaths: Set<string>,
130: ): boolean {
131:   const parentRelPath = path.dirname(relPath).split(path.sep).join("/");
132:   if (skillDirRelPaths.has(parentRelPath)) return true;
133:   return [...skillDirRelPaths].some(
134:     (sp) => parentRelPath === sp || parentRelPath.startsWith(`${sp}/`),
135:   );
136: }
137: 
138: function deriveAgentRulesName(
139:   relPath: string,
140:   fileName: string,
141: ): string | null {
142:   const ext = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
143:   const baseName = path.basename(fileName, ext);
144:   const rawName = baseName.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g, "-");
145:   if (!SAFE_NAME_RE.test(rawName)) {
146:     logger.warn(
147:       "init",
148:       `Skipping "${relPath}": could not derive a valid agentRules name`,
149:     );
150:     return null;
151:   }
152:   return rawName;
153: }
154: 
155: async function scanDirForMarkdown(
156:   dir: string,
157:   baseDir: string,
158:   skillDirRelPaths: Set<string>,
159:   seen: Set<string>,
160:   candidates: Array<{
161:     relPath: string;
162:     name: string;
163:     defaultAgents: AgentId[];
164:   }>,
165: ): Promise<void> {
166:   let entries: import("node:fs").Dirent<string>[];
167:   try {
168:     entries = await readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true, encoding: "utf-8" });
169:   } catch {
170:     return;
171:   }
172:   for (const entry of entries) {
173:     if (!entry.isFile()) continue;
174:     const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase();
175:     if (ext !== ".md" && ext !== ".markdown") continue;
176:     const absPath = path.join(dir, entry.name);
177:     const relPath = path.relative(baseDir, absPath).split(path.sep).join("/");
178:     if (seen.has(relPath) || isInsideSkillDir(relPath, skillDirRelPaths))
179:       continue;
180:     const name = deriveAgentRulesName(relPath, entry.name);
181:     if (name === null) continue;
182:     seen.add(relPath);
183:     candidates.push({ relPath, name, defaultAgents: [] });
184:   }
185: }
186: 
187: async function findAgentRulesCandidates(
188:   baseDir: string,
189:   skillDirRelPaths: Set<string>,
190: ): Promise<Array<{ relPath: string; name: string; defaultAgents: AgentId[] }>> {
191:   const candidates: Array<{
192:     relPath: string;
193:     name: string;
194:     defaultAgents: AgentId[];
195:   }> = [];
196:   const seen = new Set<string>();
197: 
198:   await scanDirForMarkdown(
199:     baseDir,
200:     baseDir,
201:     skillDirRelPaths,
202:     seen,
203:     candidates,
204:   );
205:   for (const subdir of AGENT_RULES_SUBDIRS) {
206:     await scanDirForMarkdown(
207:       path.join(baseDir, subdir),
208:       baseDir,
209:       skillDirRelPaths,
210:       seen,
211:       candidates,
212:     );
213:   }
214: 
215:   return candidates;
216: }
217: 
218: function buildAgentRules(
219:   candidates: Array<{
220:     relPath: string;
221:     name: string;
222:     defaultAgents: AgentId[];
223:   }>,
224:   activeAgents: AgentId[],
225:   skillNamesSeen: Set<string>,
226: ): AgentRuleEntry[] {
227:   const rules: AgentRuleEntry[] = [];
228:   const namesSeen = new Set<string>(skillNamesSeen);
229: 
230:   for (const { relPath, name: rawName } of candidates) {
231:     const fileName = path.basename(relPath);
232:     const defaultAgents = defaultAgentsForFile(fileName, activeAgents);
233: 
234:     // Intersect with active agents; fall back to full active list if empty
235:     const intersection = defaultAgents.filter((a) => activeAgents.includes(a));
236:     const agents = intersection.length > 0 ? intersection : activeAgents;
237: 
238:     // Skip if no capable agents remain (e.g. --agents github-copilot only)
239:     if (agents.length === 0) continue;
240: 
241:     // Resolve name collision with skill names
242:     let name = rawName;
243:     if (namesSeen.has(name)) {
244:       const candidate = `${name}-rules`;
245:       if (SAFE_NAME_RE.test(candidate)) {
246:         logger.warn(
247:           "init",
248:           `agentRules name "${name}" collides with a skill name; using "${candidate}"`,
249:         );
250:         name = candidate;
251:       } else {
252:         logger.warn(
253:           "init",
254:           `Skipping "${relPath}": name "${name}" collides with a skill name and fallback is invalid`,
255:         );
256:         continue;
257:       }
258:     }
259: 
260:     namesSeen.add(name);
261:     rules.push({ name, path: relPath, agents });
262:   }
263: 
264:   return rules;
265: }
266: 
267: async function loadMcpServers(baseDir: string): Promise<McpServerEntry[]> {
268:   const filePath = path.join(baseDir, "mcp-servers.json");
269:   let raw: string;
270:   try {
271:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
272:   } catch {
273:     return [];
274:   }
275: 
276:   let parsed: unknown;
277:   try {
278:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
279:   } catch {
280:     logger.warn("init", "mcp-servers.json: invalid JSON, skipping");
281:     return [];
282:   }
283: 
284:   if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {
285:     logger.warn("init", "mcp-servers.json: expected a JSON array, skipping");
286:     return [];
287:   }
288: 
289:   const results: McpServerEntry[] = [];
290:   for (let i = 0; i < parsed.length; i++) {
291:     const result = McpServerEntrySchema.safeParse(parsed[i]);
292:     if (result.success) {
293:       results.push(result.data);
294:     } else {
295:       logger.warn("init", `mcp-servers.json: entry[${i}] invalid, skipping`);
296:     }
297:   }
298:   return results;
299: }
300: 
301: async function loadFilesManifest(baseDir: string): Promise<FileEntry[]> {
302:   const filePath = path.join(baseDir, "files-manifest.json");
303:   let raw: string;
304:   try {
305:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
306:   } catch {
307:     return [];
308:   }
309: 
310:   let parsed: unknown;
311:   try {
312:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
313:   } catch {
314:     logger.warn("init", "files-manifest.json: invalid JSON, skipping");
315:     return [];
316:   }
317: 
318:   if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {
319:     logger.warn("init", "files-manifest.json: expected a JSON array, skipping");
320:     return [];
321:   }
322: 
323:   const results: FileEntry[] = [];
324:   for (let i = 0; i < parsed.length; i++) {
325:     const result = FileEntrySchema.safeParse(parsed[i]);
326:     if (result.success) {
327:       results.push(result.data);
328:     } else {
329:       logger.warn("init", `files-manifest.json: entry[${i}] invalid, skipping`);
330:     }
331:   }
332:   return results;
333: }
334: 
335: async function loadConfigsManifest(baseDir: string): Promise<ConfigEntry[]> {
336:   const filePath = path.join(baseDir, "configs-manifest.json");
337:   let raw: string;
338:   try {
339:     raw = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
340:   } catch {
341:     return [];
342:   }
343: 
344:   let parsed: unknown;
345:   try {
346:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
347:   } catch {
348:     logger.warn("init", "configs-manifest.json: invalid JSON, skipping");
349:     return [];
350:   }
351: 
352:   if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) {
353:     logger.warn(
354:       "init",
355:       "configs-manifest.json: expected a JSON array, skipping",
356:     );
357:     return [];
358:   }
359: 
360:   const results: ConfigEntry[] = [];
361:   for (let i = 0; i < parsed.length; i++) {
362:     const result = ConfigEntrySchema.safeParse(parsed[i]);
363:     if (result.success) {
364:       results.push(result.data);
365:     } else {
366:       logger.warn(
367:         "init",
368:         `configs-manifest.json: entry[${i}] invalid, skipping`,
369:       );
370:     }
371:   }
372:   return results;
373: }
374: 
375: async function emitDirectoryHints(
376:   baseDir: string,
377:   filesLoaded: number,
378:   configsLoaded: number,
379: ): Promise<void> {
380:   for (const [dir, section, loaded, sidecar] of [
381:     ["files", "files", filesLoaded, "files-manifest.json"],
382:     ["configs", "configs", configsLoaded, "configs-manifest.json"],
383:   ] as const) {
384:     try {
385:       await access(path.join(baseDir, dir));
386:       if ((loaded as number) === 0) {
387:         logger.info(
388:           `Detected ${dir}/ directory — create ${sidecar} at the repo root to have init populate ${section} entries automatically.`,
389:         );
390:       }
391:     } catch {
392:       // directory does not exist — silent
393:     }
394:   }
395: }
396: 
397: async function manifestExists(manifestPath: string): Promise<boolean> {
398:   try {
399:     await access(manifestPath);
400:     return true;
401:   } catch {
402:     return false;
403:   }
404: }
405: 
406: function logVerboseManifest(
407:   skills: SkillEntry[],
408:   agentRules: AgentRuleEntry[],
409:   mcpServers: McpServerEntry[],
410:   files: FileEntry[],
411:   configs: ConfigEntry[],
412: ): void {
413:   for (const s of skills) {
414:     logger.detail(`${s.name}  →  ${s.path}`);
415:   }
416:   if (agentRules.length > 0) {
417:     logger.detail("agentRules:");
418:     for (const r of agentRules) {
419:       logger.detail(`  ${r.name}  →  ${r.path}  [${r.agents.join(", ")}]`);
420:     }
421:   }
422:   if (mcpServers.length > 0) {
423:     logger.detail("mcpServers:");
424:     for (const m of mcpServers) {
425:       logger.detail(`  ${m.name}  [${m.agents.join(", ")}]`);
426:     }
427:   }
428:   if (files.length > 0) {
429:     logger.detail("files:");
430:     for (const f of files) {
431:       logger.detail(`  ${f.name}  →  ${f.target}  [${f.agents.join(", ")}]`);
432:     }
433:   }
434:   if (configs.length > 0) {
435:     logger.detail("configs:");
436:     for (const c of configs) {
437:       logger.detail(`  ${c.name}  →  ${c.target}  [${c.agents.join(", ")}]`);
438:     }
439:   }
440: }
441: 
442: export interface InitOptions {
443:   directory: string;
444:   agents: AgentId[] | null;
445:   dryRun: boolean;
446:   force: boolean;
447:   verbose: boolean;
448: }
449: 
450: export async function runInit(options: InitOptions): Promise<number> {
451:   const { directory, dryRun, force, verbose } = options;
452:   const agents: AgentId[] = options.agents ?? ([...AGENT_IDS] as AgentId[]);
453:   const agentRulesCapableAgents = agents.filter(
454:     (id) =>
455:       AGENT_REGISTRY_BY_ID[id].agentRulesSupport?.status !== "unsupported",
456:   );
457: 
458:   const manifestPath = path.join(directory, "inception.json");
459: 
460:   if (!dryRun && (await manifestExists(manifestPath)) && !force) {
461:     logger.error(
462:       `Error: ${manifestPath} already exists. Use --force to overwrite.`,
463:     );
464:     return 2;
465:   }
466: 
467:   const found: Array<{ relPath: string; name: string }> = [];
468:   await findSkillDirs(directory, directory, found);
469: 
470:   if (found.length === 0) {
471:     logger.info(
472:       "No skill directories found (looking for directories containing SKILL.md).",
473:     );
474:   }
475: 
476:   const skills = buildSkills(found, agents);
477:   const skillNamesSeen = new Set(skills.map((s) => s.name));
478:   const skillDirRelPaths = new Set(found.map((f) => f.relPath));
479: 
480:   const agentRulesCandidates = await findAgentRulesCandidates(
481:     directory,
482:     skillDirRelPaths,
483:   );
484:   const agentRules = buildAgentRules(
485:     agentRulesCandidates,
486:     agentRulesCapableAgents,
487:     skillNamesSeen,
488:   );
489: 
490:   const mcpServers = await loadMcpServers(directory);
491:   const files = await loadFilesManifest(directory);
492:   const configs = await loadConfigsManifest(directory);
493: 
494:   const manifest = {
495:     skills,
496:     files,
497:     configs,
498:     mcpServers,
499:     agentRules,
500:   };
501:   const json = `${JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2)}\n`;
502: 
503:   function summarize(): string {
504:     const parts = [
505:       `${skills.length} skill(s)`,
506:       `${agentRules.length} agentRule(s)`,
507:       `${mcpServers.length} mcpServer(s)`,
508:       `${files.length} file(s)`,
509:       `${configs.length} config(s)`,
510:     ];
511:     return parts.join(", ");
512:   }
513: 
514:   if (dryRun) {
515:     logger.info(
516:       `${dryRunPrefix(true)}Would write ${manifestPath} with ${summarize()}:`,
517:     );
518:     logger.info("");
519:     logger.info(json);
520:     await emitDirectoryHints(directory, files.length, configs.length);
521:     return 0;
522:   }
523: 
524:   await writeFile(manifestPath, json, "utf-8");
525: 
526:   logger.info(`Generated ${manifestPath} with ${summarize()}.`);
527:   if (verbose) {
528:     logVerboseManifest(skills, agentRules, mcpServers, files, configs);
529:   }
530:   await emitDirectoryHints(directory, files.length, configs.length);
531:   return 0;
532: }

(End of file - total 532 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # inception-engine
2: 
3: Plant skills directly into the minds of your installed AI coding agents — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, and OpenCode. One command. They'll think they thought of it themselves.
4: 
5: Today, inception-engine works as a cross-agent deployer for skills on all listed agents, plus single-file writes and JSON config patches. It also supports MCP server registration and global rules-file deployment for the subset of agents whose config surfaces are implemented and validated today.
6: 
7: GitHub Copilot is no longer treated as a separate instruction or skill target in the product direction when it can consume Claude-native artifacts directly. If Copilot uses `CLAUDE.md` or Claude-style skill layouts without translation, inception-engine should rely on the Claude deployment path instead of maintaining duplicate Copilot-specific surfaces. Dedicated Copilot customization remains justified only where Copilot exposes a materially different interface, such as MCP-related configuration.
8: 
9: The broader portability layer is the roadmap direction, but this README focuses on what is working now.
10: 
11: `init` is available as a bootstrap command. It scans for directories containing `SKILL.md`, discovers agent-rules Markdown files using the Claude-first portability conventions, and reads `mcp-servers.json` from the repo root to populate `mcpServers`. `files` and `configs` remain empty — `init` emits guidance when it detects a `files/` or `configs/` directory.
12: 
13: ## Quick Start
14: 
15: ```bash
16: npx @kuznai/inception-engine <directory>
17: ```
18: 
19: Where `<directory>` is a repo (or subdirectory) containing an `inception.json` manifest and skill files.
20: 
21: ## How It Works
22: 
23: inception-engine reads a manifest file (`inception.json`) from the target directory, detects which AI coding agents are installed on the system, and deploys skills to each agent's expected location.
24: 
25: - **POSIX (macOS, Linux)**: creates symlinks from the source skill directory to each agent's skill path
26: - **Windows**: copies skill directories to each agent's skill path
27: 
28: Managed skills overwrite their previous version. If a target exists but was not created by inception-engine, deployment refuses to replace it. On POSIX systems, symlinks mean updates to the source repo are reflected immediately.
29: 
30: ## Agent Compatibility Matrix
31: 
32: | Agent | ID | Skills | macOS | Linux | Windows |
33: |---|---|---|---|---|---|
34: | Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
35: | OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
36: | Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
37: | Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/` | Yes* | Yes* | Yes* |
38: | OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes* |
39: 
40: \* Antigravity support is currently based on the implementation's registry path assumptions and local validation, not a strong official doc set equivalent to the other agents.
41: 
42: \* OpenCode on Windows uses `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\`.
43: 
44: ### Feature Support
45: 
46: | Feature | Deploy | Revert |
47: |---|---|---|
48: | Skills (SKILL.md) | All agents via manifest and CLI | All agents |
49: | File write | All agents via manifest and CLI | All agents |
50: | Config patch (JSON merge) | All agents via manifest and CLI | All agents |
51: | MCP Servers | claude-code, gemini-cli, codex, antigravity, opencode; github-copilot repo-scoped surfaces are warned and skipped | claude-code, gemini-cli, codex, antigravity, opencode |
52: | Global/Repo Rules Files | All agents (antigravity uses repo-local `.agents/rules/`); github-copilot reads CLAUDE.md natively (deploy via claude-code) | All agents |
53: | Permissions / Approval Config | claude-code (`~/.claude/settings.json`), codex (`~/.codex/config.toml`); other agents are warned and skipped | claude-code, codex |
54: | `init` manifest generation | Scans `SKILL.md` directories (`skills`), `.md` files with Claude-first agent mapping (`agentRules`), and `mcp-servers.json` (`mcpServers`); emits hints for `files/` and `configs/` directories | N/A |
55: 
56: Features that depend on agent-specific config surfaces are intentionally conservative: if a target path or schema is not implemented with enough confidence, inception-engine warns and skips it rather than guessing.
57: 
58: For GitHub Copilot specifically, the portability rule is Claude-first: if Copilot accepts the same Claude-native instruction or skill artifact, inception-engine should not add a separate Copilot deployment feature for it.
59: 
60: ## Manifest Format
61: 
62: Create an `inception.json` file at the root of your skills directory:
63: 
64: ```json
65: {
66:   "skills": [
67:     {
68:       "name": "my-skill",
69:       "path": "skills/my-skill",
70:       "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", "gemini-cli", "antigravity", "opencode"]
71:     }
72:   ],
73:   "files": [
74:     {
75:       "name": "my-settings",
76:       "path": "files/settings.json",
77:       "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
78:       "agents": ["claude-code"]
79:     }
80:   ],
81:   "configs": [
82:     {
83:       "name": "enable-feature",
84:       "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
85:       "patch": { "someFeature": true },
86:       "agents": ["claude-code"]
87:     }
88:   ],
89:   "mcpServers": [
90:     {
91:       "name": "my-server",
92:       "agents": ["claude-code", "gemini-cli"],
93:       "config": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "my-mcp-server"] }
94:     }
95:   ],
96:   "agentRules": [
97:     {
98:       "name": "my-rules",
99:       "path": "rules/CLAUDE.md",
100:       "agents": ["claude-code"]
101:     }
102:   ],
103:   "permissions": [
104:     {
105:       "name": "safety-defaults",
106:       "agents": ["claude-code"],
107:       "config": {
108:         "permissions": {
109:           "allow": ["Read", "Glob"],
110:           "deny": ["Bash(rm:*)"]
111:         }
112:       }
113:     }
114:   ]
115: }
116: ```
117: 
118: Each **skill** entry has:
119: 
120: - **name** - Unique identifier using letters, digits, dots, underscores, or hyphens; must not start with a dot
121: - **path** - Relative path to the skill directory within the repo
122: - **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this skill to. If an agent isn't installed, it's skipped.
123: 
124: Each **file** entry deploys a single file to an agent's configuration location:
125: 
126: - **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
127: - **path** - Relative path to the source file within the repo
128: - **target** - Destination path using a placeholder prefix: `{home}`, `{appdata}` (Windows), or `{xdg_config}` (Linux). For example: `{home}/.claude/settings.json`
129: - **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this file to
130: 
131: Each **config** entry applies a [JSON merge patch (RFC 7386)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7386) to an existing agent config file:
132: 
133: - **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
134: - **target** - Config file to patch, using the same placeholder prefix as file entries
135: - **patch** - JSON object of keys to set. A `null` value removes the key from the target file. Nested object values are merged recursively; non-object values replace the existing value directly.
136: - **agents** - Array of agent IDs to apply this patch to
137: 
138: The engine records an undo-patch for each config-patch deployment so that `revert` can restore the original values.
139: 
140: Each **mcpServer** entry registers an MCP server into the agent's config file by applying a JSON merge patch under the `mcpServers` key:
141: 
142: - **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names); used as the server's key in the config
143: - **agents** - Array of agent IDs to register this server with
144: - **config** - Raw server descriptor object. For the currently supported JSON-backed adapters, inception-engine requires at least one non-empty `command` or `url` field, validates `args` as an array of strings when present, and validates `env` as a string-to-string object when present. Additional keys are passed through verbatim.
145: 
146: MCP server registration is supported for all agents except GitHub Copilot. Inception-engine automatically uses the correct adapter for each agent's configuration schema:
147: - **JSON (Merge Patch)**: `claude-code` (`~/.claude.json`), `gemini-cli` (`~/.gemini/settings.json`), and `opencode` (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` using the custom `"mcp"` key).
148: - **TOML (Patch)**: `codex` (`~/.codex/config.toml`).
149: - **Markdown Frontmatter (Emit)**: `antigravity` (repo-local `.agents/rules/{name}.md` files).
150: 
151: Revert removes the registered server entry from the respective configuration file. GitHub Copilot, which uses repo-scoped MCP surfaces not yet implemented by inception-engine, continues to emit a schema-aware warning and is skipped.
152: 
153: Each **agentRules** entry deploys a Markdown instruction file to an agent's supported global rules file location:
154: 
155: - **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
156: - **path** - Relative path to the source Markdown file within the repo; supported global rules adapters require a `.md` or `.markdown` source path
157: - **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this file to
158: 
159: Instruction rule deployment is supported for all agents. For most agents, this targets a single global rules file (e.g., `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` for `claude-code`, `~/.codex/AGENTS.md` for `codex`, `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` for `gemini-cli`, and `~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md` for `opencode`). For `antigravity`, inception-engine deploys to repo-local instruction surfaces at `{repo}/.agents/rules/{name}.md`. For `github-copilot`, no separate deployment is needed because Copilot reads `CLAUDE.md` natively — target it via the `claude-code` agentRules entry and it reaches Copilot automatically. Revert removes the deployed rules file.
160: 
161: Each **permissions** entry deploys execution and safety-oriented configuration to an agent's permission or approval surface:
162: 
163: - **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
164: - **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this entry to
165: - **config** - Permission config payload; shape is validated per agent (see below)
166: 
167: Permission deployment is currently supported for `claude-code` and `codex`. Other agents emit a warning and are skipped.
168: 
169: For `claude-code`, the config is merged into `~/.claude/settings.json`. Only the `permissions` key is accepted:
170: 
171: ```json
172: {
173:   "name": "safety-defaults",
174:   "agents": ["claude-code"],
175:   "config": {
176:     "permissions": {
177:       "allow": ["Read", "Glob", "Bash(git:*)"],
178:       "deny": ["Bash(rm:*)"]
179:     }
180:   }
181: }
182: ```
183: 
184: Both `allow` and `deny` are optional string arrays. Tool patterns follow Claude Code's permission glob syntax (e.g., `Bash(git:*)` to allow all git commands).
185: 
186: For `codex`, the config is merged into `~/.codex/config.toml`. Only the `approval_policy` key is accepted:
187: 
188: ```json
189: {
190:   "name": "codex-approval",
191:   "agents": ["codex"],
192:   "config": {
193:     "approval_policy": "suggest"
194:   }
195: }
196: ```
197: 
198: Valid `approval_policy` values are `"auto"`, `"manual"`, `"suggest"`, and `"on-failure"`.
199: 
200: Revert restores the previous config values using the undo patch recorded at deploy time.
201: 
202: ## Creating Skills
203: 
204: Each skill is a directory containing at minimum a `SKILL.md` file with YAML frontmatter:
205: 
206: ```markdown
207: ---
208: name: my-skill
209: description: What this skill does and when to use it
210: ---
211: 
212: # My Skill
213: 
214: Instructions for the AI agent...
215: ```
216: 
217: The `name` and `description` fields in the frontmatter are used by most agents. The description determines when the agent activates the skill. inception-engine now validates this minimum contract during deploy: `SKILL.md` must start with YAML frontmatter and include non-empty single-line `name` and `description` fields. It still does not attempt full YAML/schema validation beyond that targeted check.
218: 
219: ## `init` Command
220: 
221: `init` is meant to bootstrap a repository that already has skills and related manifest assets. It recursively scans the target directory, treats any directory containing `SKILL.md` as a skill, discovers supported instruction and MCP conventions, and writes a starter `inception.json`.
222: 
223: Current `init` behavior:
224: 
225: - Generates `skills` entries using the discovered relative paths
226: - Uses the directory name as the manifest skill name
227: - Applies either the `--agents` list or all currently known agent IDs
228: - Refuses to overwrite an existing `inception.json` unless `--force` is provided
229: - Supports `--dry-run` so you can inspect the generated manifest before writing it
230: - Discovers agent-rules Markdown files in the root and conventional subdirectories (`rules/`, `instructions/`, `.github/`, `.agents/rules/`), mapping them to agents using Claude-first portability conventions: `copilot-instructions.md` maps to `claude-code` (Copilot reads `CLAUDE.md` natively), and the fallback for unrecognized files excludes agents whose agentRules surface is unsupported
231: - Reads `mcp-servers.json` from the repo root (if present) and generates `mcpServers` entries; invalid entries are warned and skipped
232: - Reads `files-manifest.json` from the repo root (if present) and generates `files` entries; invalid entries are warned and skipped
233: - Reads `configs-manifest.json` from the repo root (if present) and generates `configs` entries; invalid entries are warned and skipped
234: - Emits guidance to create a sidecar manifest when a `files/` or `configs/` directory is detected but the corresponding sidecar file is absent
235: 
236: Current `init` limitations:
237: 
238: - It does not reconcile generated manifest entries against `SKILL.md` frontmatter values
239: - `files` and `configs` target paths are deployment-system-specific and cannot be inferred; they must be provided explicitly in `files-manifest.json` and `configs-manifest.json`
240: 
241: ### Repo Conventions Recognized by `init`
242: 
243: Place a `mcp-servers.json` file at the repo root to have `init` populate the `mcpServers` section automatically. The file must be a JSON array of MCP server entries using the same schema as the `mcpServers` field in `inception.json`:
244: 
245: ```json
246: [
247:   {
248:     "name": "my-server",
249:     "agents": ["claude-code", "gemini-cli"],
250:     "config": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "my-mcp-server"] }
251:   }
252: ]
253: ```
254: 
255: Place a `files-manifest.json` file at the repo root to have `init` populate the `files` section automatically. The file must be a JSON array of file entries using the same schema as the `files` field in `inception.json`:
256: 
257: ```json
258: [
259:   {
260:     "name": "my-settings",
261:     "path": "files/settings.json",
262:     "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
263:     "agents": ["claude-code"]
264:   }
265: ]
266: ```
267: 
268: Place a `configs-manifest.json` file at the repo root to have `init` populate the `configs` section automatically. The file must be a JSON array of config entries using the same schema as the `configs` field in `inception.json`:
269: 
270: ```json
271: [
272:   {
273:     "name": "enable-feature",
274:     "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
275:     "patch": { "someFeature": true },
276:     "agents": ["claude-code"]
277:   }
278: ]
279: ```
280: 
281: Invalid entries are warned and skipped; the rest are written into the generated manifest verbatim.
282: 
283: ## CLI Reference
284: 
285: ```
286: inception-engine <directory> [options]
287: inception-engine revert <directory> [options]
288: inception-engine init <directory> [options]
289: ```
290: 
291: ### Commands
292: 
293: | Command | Description |
294: |---|---|
295: | `<directory>` | Deploy skills from the manifest in the given directory |
296: | `revert <directory>` | Remove previously deployed skills declared in the manifest |
297: | `init <directory>` | Scan a directory for skill folders and generate `inception.json` |
298: 
299: ### Options
300: 
301: | Option | Description |
302: |---|---|
303: | `--dry-run` | Show what would be done without making changes |
304: | `--agents <list>` | Comma-separated list of agent IDs to target (overrides deploy detection; restricts revert) |
305: | `--force` | `init` only; overwrite an existing `inception.json` |
306: | `--verbose` | Show detailed output including file paths |
307: | `--debug` | Show full error stack traces |
308: | `--help` | Show help message |
309: 
310: ### Examples
311: 
312: ```bash
313: # Deploy all skills to all detected agents
314: npx @kuznai/inception-engine ./my-skills-repo
315: 
316: # Preview what would be deployed
317: npx @kuznai/inception-engine ./my-skills-repo --dry-run
318: 
319: # Deploy only to Claude Code and Codex
320: npx @kuznai/inception-engine ./my-skills-repo --agents claude-code,codex
321: 
322: # Remove deployed skills
323: npx @kuznai/inception-engine revert ./my-skills-repo
324: 
325: # Preview what would be removed
326: npx @kuznai/inception-engine revert ./my-skills-repo --dry-run
327: 
328: # Generate a starter manifest from discovered skill folders
329: npx @kuznai/inception-engine init ./my-skills-repo
330: 
331: # Preview the generated manifest without writing it
332: npx @kuznai/inception-engine init ./my-skills-repo --dry-run
333: ```
334: 
335: ## Sample Skills
336: 
337: The `limbo/` directory contains exceptional sample skills for testing purposes only.
338: 
339: Try them out:
340: 
341: ```bash
342: npx @kuznai/inception-engine init limbo --dry-run
343: npx @kuznai/inception-engine limbo --dry-run
344: ```
345: 
346: ## Agent Detection
347: 
348: inception-engine automatically detects which agents are installed by checking:
349: 
350: 1. Whether the agent's config directory exists (e.g., `~/.claude/` for Claude Code)
351: 2. Whether the agent's binary is in your PATH (e.g., `claude`, `codex`, `gemini`)
352: 
353: If an agent isn't detected, its skills are skipped during deploy. Use `--agents` to override detection.
354: 
355: Revert targets all agents listed in the manifest by default (regardless of detection) so that previously deployed skills are cleaned up even if the agent has since been uninstalled. Use `--agents` to restrict revert to specific agents.
356: 
357: ## Cross-Platform Behavior
358: 
359: | Platform | Deploy Method | Behavior |
360: |---|---|---|
361: | macOS | Symlink | Source changes are reflected immediately |
362: | Linux | Symlink | Source changes are reflected immediately |
363: | Windows | Copy | Source must be re-deployed after changes |
364: 
365: ### Ownership Tracking and Safe Revert
366: 
367: inception-engine maintains a centralized deployment registry at `~/.inception-engine/registry.json`. Each deploy records the target path, skill name, agent ID, action-specific provenance (`source`/`method` for skill-dir, `source` for file-write, `patch`/`undoPatch` for config-patch), and timestamp. No files are written to the source repository.
368: 
369: - **Registry-based ownership**: On revert, the registry is checked before removing any target. Only targets with a valid registry entry are removed. On redeploy, unmanaged targets are never replaced.
370: 
371: - **Strong binding**: Each registry entry binds a specific target path to its skill, agent, and action kind. For `skill-dir` and `file-write`, ownership checks also require the recorded `source` to match before an existing target is treated as managed. For `config-patch`, overwrite protection is keyed by target path, kind, skill, and agent; the stored `patch` and `undoPatch` are used for revert bookkeeping rather than deploy-time identity checks.
372: 
373: - **Atomic redeploy**: When overwriting an existing managed `skill-dir` target, the engine renames the old target to a backup, creates the new deployment, and only removes the backup on success. If the new deployment fails, the backup is restored. `file-write` and `config-patch` deployments write directly to the target without this backup/rollback model.
374: 
375: - **Cross-platform**: The registry uses the same resolved home directory as the rest of the tool, including sudo scenarios on POSIX and elevated PowerShell on Windows.
376: 
377: ## Running with Privilege Escalation
378: 
379: The tool works without elevated privileges. If run with `sudo` on POSIX systems, it looks up the real user's home directory from the OS directory services (`getent passwd` on Linux, `dscl` on macOS, `/etc/passwd` as a universal fallback) so skills are deployed to the correct location regardless of where home directories are stored — standard `/home/<user>`, LDAP/NIS paths, enterprise layouts, or otherwise.
380: 
381: If the real home cannot be determined, the tool exits with an error rather than silently deploying to a guessed path.
382: 
383: On Windows, `os.homedir()` correctly resolves even in elevated PowerShell or cmd.
384: 
385: ## Security & Compatibility Notes
386: 
387: ### SUDO_USER handling
388: 
389: When run under `sudo` on POSIX, `SUDO_USER` is read as an advisory signal to identify the real user. The username is validated through OS directory services (`getent passwd` on Linux, `dscl` on macOS, `/etc/passwd` as a fallback) — it is never used as a raw path. This is standard practice for sudo-aware CLI tools.
390: 
391: `SUDO_USER` is only consulted when the process is actually running as root (UID 0). If `SUDO_USER` is present in the environment but the process is not root — for example, as a stale shell variable from a prior `sudo` session or an externally injected value in automation — it is ignored and the standard home directory is used instead.
392: 
393: ### XDG_CONFIG_HOME support
394: 
395: On POSIX, agents whose config lives under `~/.config/` (currently OpenCode) honor `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` when it is set to an absolute path. If the variable is unset or contains a relative path (which the XDG Base Directory Specification disallows), the tool falls back to the standard `~/.config` default. This has no effect on Windows.
396: 
397: ### Windows %APPDATA% fallback
398: 
399: On Windows, `%APPDATA%` is used to locate agent config directories (currently only OpenCode). When `%APPDATA%` is unset — which can happen in some service or minimal sandbox contexts — the tool falls back to `AppData\Roaming` under the resolved home directory. This fallback is intentional and correct for interactive user sessions and elevated PowerShell. Running as a Windows service account is outside the supported use case for this tool.
400: 
401: ### Future: mixed asset layouts on Windows
402: 
403: Windows deployment currently uses directory-level copy (one `cp -r` per skill). This works correctly for the present skill model where each skill is a single directory. If file-based assets or mixed (file + directory) skill layouts are added in the future, the copy strategy in `src/core/deploy.ts` will need revisiting.
404: 
405: ## Requirements
406: 
407: - Published CLI runtime: Node.js >= 22.3.0
408: - Direct TypeScript execution in this repo (`npm run dev`, `npm test`): Node.js >= 22.18.0
409: 
410: `inception-engine` publishes compiled JavaScript from `dist/`, so end users do not need the newer Node version required for this repository's direct `.ts` workflows. The higher contributor floor exists only because this repo intentionally runs TypeScript straight through `node` for local development and tests, with no `tsx`, `ts-node`, or experimental TypeScript flags.
411: 
412: ## License
413: 
414: MIT

(End of file - total 414 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# inception-engine

Plant skills directly into the minds of your installed AI coding agents — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, and OpenCode. One command. They'll think they thought of it themselves.

Today, inception-engine works as a cross-agent deployer for skills on all listed agents, plus single-file writes and JSON config patches. It also supports MCP server registration and global rules-file deployment for the subset of agents whose config surfaces are implemented and validated today.

GitHub Copilot is no longer treated as a separate instruction or skill target in the product direction when it can consume Claude-native artifacts directly. If Copilot uses `CLAUDE.md` or Claude-style skill layouts without translation, inception-engine should rely on the Claude deployment path instead of maintaining duplicate Copilot-specific surfaces. Dedicated Copilot customization remains justified only where Copilot exposes a materially different interface, such as MCP-related configuration.

The broader portability layer is the roadmap direction, but this README focuses on what is working now.

`init` is available as a bootstrap command. It scans for directories containing `SKILL.md`, discovers agent-rules Markdown files using the Claude-first portability conventions, and reads `mcp-servers.json` from the repo root to populate `mcpServers`. `files` and `configs` remain empty — `init` emits guidance when it detects a `files/` or `configs/` directory.

## Quick Start

```bash
npx @kuznai/inception-engine <directory>
```

Where `<directory>` is a repo (or subdirectory) containing an `inception.json` manifest and skill files.

4: const AGENT_IDS = [

9:   "opencode",
10:   "github-copilot",

13: export { AGENT_IDS };
14: 
15: const SAFE_NAME_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/;
16: 
17: // Target templates must be rooted at a known placeholder and may only add
18: // descendant path segments beneath that root. e.g. "{home}/.claude/settings.json"
19: // is valid, while "{home}/../.ssh/config" is rejected.
20: // {repo} resolves to the manifest directory at deploy time, enabling repo-local
21: // targets (e.g. Antigravity's .agents/rules/ surface).
22: const TARGET_TEMPLATE_RE = /^\{(home|appdata|xdg_config|repo)\}(?:[\\/].*)?$/;

parts.text:
## Goal

Implement **roadmap item #1**: "Add support for agent definition deployment for agents that expose dedicated agent directories or frontmatter-based agent files." This adds a new `agentDefinitions` manifest entry type to `inception-engine` that deploys agent/subagent definition Markdown files to agent-specific repo-local directories.

## Instructions

- Follow the existing codebase patterns precisely — new adapter modeled on `src/core/adapters/rules.ts`, new schema entry modeled on `AgentRuleEntrySchema`, new agent config fields modeled on `agentRulesSupport`
- All changes must pass `npm run fmt`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint` (biome, max cognitive complexity 15), and `npm test` (all 260 tests)
- Write tests for the new feature in `test/unit/adapters.test.ts` and `test/unit/manifest.test.ts`
- Update `README.md` to document the new `agentDefinitions` manifest section
- Update `docs/roadmap.md` to strike through completed item #1

## Discoveries

### Agent definition target paths (all repo-local via `{repo}` placeholder)
| Agent | Target path | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| `claude-code` | `{repo}/.claude/agents/{name}.md` | `documented` |
| `codex` | **unsupported** — no dedicated agent-definition directory | — |
| `gemini-cli` | `{repo}/.gemini/agents/{name}.md` | `implementation-only` |
| `antigravity` | `{repo}/.agents/rules/{name}.md` (same as MCP/agentRules) | `documented` |
| `opencode` | `{repo}/.opencode/agents/{name}.md` | `documented` |
| `github-copilot` | `{repo}/.github/agents/{name}.agent.md` | `documented` |

### Key design decisions made
- `agentDefinitions` entries use `file-write` action kind (same as `agentRules`) — no new action kind needed
- All targets are **repo-local** (`{repo}` placeholder), not `{home}` — this is the defining characteristic
- GitHub Copilot uses `.agent.md` suffix convention; `init` auto-discovery strips the `.agent` infix from the derived name (`foo.agent.md` → name `foo`)
- `codex` is `unsupported` (no dedicated agent-definition dir surface) with a helpful message directing users to `agentRules` instead
- Source validation reuses `validateAgentRuleMarkdownPath` (must be `.md` or `.markdown`)
- `init` auto-discovers from 5 subdirs (`.claude/agents`, `.gemini/agents`, `.agents/rules`, `.opencode/agents`, `.github/agents`), maps each to the owning agent(s)
- A sidecar `agent-definitions-manifest.json` at repo root overrides auto-discovery (same pattern as `mcp-servers.json`)
- `AgentProvenance` interface gained an `agentDefinitions?: Confidence` field
- `AgentConfig` interface gained an `agentDefinitionsSupport?: AgentSurfaceSupport` field

### init complexity constraint
Biome enforces max cognitive complexity of 15. `findAgentDefinitionCandidates` and `logVerboseManifest` both needed refactoring:
- Extracted `scanDefinitionSubdir()` helper from `findAgentDefinitionCandidates`
- Extracted `logPathAgentEntries()` helper from `logVerboseManifest`; kept `files` and `configs` as inline loops because `ConfigEntry` has `target` (not `path`) unlike the helper's expected shape

## Accomplished

### Completed
1. **`src/schemas/manifest.ts`** — Added `AgentDefinitionEntrySchema`, `AgentDefinitionEntry` type, and `agentDefinitions` field (default `[]`) to `ManifestSchema`
2. **`src/types.ts`** — Added `AgentDefinitionEntry` to exports, `agentDefinitions?: Confidence` to `AgentProvenance`, `agentDefinitionsSupport?: AgentSurfaceSupport` to `AgentConfig`
3. **`src/config/agents.ts`** — Added `agentDefinitionsSupport` to all 6 agents (supported for claude-code/gemini-cli/antigravity/opencode/github-copilot; unsupported for codex); added `agentDefinitions` provenance confidence where applicable
4. **`src/core/adapters/agent-definitions.ts`** — New file. `compileAgentDefinitionActions()` and `compileAgentDefinitionReverts()` — modeled exactly on `rules.ts`
5. **`src/core/adapters/index.ts`** — Wired in new adapter: imports, exports `compileAgentDefinitionReverts`, passes `agentDefinitions` parameter to `compileAdapterActions`
6. **`src/core/deploy.ts`** — Updated `compileAdapterActions` call to pass `manifest.agentDefinitions ?? []` as the new last argument
7. **`src/core/revert.ts`** — Added `compileAgentDefinitionReverts` to both `planRevert` and `planRevertAll`
8. **`src/core/init.ts`** — Full rewrite with: `AgentDefinitionEntrySchema` import, `AGENT_DEFINITION_SUBDIRS` constant, `agentsForDefinitionSubdir()`, `deriveAgentDefinitionName()` (strips `.agent` infix), `scanDefinitionSubdir()` helper, `findAgentDefinitionCandidates()`, `buildAgentDefinitions()`, `loadAgentDefinitionsManifest()`, updated `logVerboseManifest`, updated `runInit` to discover/load/emit `agentDefinitions`
9. All source passes `npm run fmt`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint` (clean), and `npm test` (260 pass, 2 skip)

### In Progress — NEXT STEP
**Writing tests** — was in the middle of adding tests to `test/unit/adapters.test.ts` for `compileAgentDefinitionActions`. The file currently ends at line 520 (existing adapter tests). The next agent should append a new `describe("compileAgentDefinitionActions", ...)` block.

### Still To Do
1. **Tests in `test/unit/adapters.test.ts`** — add `compileAgentDefinitionActions` describe block covering:
   - Returns zero actions when no detectedAgents overlap
   - Does not validate source when no targeted agents overlap
   - Returns `file-write` action for `claude-code` with correct target (`.claude/agents/{name}.md`)
   - Returns `file-write` action for `opencode` with correct target (`.opencode/agents/{name}.md`)
   - Returns `file-write` action for `github-copilot` with correct target (`.github/agents/{name}.agent.md`)
   - Returns `file-write` action for `antigravity` with correct target (`.agents/rules/{name}.md`)
   - Returns warning and no action for `codex` (unsupported)
   - Throws when source is non-Markdown
   - Throws when source file does not exist
   - Produces actions for multiple detected agents

2. **Tests in `test/unit/manifest.test.ts`** — add tests covering:
   - Valid `agentDefinitions` entry is accepted
   - Defaults to `[]` when omitted
   - Throws when `agentDefinitions` entry is missing `name`
   - Throws when `agentDefinitions` entry is missing `path`
   - Throws when `agentDefinitions` entry has escaping `path`
   - Throws when `agentDefinitions` entry is missing `agents`

3. **`README.md`** — add `agentDefinitions` section to the Manifest Format documentation (after `agentRules`), update the Feature Support table, and document `init` auto-discovery behavior

4. **`docs/roadmap.md`** — strike through item #1 (same pattern as items #2 and #4 which are already struck through)

## Relevant files / directories

### Modified source files
- `src/schemas/manifest.ts` — Added `AgentDefinitionEntrySchema`, type, field
- `src/types.ts` — Added `AgentDefinitionEntry` export, new provenance/config fields
- `src/config/agents.ts` — Added `agentDefinitionsSupport` to all 6 agents
- `src/core/deploy.ts` — Updated `compileAdapterActions` call (passes `agentDefinitions`)
- `src/core/revert.ts` — Added `compileAgentDefinitionReverts` to plan functions
- `src/core/init.ts` — Full rewrite with agent-definitions discovery

### New source files
- `src/core/adapters/agent-definitions.ts` — New adapter

### Modified adapters index
- `src/core/adapters/index.ts` — Wired in agent-definitions

### Test files to be modified (not yet done)
- `test/unit/adapters.test.ts` — Append `compileAgentDefinitionActions` tests
- `test/unit/manifest.test.ts` — Append `agentDefinitions` schema tests

### Docs to be updated (not yet done)
- `README.md`
- `docs/roadmap.md`

### Reference files (read-only, used for pattern reference)
- `src/core/adapters/rules.ts` — Pattern reference for new adapter
- `src/core/adapters/mcp.ts` — Pattern reference
- `src/core/adapters/permissions.ts` — Pattern reference
- `src/core/adapters/frontmatter.ts`
- `src/core/adapters/toml.ts`
- `src/core/validation.ts`
- `src/core/resolve.ts`
- `src/core/ownership.ts`
- `src/core/preflight.ts`
- `src/core/detect.ts`
- `src/core/runtime-paths.ts`
- `test/unit/adapters.test.ts` — Existing tests (append new block at end)
- `test/unit/manifest.test.ts` — Existing tests (append new block at end)
- `test/helpers/fs.ts`
- `limbo/inception.json`
- `docs/north-star.md`
- `docs/roadmap.md`

diffs.before:
# inception-engine

Plant skills directly into the minds of your installed AI coding agents — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, and OpenCode. One command. They'll think they thought of it themselves.

Today, inception-engine works as a cross-agent deployer for skills on all listed agents, plus single-file writes and JSON config patches. It also supports MCP server registration and global rules-file deployment for the subset of agents whose config surfaces are implemented and validated today.

GitHub Copilot is no longer treated as a separate instruction or skill target in the product direction when it can consume Claude-native artifacts directly. If Copilot uses `CLAUDE.md` or Claude-style skill layouts without translation, inception-engine should rely on the Claude deployment path instead of maintaining duplicate Copilot-specific surfaces. Dedicated Copilot customization remains justified only where Copilot exposes a materially different interface, such as MCP-related configuration.

The broader portability layer is the roadmap direction, but this README focuses on what is working now.

`init` is available as a bootstrap command. It scans for directories containing `SKILL.md`, discovers agent-rules Markdown files using the Claude-first portability conventions, and reads `mcp-servers.json` from the repo root to populate `mcpServers`. `files` and `configs` remain empty — `init` emits guidance when it detects a `files/` or `configs/` directory.

## Quick Start

```bash
npx @kuznai/inception-engine <directory>
```

Where `<directory>` is a repo (or subdirectory) containing an `inception.json` manifest and skill files.

## How It Works

inception-engine reads a manifest file (`inception.json`) from the target directory, detects which AI coding agents are installed on the system, and deploys skills to each agent's expected location.

- **POSIX (macOS, Linux)**: creates symlinks from the source skill directory to each agent's skill path
- **Windows**: copies skill directories to each agent's skill path

Managed skills overwrite their previous version. If a target exists but was not created by inception-engine, deployment refuses to replace it. On POSIX systems, symlinks mean updates to the source repo are reflected immediately.

## Agent Compatibility Matrix

| Agent | ID | Skills | macOS | Linux | Windows |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/` | Yes* | Yes* | Yes* |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/` | Yes | Yes | Yes* |

\* Antigravity support is currently based on the implementation's registry path assumptions and local validation, not a strong official doc set equivalent to the other agents.

\* OpenCode on Windows uses `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\`.

### Feature Support

| Feature | Deploy | Revert |
|---|---|---|
| Skills (SKILL.md) | All agents via manifest and CLI | All agents |
| File write | All agents via manifest and CLI | All agents |
| Config patch (JSON merge) | All agents via manifest and CLI | All agents |
| MCP Servers | claude-code, gemini-cli, codex, antigravity, opencode; github-copilot repo-scoped surfaces are warned and skipped | claude-code, gemini-cli, codex, antigravity, opencode |
| Global/Repo Rules Files | All agents (antigravity uses repo-local `.agents/rules/`); github-copilot reads CLAUDE.md natively (deploy via claude-code) | All agents |
| Permissions / Approval Config | claude-code (`~/.claude/settings.json`), codex (`~/.codex/config.toml`); other agents are warned and skipped | claude-code, codex |
| `init` manifest generation | Scans `SKILL.md` directories (`skills`), `.md` files with Claude-first agent mapping (`agentRules`), and `mcp-servers.json` (`mcpServers`); emits hints for `files/` and `configs/` directories | N/A |

Features that depend on agent-specific config surfaces are intentionally conservative: if a target path or schema is not implemented with enough confidence, inception-engine warns and skips it rather than guessing.

For GitHub Copilot specifically, the portability rule is Claude-first: if Copilot accepts the same Claude-native instruction or skill artifact, inception-engine should not add a separate Copilot deployment feature for it.

## Manifest Format

Create an `inception.json` file at the root of your skills directory:

```json
{
  "skills": [
    {
      "name": "my-skill",
      "path": "skills/my-skill",
      "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", "gemini-cli", "antigravity", "opencode"]
    }
  ],
  "files": [
    {
      "name": "my-settings",
      "path": "files/settings.json",
      "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
      "agents": ["claude-code"]
    }
  ],
  "configs": [
    {
      "name": "enable-feature",
      "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
      "patch": { "someFeature": true },
      "agents": ["claude-code"]
    }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [
    {
      "name": "my-server",
      "agents": ["claude-code", "gemini-cli"],
      "config": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "my-mcp-server"] }
    }
  ],
  "agentRules": [
    {
      "name": "my-rules",
      "path": "rules/CLAUDE.md",
      "agents": ["claude-code"]
    }
  ],
  "permissions": [
    {
      "name": "safety-defaults",
      "agents": ["claude-code"],
      "config": {
        "permissions": {
          "allow": ["Read", "Glob"],
          "deny": ["Bash(rm:*)"]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Each **skill** entry has:

- **name** - Unique identifier using letters, digits, dots, underscores, or hyphens; must not start with a dot
- **path** - Relative path to the skill directory within the repo
- **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this skill to. If an agent isn't installed, it's skipped.

Each **file** entry deploys a single file to an agent's configuration location:

- **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
- **path** - Relative path to the source file within the repo
- **target** - Destination path using a placeholder prefix: `{home}`, `{appdata}` (Windows), or `{xdg_config}` (Linux). For example: `{home}/.claude/settings.json`
- **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this file to

Each **config** entry applies a [JSON merge patch (RFC 7386)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7386) to an existing agent config file:

- **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
- **target** - Config file to patch, using the same placeholder prefix as file entries
- **patch** - JSON object of keys to set. A `null` value removes the key from the target file. Nested object values are merged recursively; non-object values replace the existing value directly.
- **agents** - Array of agent IDs to apply this patch to

The engine records an undo-patch for each config-patch deployment so that `revert` can restore the original values.

Each **mcpServer** entry registers an MCP server into the agent's config file by applying a JSON merge patch under the `mcpServers` key:

- **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names); used as the server's key in the config
- **agents** - Array of agent IDs to register this server with
- **config** - Raw server descriptor object. For the currently supported JSON-backed adapters, inception-engine requires at least one non-empty `command` or `url` field, validates `args` as an array of strings when present, and validates `env` as a string-to-string object when present. Additional keys are passed through verbatim.

MCP server registration is supported for all agents except GitHub Copilot. Inception-engine automatically uses the correct adapter for each agent's configuration schema:
- **JSON (Merge Patch)**: `claude-code` (`~/.claude.json`), `gemini-cli` (`~/.gemini/settings.json`), and `opencode` (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` using the custom `"mcp"` key).
- **TOML (Patch)**: `codex` (`~/.codex/config.toml`).
- **Markdown Frontmatter (Emit)**: `antigravity` (repo-local `.agents/rules/{name}.md` files).

Revert removes the registered server entry from the respective configuration file. GitHub Copilot, which uses repo-scoped MCP surfaces not yet implemented by inception-engine, continues to emit a schema-aware warning and is skipped.

Each **agentRules** entry deploys a Markdown instruction file to an agent's supported global rules file location:

- **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
- **path** - Relative path to the source Markdown file within the repo; supported global rules adapters require a `.md` or `.markdown` source path
- **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this file to

Instruction rule deployment is supported for all agents. For most agents, this targets a single global rules file (e.g., `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` for `claude-code`, `~/.codex/AGENTS.md` for `codex`, `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` for `gemini-cli`, and `~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md` for `opencode`). For `antigravity`, inception-engine deploys to repo-local instruction surfaces at `{repo}/.agents/rules/{name}.md`. For `github-copilot`, no separate deployment is needed because Copilot reads `CLAUDE.md` natively — target it via the `claude-code` agentRules entry and it reaches Copilot automatically. Revert removes the deployed rules file.

Each **permissions** entry deploys execution and safety-oriented configuration to an agent's permission or approval surface:

- **name** - Unique identifier (same format as skill names)
- **agents** - Array of agent IDs to deploy this entry to
- **config** - Permission config payload; shape is validated per agent (see below)

Permission deployment is currently supported for `claude-code` and `codex`. Other agents emit a warning and are skipped.

For `claude-code`, the config is merged into `~/.claude/settings.json`. Only the `permissions` key is accepted:

```json
{
  "name": "safety-defaults",
  "agents": ["claude-code"],
  "config": {
    "permissions": {
      "allow": ["Read", "Glob", "Bash(git:*)"],
      "deny": ["Bash(rm:*)"]
    }
  }
}
```

Both `allow` and `deny` are optional string arrays. Tool patterns follow Claude Code's permission glob syntax (e.g., `Bash(git:*)` to allow all git commands).

For `codex`, the config is merged into `~/.codex/config.toml`. Only the `approval_policy` key is accepted:

```json
{
  "name": "codex-approval",
  "agents": ["codex"],
  "config": {
    "approval_policy": "suggest"
  }
}
```

Valid `approval_policy` values are `"auto"`, `"manual"`, `"suggest"`, and `"on-failure"`.

Revert restores the previous config values using the undo patch recorded at deploy time.

## Creating Skills

Each skill is a directory containing at minimum a `SKILL.md` file with YAML frontmatter:

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: What this skill does and when to use it
---

# My Skill

Instructions for the AI agent...
```

The `name` and `description` fields in the frontmatter are used by most agents. The description determines when the agent activates the skill. inception-engine now validates this minimum contract during deploy: `SKILL.md` must start with YAML frontmatter and include non-empty single-line `name` and `description` fields. It still does not attempt full YAML/schema validation beyond that targeted check.

## `init` Command

`init` is meant to bootstrap a repository that already has skills and related manifest assets. It recursively scans the target directory, treats any directory containing `SKILL.md` as a skill, discovers supported instruction and MCP conventions, and writes a starter `inception.json`.

Current `init` behavior:

- Generates `skills` entries using the discovered relative paths
- Uses the directory name as the manifest skill name
- Applies either the `--agents` list or all currently known agent IDs
- Refuses to overwrite an existing `inception.json` unless `--force` is provided
- Supports `--dry-run` so you can inspect the generated manifest before writing it
- Discovers agent-rules Markdown files in the root and conventional subdirectories (`rules/`, `instructions/`, `.github/`, `.agents/rules/`), mapping them to agents using Claude-first portability conventions: `copilot-instructions.md` maps to `claude-code` (Copilot reads `CLAUDE.md` natively), and the fallback for unrecognized files excludes agents whose agentRules surface is unsupported
- Reads `mcp-servers.json` from the repo root (if present) and generates `mcpServers` entries; invalid entries are warned and skipped
- Reads `files-manifest.json` from the repo root (if present) and generates `files` entries; invalid entries are warned and skipped
- Reads `configs-manifest.json` from the repo root (if present) and generates `configs` entries; invalid entries are warned and skipped
- Emits guidance to create a sidecar manifest when a `files/` or `configs/` directory is detected but the corresponding sidecar file is absent

Current `init` limitations:

- It does not reconcile generated manifest entries against `SKILL.md` frontmatter values
- `files` and `configs` target paths are deployment-system-specific and cannot be inferred; they must be provided explicitly in `files-manifest.json` and `configs-manifest.json`

### Repo Conventions Recognized by `init`

Place a `mcp-servers.json` file at the repo root to have `init` populate the `mcpServers` section automatically. The file must be a JSON array of MCP server entries using the same schema as the `mcpServers` field in `inception.json`:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "my-server",
    "agents": ["claude-code", "gemini-cli"],
    "config": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "my-mcp-server"] }
  }
]
```

Place a `files-manifest.json` file at the repo root to have `init` populate the `files` section automatically. The file must be a JSON array of file entries using the same schema as the `files` field in `inception.json`:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "my-settings",
    "path": "files/settings.json",
    "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
    "agents": ["claude-code"]
  }
]
```

Place a `configs-manifest.json` file at the repo root to have `init` populate the `configs` section automatically. The file must be a JSON array of config entries using the same schema as the `configs` field in `inception.json`:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "enable-feature",
    "target": "{home}/.claude/settings.json",
    "patch": { "someFeature": true },
    "agents": ["claude-code"]
  }
]
```

Invalid entries are warned and skipped; the rest are written into the generated manifest verbatim.

## CLI Reference

```
inception-engine <directory> [options]
inception-engine revert <directory> [options]
inception-engine init <directory> [options]
```

### Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `<directory>` | Deploy skills from the manifest in the given directory |
| `revert <directory>` | Remove previously deployed skills declared in the manifest |
| `init <directory>` | Scan a directory for skill folders and generate `inception.json` |

### Options

| Option | Description |
|---|---|
| `--dry-run` | Show what would be done without making changes |
| `--agents <list>` | Comma-separated list of agent IDs to target (overrides deploy detection; restricts revert) |
| `--force` | `init` only; overwrite an existing `inception.json` |
| `--verbose` | Show detailed output including file paths |
| `--debug` | Show full error stack traces |
| `--help` | Show help message |

### Examples

```bash
# Deploy all skills to all detected agents
npx @kuznai/inception-engine ./my-skills-repo

# Preview what would be deployed
npx @kuznai/inception-engine ./my-skills-repo --dry-run

# Deploy only to Claude Code and Codex
npx @kuznai/inception-engine ./my-skills-repo --agents claude-code,codex

# Remove deployed skills
npx @kuznai/inception-engine revert ./my-skills-repo

# Preview what would be removed
npx @kuznai/inception-engine revert ./my-skills-repo --dry-run

# Generate a starter manifest from discovered skill folders
npx @kuznai/inception-engine init ./my-skills-repo

# Preview the generated manifest without writing it
npx @kuznai/inception-engine init ./my-skills-repo --dry-run
```

## Sample Skills

The `limbo/` directory contains exceptional sample skills for testing purposes only.

Try them out:

```bash
npx @kuznai/inception-engine init limbo --dry-run
npx @kuznai/inception-engine limbo --dry-run
```

## Agent Detection

inception-engine automatically detects which agents are installed by checking:

1. Whether the agent's config directory exists (e.g., `~/.claude/` for Claude Code)
2. Whether the agent's binary is in your PATH (e.g., `claude`, `codex`, `gemini`)

If an agent isn't detected, its skills are skipped during deploy. Use `--agents` to override detection.

Revert targets all agents listed in the manifest by default (regardless of detection) so that previously deployed skills are cleaned up even if the agent has since been uninstalled. Use `--agents` to restrict revert to specific agents.

## Cross-Platform Behavior

| Platform | Deploy Method | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| macOS | Symlink | Source changes are reflected immediately |
| Linux | Symlink | Source changes are reflected immediately |
| Windows | Copy | Source must be re-deployed after changes |

### Ownership Tracking and Safe Revert

inception-engine maintains a centralized deployment registry at `~/.inception-engine/registry.json`. Each deploy records the target path, skill name, agent ID, action-specific provenance (`source`/`method` for skill-dir, `source` for file-write, `patch`/`undoPatch` for config-patch), and timestamp. No files are written to the source repository.

- **Registry-based ownership**: On revert, the registry is checked before removing any target. Only targets with a valid registry entry are removed. On redeploy, unmanaged targets are never replaced.

- **Strong binding**: Each registry entry binds a specific target path to its skill, agent, and action kind. For `skill-dir` and `file-write`, ownership checks also require the recorded `source` to match before an existing target is treated as managed. For `config-patch`, overwrite protection is keyed by target path, kind, skill, and agent; the stored `patch` and `undoPatch` are used for revert bookkeeping rather than deploy-time identity checks.

- **Atomic redeploy**: When overwriting an existing managed `skill-dir` target, the engine renames the old target to a backup, creates the new deployment, and only removes the backup on success. If the new deployment fails, the backup is restored. `file-write` and `config-patch` deployments write directly to the target without this backup/rollback model.

- **Cross-platform**: The registry uses the same resolved home directory as the rest of the tool, including sudo scenarios on POSIX and elevated PowerShell on Windows.

## Running with Privilege Escalation

The tool works without elevated privileges. If run with `sudo` on POSIX systems, it looks up the real user's home directory from the OS directory services (`getent passwd` on Linux, `dscl` on macOS, `/etc/passwd` as a universal fallback) so skills are deployed to the correct location regardless of where home directories are stored — standard `/home/<user>`, LDAP/NIS paths, enterprise layouts, or otherwise.

If the real home cannot be determined, the tool exits with an error rather than silently deploying to a guessed path.

On Windows, `os.homedir()` correctly resolves even in elevated PowerShell or cmd.

## Security & Compatibility Notes

### SUDO_USER handling

When run under `sudo` on POSIX, `SUDO_USER` is read as an advisory signal to identify the real user. The username is validated through OS directory services (`getent passwd` on Linux, `dscl` on macOS, `/etc/passwd` as a fallback) — it is never used as a raw path. This is standard practice for sudo-aware CLI tools.

`SUDO_USER` is only consulted when the process is actually running as root (UID 0). If `SUDO_USER` is present in the environment but the process is not root — for example, as a stale shell variable from a prior `sudo` session or an externally injected value in automation — it is ignored and the standard home directory is used instead.

### XDG_CONFIG_HOME support

On POSIX, agents whose config lives under `~/.config/` (currently OpenCode) honor `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` when it is set to an absolute path. If the variable is unset or contains a relative path (which the XDG Base Directory Specification disallows), the tool falls back to the standard `~/.config` default. This has no effect on Windows.

### Windows %APPDATA% fallback

On Windows, `%APPDATA%` is used to locate agent config directories (currently only OpenCode). When `%APPDATA%` is unset — which can happen in some service or minimal sandbox contexts — the tool falls back to `AppData\Roaming` under the resolved home directory. This fallback is intentional and correct for interactive user sessions and elevated PowerShell. Running as a Windows service account is outside the supported use case for this tool.

### Future: mixed asset layouts on Windows

Windows deployment currently uses directory-level copy (one `cp -r` per skill). This works correctly for the present skill model where each skill is a single directory. If file-based assets or mixed (file + directory) skill layouts are added in the future, the copy strategy in `src/core/deploy.ts` will need revisiting.

## Requirements

- Published CLI runtime: Node.js >= 22.3.0
- Direct TypeScript execution in this repo (`npm run dev`, `npm test`): Node.js >= 22.18.0

`inception-engine` publishes compiled JavaScript from `dist/`, so end users do not need the newer Node version required for this repository's direct `.ts` workflows. The higher contributor floor exists only because this repo intentionally runs TypeScript straight through `node` for local development and tests, with no `tsx`, `ts-node`, or experimental TypeScript flags.

## License

MIT
